Justin Wu: Once this farm closure thing blows over.
Justin Wu: But now...
<<#Simple(8)>>
->I believe you will still be friends.
    Justin Wu: Probably.
    Thought: I can tell he doesn't mean it...
->There's not much hope.;<<Set($SaidCantGetBackTogether,true)>>
    Justin Wu: Yeah.
    Thought: A bitter smile.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: None of that matters now.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站低頭返回)>>
Justin Wu: The top priority is to make sure Silencio is shut down for good.
Justin Wu: As long as we follow the plan, it shouldn't be a problem.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站右看)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Wayne said he's gonna stop you.
    Justin Wu: Impossible.
->How confident are you?
    Justin Wu: 100% positive.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: The director definitely wants to shut down this farm.
Justin Wu: The report he asked for is more like a test.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站右看返回)>>
Justin Wu: A test to find out how capable I am.
Justin Wu: Leave the rest to me.
Justin Wu: Let's go.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_正站左看返回)>>
<<#End>>

title: 俊恩解釋執行長
body: Justin Wu: Jason. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I'm here. Justin Wu: Thank you. ->What happened? Justin Wu: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: I know this is out of the blue, but... Justin Wu: I need you to come with me. We're meeting with the CEO. Frances Cheng(c): Yuna was right... <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What is the meeting about? // ->What do you need me for?;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> //if昨天沒有發現許大哥 <<#If($KnowTruthControlDepartment=-1)>> Justin Wu: ...;<> Justin Wu: Remember the investigation yesterday? About the Cellar malfunction? Justin Wu: I found the culprit. Justin Wu: It's Liam Hsu.;<> Thought: Him!? Considering Yale's response, could he be an accomplice, too? Justin Wu: He came to me later yesterday and told me everything.;<> Justin Wu: Yale did know about it, but he's not an accomplice. Justin Wu: We don't have time to go over the details. I'll jump to the conclusion.;<> Justin Wu: A certain director, or directors, told Liam to sabotage the Cellar. Justin Wu: It's part of a larger plan to shut down this farm.;<> Justin Wu: To this end, we share the same goal. However, I don't agree with the way they're doing it. Justin Wu: Now I have a chance to take over the initiative.;<> <<#GoTo(@操作版開始說明和董事見面細節)>> <<#EndIf>> Justin Wu: About what happened yesterday, I have made some progress. Thought: He's talking about members of the board sabotaging the Cellar. <<@操作版開始說明和董事見面細節>> Justin Wu: Through the CEO, I have made contact with directors in favor of shutting down Silencio.;<> Justin Wu: Although I don't know which directors they are yet... Justin Wu: Mr. Yan will see me later today. By then we will find out more.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Is that good or bad news? Justin Wu: It's an opportunity. Although for me personally, it could go either way. ->That was quick. Justin Wu: I'm surprised, too. Normally this should take a few days. Justin Wu: But anyway... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Now I have a chance to pitch my ideas to them in person.;<> Justin Wu: If I manage to convince them, I can shut down Silencio with the least damage. <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->So, what do you need me for? //if剛剛有選為什麼要找我依氣去 <<#If($DLState=你為什麼要找我一起去？)>> Justin Wu: Oh, sorry, I forgot to answer your question. <<#EndIf>> Justin Wu: I asked to bring a colleague along. Justin Wu: I've had enough of their sneaky approach. Going forward, I want to increase transparency.;<> Justin Wu: This particular matter, though, is too sensitive for public disclosure. Justin Wu: The bottom line is, my team should have the right to know.;<> Justin Wu: Since the CEO and the director have agreed to let me bring a colleague along, it means they must be in agreement, too. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I see. // ->Why me? Justin Wu: Because the others have work to do. Justin Wu: Most importantly, I appreciate your objectivity. //if有下車 <<#If($DidGetOffTheCar=true)>> Justin Wu: And you're perceptive, too. Justin Wu: You noticed something's wrong with Yale, and found out he's trying to protect Mr. Hsu. <<#EndIf>> //if有讓采欣留下 <<#If($LetTsaiShinStayHelp=true)>> Justin Wu: And you make good judgments. Justin Wu: You realized Cathy could do more good working with Jesse and Packs, instead of running errands. <<#EndIf>> //if有啦拉桿 <<#If($DoPullEmergencyBar=true)>> Justin Wu: And fundamentally. Justin Wu: You're a good man. Justin Wu: When the racking system went awry, you didn't hesitate to pull the lever. Thought: An encouraging smile. Justin Wu: Not everyone could do that. <<#EndIf>> Justin Wu: Considering the nature of the upcoming meeting, I need a trustworthy colleague as witness. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I'll do what I can to play support. Justin Wu: Thank you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: OK, let's go. Justin Wu: Mr. Yan is waiting. <<#End>>

title: 操作路線_回辦公室
操作路線_見執行長
body: Shane Yan: Allow me to introduce. This is Ms. Ann Jiang, engineering manager of the Red Room lab.;<> Thought: The real engineering manager, huh... Justin Wu: I thought we would be meeting with directors in person.;<> Shane Yan: Ha, don't flatter yourself. The board is too busy for something like this.;<> Justin Wu: I— Shane Yan: Ms. Jiang is joining our meeting as the appointed representative of a director.;<> Thought: Clearly, he doesn't want to disclose the director's name... Shane Yan: You have earned the attention of the director, Justin.;<> Shane Yan: What you did was counterproductive to his plan, but since you proposed to take over... Shane Yan: He would like to hear what you propose, hence our meeting today. Justin Wu: At least I deserve to know his name...;<>;<> Shane Yan: Know your place.;<> Shane Yan: You're playing with fire, Justin.;<> Shane Yan: You haven't been burned yet. Chalk it up to sheer luck.;<> Shane Yan: If you keep pushing your luck, though, things could take a nasty turn any moment.;<> Shane Yan: Am I clear? Justin Wu: ...;<>;<> Justin Wu: ...I see. Ann Jiang: Enough prep talk. Can we start now?;<>;<> Shane Yan: Sorry, Ms. Jiang. Please start.;<> Ann Jiang: Tell me what's your verdict on the Cellar of this farm.;<>;<> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: It's a mess. Can't be salvaged. We must shut it down. Now. Ann Jiang: Good. We have a common ground to work on.;<> Ann Jiang: This farm is not contributing any value. The hardware is not reliable. And the revenue is sliding, too. Justin Wu: It's not safe for the juicers...;<> Ann Jiang: And that, too. Ann Jiang: Considering everything, this farm is a burden.;<> Ann Jiang: When you have a burden, you get rid of it. Plain and simple. Ann Jiang: To do that, we must address several issues.;<> Ann Jiang: First of all... Justin Wu: The shortage of flops once Silencio is shut down. Ann Jiang: Correct. We don't rely on Silencio as much as we used to, but it's still churning out a considerable amount of flops.;<> Ann Jiang: However. Ann Jiang: The Red Room has ways to make up for that, even if we shut the farm down right now.;<>;<> Ann Jiang: That said, it's a non-issue. Justin Wu: May I know what your ways are? Because I've been—;<>;<> Ann Jiang: No.;<> Ann Jiang: The next point.;<> Ann Jiang: The cost of terminating hundreds of thousands of contracts.;<> Ann Jiang: I have consulted legal professionals. If we terminate these contracts on the grounds of force majeure, such as facility failure, the compensation won't be too high. Justin Wu: …I see you left no stone unturned. Ann Jiang: Of course.;<> Ann Jiang: Costs and damages are all accounted for. Now we need to establish the necessity of shutting this farm down. Ann Jiang: The board will make a decision on the fate of this farm soon. Ann Jiang: This is what we need you for.;<> Ann Jiang: Give me a detailed report, highlighting all existing issues of the Cellar. Frances Cheng(c): They want to present this report at the board meeting.;<> <> Ann Jiang: This should be the farm manager's job. Unfortunately, Ms. Yeh is not available at the moment. Ann Jiang: Board members are not expecting to see a report.;<> Justin Wu: But I can still compose and present one. Ann Jiang: Correct. I trust you can write a compelling report.;<> Ann Jiang: A division lead shouldn't present anything to the board, but that can be taken care of. Justin Wu: ... Ann Jiang: You don't have to exaggerate. Just document everything as it is.;<> Ann Jiang: We both know this farm is beyond saving. It's a fact. Ann Jiang: Now we need to show this reality to the rest of them. Justin Wu: But... Ann Jiang: You know better than anyone else, that the Screening division has been easing the qualification of juicers.;<> Ann Jiang: And they're paying them more. Ann Jiang: How could they have done that? Justin Wu: I think it's because— Ann Jiang: I'm asking you, the other guy.;<> Thought: Why me!?;<> Ann Jiang: Consider it a test. I wonder what kind of people Mr. Wu works with. Frances Cheng(c): I think it's because they're backed by certain members of the board... <> <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->They're backed by certain members of the board...;<> Ann Jiang: ...Correct.;<> Ann Jiang: You have smart people, Mr. Wu.;<> Shane Yan: You think a lot like Ms. Cheng.;<> Thought: Yan flashed me a smile that is probably more menacing than encouraging...;<> Ann Jiang: You're correct. ->Because they are nice people. Ann Jiang: Where did they get the money? Justin Wu: Their expenditure should be way more than the total budget... ->I don't know. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ann Jiang: Certain members of the board are secretly funding them to create an illusion that this farm can still sustain itself.;<>;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Like how certain other members are sabotaging the Cellar. Ann Jiang: ...;<> Ann Jiang: It's called planned phasing out. Justin Wu: But—;<> Ann Jiang: It's not your turn to speak yet. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Ann Jiang: Listen, Mr. Wu. Ann Jiang: This farm is no longer sustainable. Juicers will keep coming if we keep it running. Ann Jiang: Better shut it down before it implodes. Justin Wu: ...;<>;<> Justin Wu: I need to have a talk with the Screening team. As you said...;<> Ann Jiang: Vision competes with technological advancement.;<>;<> Ann Jiang: We have come this far because we value technological talents. We let them take leadership. Ann Jiang: The Screening division and the directors behind them tend to make things unnecessarily complicated.;<> Ann Jiang: Don't let them distract you, acting lead of Ops. Shane Yan: Acting lead.;<> Shane Yan: Not a very nice title, is it? Shane Yan: Work with Ms. Jiang, and we can make you the permanent lead. Or even the farm manager.;<> Shane Yan: This farm can be yours, if you manage to complete the report within today. Shane Yan: And when it's shut down, you will have a matching position elsewhere. Justin Wu: ...Within today?;<> Ann Jiang: Don't tell me you can't?;<> Ann Jiang: If so, this meeting is a complete waste of time.;<> Justin Wu: I need time to collect data, I—;<> Ann Jiang: I'm giving you a whole day for it.;<> Ann Jiang: Do you still think you have time to consult the Screening team? Justin Wu: ...;<> Ann Jiang: We value talents, Mr. Wu.;<> Ann Jiang: From this brief meeting, I can already tell you're a smart man. Ann Jiang: Think carefully. This offer won't stand forever.;<> Justin Wu: ...;<> Justin Wu: I have one demand. Shane Yan: You're not in a position to—;<>;<> Ann Jiang: Let him speak. Justin Wu: For every contract terminated due to farm closure, we pay them the maximum amount allowed by the terms.;<>;<>;<> Ann Jiang: ...You are talking about a lot of money. Justin Wu: Not so much, comparing it against the cost of keeping Silencio running.;<> Ann Jiang: You're not our only card, Mr. Wu. We don't have to do things this way. Justin Wu: I'm your best card. You have other alternatives, but not necessarily better. Ann Jiang: ...;<> Ann Jiang: I said we value talents.;<> Ann Jiang: OK, I can arrange that.;<> Justin Wu: ...Thank you. Ann Jiang: That seals our deal. I'm looking forward to your report.;<>;<> Ann Jiang: Finish it and submit within today, or else there is no deal. Ann Jiang: I have work at the lab, Mr. Yan. Nice meeting you.;<> Shane Yan: Oh, don't let me keep you. There's a direct elevator to the helipad.;<> Ann Jiang: That was the plan. Aren't you going? Shane Yan: I'd like to have a few more words with them. Ann Jiang: Suit yourself.;<> <> //江小姐走掉走調 <> <>;<> <<#Wait(4)>> Shane Yan: ...;<> Shane Yan: ...;<>;<> Shane Yan: Let me make this clear, Justin. Shane Yan: I don't care if this farm stays or goes. Shane Yan: Either way is fine, as long as we can break this status quo.;<> Shane Yan: Silencio is my worst-performing asset, if it can still be called an asset. Shane Yan: You have a chance to end it all. Don't let it go to waste. Shane Yan: You'd be doing everyone a favor, including our contributors. Justin Wu: I know that. Shane Yan: Good.;<> Shane Yan: And you, Jason. Thought: That creepy smile again.;<> Shane Yan: I have one question. What's the relationship between you and Ms. Frances Cheng? //開始微弱GLITCH Frances Cheng(c): Don't tell him! //if剛剛說出程芳的推論關於董事 <<#If($DLStateFloat=1)>> Shane Yan: Ah, sorry. I suppose this feels irrelevant.;<> Shane Yan: I was just intrigued that you said something that Ms. Cheng would have said. Shane Yan: Are you close to her?;<> <<#EndIf>> Frances Cheng(c): J, just tell him I asked you to check on Ms. Yeh. <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She's my girlfriend, you piece of junk.;<> Justin Wu: Jason!? Frances Cheng(c): JASON!? <> Shane Yan: ...Hey, what's this about? Shane Yan: You're Ms. Cheng's boyfriend? How surprising.;<> Shane Yan: Although I don't understand where this hostility comes from. Have I done anything wrong?;<> Shane Yan: Ah, right. Ms. Cheng had an accident, and is currently missing.;<> Shane Yan: You must be worried sick. Shane Yan: Do you blame me for failing to protect my employees?;<> Shane Yan: In a sense, I should be responsible for her. I'm the CEO, after all. Shane Yan: I have told them to enhance safety measures at HQ. Hopefully that helps;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->We both know what you did to her. //GLITCH再增強 Shane Yan: ...;<> Shane Yan: I won't argue, because I see you're blinded by grief.;<> Shane Yan: Don't be too hard on yourself.;<> <> Thought: Motherfucker! How could you say that with a straight face? ->Sorry, I should have got a grip. Shane Yan: It's OK. I know how you feel. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: Let's talk about something less personal.;<>;<> ->She asked me to check on Ms. Yeh. Shane Yan: Hmm, that does sound like her style.;<> Shane Yan: She's proactive and thorough, often pointing out the root cause before an issue fully festers.;<> Shane Yan: I guess she must have found out Ms. Yeh was stealing company secrets. Shane Yan: Good.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: I have one more question. Shane Yan: Are you aware that the Flop Core of Silencio was infiltrated yesterday?;<> Justin Wu: ...I heard about it. There are IIB agents everywhere. Shane Yan: Heh. Hopefully they're not disrupting your daily workflow. Shane Yan: What about you, Mr. Dai? <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Never heard of it.;<> Shane Yan: Surely you have.;<> Shane Yan: Didn't you just talk to that IIB officer? Yuna Hsu. Thought: How does he know...!? Frances Cheng(c): Think twice before you say anything, J... Frances Cheng(c): Don't let him find out about your deal with Yuna. <> Shane Yan: I thought you were talking about the Flop Core, no? <<@不然徐媛媛是為什麼找上你啊啊？>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She was asking me about Ms. Yeh. Shane Yan: How strange.;<>;<> Shane Yan: She submitted a closing report yesterday. I thought the case was closed. Thought: Dammit! She did mention the closing report. I forgot!;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Maybe they had more discoveries. Shane Yan: New discoveries, huh? Shane Yan: Could be. ->That you should ask her. ->I don't know. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: I will have a separate conversation with her later.;<>;<> Thought: I'm counting on you, Hsu.;<> ->She asked me some questions but didn't tell me what it was about. Shane Yan: I see. She does that a lot.;<>;<> Shane Yan: But as far as I know, she seldom does field work, unless there's a very good reason. Thought: How much does he know...?;<> <<#EndSimple>> <<@我聽說過她會或多或少的威脅調查的員工。>> Shane Yan: And I heard she employs unorthodox approaches in her investigations, such as threats and coercion. Shane Yan: Hopefully she didn't do that to you? <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She didn't. Shane Yan: Good to know.;<>;<> ->Can you do anything about it if she did? Shane Yan: I'm sorry to say I can't.;<>;<> Shane Yan: The IIB answers directly to the board of directors. All I can do is to file a complaint. Shane Yan: Don't hesitate to let me know, if you need me to do that.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Thank you. Shane Yan: Don't mention it. ->Come on, you're the CEO. Shane Yan: Haha, the CEO is just another employee. I answer to the board, too. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I heard about it, too. Shane Yan: I thought so.;<>;<> Shane Yan: Ms. Yuna Hsu is in charge of the investigation and she had a meeting with you earlier today. Shane Yan: The meeting was about the Flop Core, wasn't it?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. Shane Yan: Oh? What else could it be? <<#GoTo(@不然徐媛媛是為什麼找上你啊啊？)>> ->Yeah. Shane Yan: I thought so.;<>;<> Shane Yan: But as far as I know, she seldom does field work, unless there's a very good reason. Shane Yan: This is getting interesting.;<> <<#GoTo(@我聽說過她會或多或少的威脅調查的員工。)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: Alright. I've said it all. Shane Yan: Don't fail us, manager-to-be. Justin Wu: ...I won't. //執行長走掉 <> <>;<> <<#Wait(4)>> Frances Cheng(c): Phew, that ended better than I expected.;<> <> Thought: How much does Yan know...? Thought: Is he already suspecting me? Thought: I can't stay here much longer. They could catch me any time. //if剛剛罵妍繩和 <<#If($CurseBossHurtFrances=0)>> Justin Wu: Why did you say that to Mr. Yan, Jason?;<> Justin Wu: Ms. Cheng is your girlfriend? The farm supervisor? Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Do you really think Mr. Yan is responsible for what happened to her? Frances Cheng(c): Don't tell him, J. He's better off not knowing about it. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sorry, I was pointing fingers. I didn't mean it. <> Justin Wu: It's alright. Justin Wu: ... ->Right, he's a murderer. Frances Cheng(c): Jason...!? <> Justin Wu: ... Thought: He's visibly shocked. Thought: I've hardly ever seen so much surprise on his face... Justin Wu: I'd otherwise pursue this subject further, but... Justin Wu: Let's just say, contact the police if you have any evidence or proof, Jason. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You believe me? Justin Wu: I— Justin Wu: I'm not sure.;<> Justin Wu: The bottom line is, I don't want to consider the possibility that you're insane. Justin Wu: This is putting me in a very difficult position...;<> ->The police won't help. Justin Wu: ...You think so? Justin Wu: In that case, there's nothing you can do. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Anyway, I respect your privacy.;<> Justin Wu: If you do need help, come to me any time. <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Thank you. ->Sure. Justin Wu: Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: So...;<> <<#EndIf>> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: What do you make of it, Jason?;<> Justin Wu: About their proposal. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm not sure if shutting down the farm is the right thing to do. Justin Wu: It's absolutely right.;<> Justin Wu: Admittedly, it's not good. A lot of people won't like the idea. Justin Wu: But considering everything, it's the least harmful option. Otherwise people will die. Justin Wu: At this stage, there is no such thing as a perfect solution.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->There could be other ways if we work together with Screening. Justin Wu: I've been thinking about that... ->What's your plan? ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@具嗯說時間不夠讓阿緯理解自己唉唉)>> ->What do you think? <<@具嗯說時間不夠讓阿緯理解自己唉唉>> Justin Wu: I'm trying to find a way to get through to Wayne. Justin Wu: If we can get Screening on our side, the whole situation will be a lot easier to navigate.;<> Justin Wu: But like she said, we don't have that much time. Justin Wu: Now is the time to choose between pragmatism and idealism.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Let's not go to extremes? Justin Wu: Not extremes. It's a matter of necessity. Justin Wu: There is no perfect solution. We must choose a path with the least damage. ->I'd say pragmatism. Justin Wu: Yes, it's the most reasonable. ->What does this have to do with pragmatism or idealism? Justin Wu: Wayne firmly believes this farm could help more people. It's just wishful thinking.;<> Justin Wu: He doesn't understand the technical difficulties, and refuses to listen to the professionals. Justin Wu: We are the professionals and we make practical decisions.;<> Justin Wu: You could say Wayne is a different kind of professional, but his profession doesn't count in this matter. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: We are dealing with real life, not an imagined perfect world.;<> Justin Wu: If given unlimited time, maybe I'd convince him bit by bit. Justin Wu: The truth is we have mere hours to write this report. Time is not on our side. Justin Wu: I must take actions, by brute force if necessary. Justin Wu: I will inform Wayne of this. He has the right to know.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Won't he fight back? Justin Wu: That won't make a difference. He must live with it. ->Why tell him? Justin Wu: We may not agree with each other, but we have both bet our lives on the fate of this farm. Justin Wu: He deserves to know. ->I agree. Justin Wu: Thank you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Now, I must inform the entire Ops staff.;<>;<>;//俊恩用投影或手機 Justin Wu: For the report, we have to handle it on our own. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why? ->Why don't we ask the team for help? Justin Wu: The team is taking today off. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Between you and me, we must somehow figure out a way to get it done. Justin Wu: Let's go to the office.;<> <<#End>>

title: 遇到鄭緯
body: //到電梯時政委剛好出來看到菌嗯衝過來 //正偉機動站姿 Wayne Zheng: JUSTIN WU! Justin Wu: Wayne. Justin Wu: You found out. Wayne Zheng: ...I knew you want to destroy this farm! Justin Wu: You still don't get it? Justin Wu: This farm has been destroying itself for a long time.;<> Wayne Zheng: ...Listen to me, just for once, please. Wayne Zheng: Don't do it. You have no idea what the consequences are. Justin Wu: You should know the consequences if I don't do it.;<> Wayne Zheng: Damn...;<> Wayne Zheng: You're ruining everyone's hard work! Justin Wu: Wrong. I'm freeing everyone from this ticking bomb. Wayne Zheng: Why the hell can't you just listen for once!?;<> Justin Wu: I could say the same to you.;<> Justin Wu: I know you're frustrated, Wayne. You really believe you're on the right path. Justin Wu: Face it, man. Belief doesn't count. Reality is what it is.;<> Justin Wu: The reality of Silencio is that it's retirement is overdue. Wayne Zheng: ...Enough.;<> Justin Wu: What? Wayne Zheng: I said ENOUGH!!!;<> Wayne Zheng: I've had enough of this bullshit!!! Justin Wu: Calm down, Wayne. Wayne Zheng: I'm done listening!!;<> Wayne Zheng: You've been repeating this arrogant crap over and over and over again! Wayne Zheng: Every. Single. Time. The same thing!;<> Wayne Zheng: Yapping and yapping and yapping and yapping! Wayne Zheng: You never cared about my thoughts and difficulties! Wayne Zheng: Why are you so afraid to listen? Why keep drowning me out!?;<> Justin Wu: I'm afraid you're wrong. I have been listening. Justin Wu: I have been listening all this time. It's just that we are not equal.;<> Justin Wu: You have not worked in the Cellar, and don't have the technological know-how to understand our issues. Wayne Zheng: That crap again...;<> Justin Wu: I know you have spent time and effort trying to tackle the problem in your own way. Justin Wu: But at the end of the day, good intentions must give way to practicality, right? Justin Wu: I do admit that I've been putting my own opinions before yours.;<> Justin Wu: But that is well-justified, I believe, because on this subject I do have more knowledge. Justin Wu: Sometimes you need to listen, my friend. Wayne Zheng: I'm not here for a debate. I'm warning you of grave consequences. Wayne Zheng: Stop before it's too late, Justin.;<> Justin Wu: Calm down. Don't make a scene in the public—;<> Wayne Zheng: I DON'T FUCKING CARE!;<> Wayne Zheng: Do you have any idea how many people depend on this farm as their last income? Wayne Zheng: Do you know how many within this farm will be left jobless? The whole Screening team— Justin Wu: I see. This is your concern. Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Justin Wu: You feel guilty that your people will lose their jobs once Silencio is shut down. Wayne Zheng: I'm not— Justin Wu: You just don't want to be the bad guy. For that, you're willing to do anything. Wayne Zheng: It's not like—;<> Justin Wu: Stop. You're only proving me right.;<> Wayne Zheng: YOU DON'T KNOW SHIT!;<> Wayne Zheng: You don't feel a thing because you've never been a division lead! Wayne Zheng: You're not held responsible for your people. Wayne Zheng: All your talk about knowledge and technology!;<> Wayne Zheng: It's an excuse to hide your ignorance!!! <<#Wait(2)>> <> Wayne Zheng: I'm...;<> Justin Wu: ...;<> Justin Wu: I know your frustration, Wayne. Justin Wu: I know you're a good man, a capable man.;<> Justin Wu: But what we're dealing with, even you must agree, is not about what we want.;<> Wayne Zheng: ...;//政委低頭糾結 Justin Wu: I have important work to do, and not enough time to do it. Justin Wu: If you'd like, we can continue this conversation another day. Justin Wu: Goodbye. <<#End>>

title: 操作路線_地窖電梯
body:
Justin Wu: ...
Justin Wu: ...
Justin Wu: Jason.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站左看)>>
Justin Wu: Honestly, I was only hoping to talk them into shutting down the farm in a less disruptive way.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_正站右看)>>
Justin Wu: Much to my surprise, they had everything planned out, and even offered to let me lead the process.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Congrats!
    Justin Wu: I don't know if this is something worth celebrating...
->Isn't that good news?
    Justin Wu: It's a lot of pressure for me.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: Now I have the power to dispose of the Screening division.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->What will you do?
    //
->That sounds scary.
    Justin Wu: Why? You think I'll make their life hard?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Probably...?
        //
    ->Aren't you planning to fire the whole division?
        Justin Wu: ...
        Justin Wu: Ultimately, yes.
    ->I don't mean that.
        Justin Wu: It's OK. I know your concern.
    <<#EndSimple>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: ...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站左看返回)>>
Justin Wu: This is what I think.
Justin Wu: Once farm closure is authorized, and I'm given the authority...
Justin Wu: I'll start to dismantle the Screening division.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站左看)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->So soon?
    Justin Wu: Yes.
->Why?
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: Because the first thing we need to do is to make sure we stop taking in new juicers.
Justin Wu: In that case, there's no need for the Screening division anymore.
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->You're right.
    //
->But that's cruel.
    Justin Wu: ...I know.
->Can't you assign them other jobs?
    Justin Wu: That's the plan.
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: I will keep about two thirds of the Screening staff.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站左看返回)>>
Justin Wu: Form them into a new division, dedicated to handling contract termination.
Justin Wu: I need them to ensure juicers' benefits and welfare in the process.
<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站左看)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Will you keep Wayne?
    Justin Wu: I'd like to, although it's ultimately up to him.
    Justin Wu: ...And you have seen what he's like.
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->He just needs some time to calm down.
        Justin Wu: ...Hopefully.
    ->Yeah.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
->What about afterwards? Will they be fired?
    Justin Wu: Of course. Eventually, everyone will take a severance package and go.
    Justin Wu: The farm itself will be gone, after all.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: I know you're worried about the Screening team. I don't want to see them jobless, either.
Justin Wu: I will try everything I can to send them to other Vision farms.
Justin Wu: There is no guarantee, though. There will be people left out in the cold.
Justin Wu: They will blame me. For that I'm mentally prepared.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站左看返回)>>
Thought: He's showing a side that I've never seen before...
<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_正站右看返回)>>
<<#Wait(2)>>
Justin Wu: ...
Justin Wu: I know what I'm doing will hurt many.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站低頭)>>
Justin Wu: But on the other hand, if I don't do it, I will hurt more.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_正站右看)>>
Justin Wu: Doing the right thing comes with consequences.
Justin Wu: It's the necessary evil.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站低頭返回)>>
Justin Wu: Or so I believe.
Justin Wu: ...
Justin Wu: Do you agree, Jason?;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站左看)>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->I do.
    Thought: He seems much relieved.
    Justin Wu: ...
->You should ask yourself.
    Justin Wu: Yeah...
->I wouldn't do that if I were you.
    Justin Wu: Is that so...
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Do you regret?
        Justin Wu: ...
        Justin Wu: ...No. I stand by my decision.
    ->But I won't blame you.
        Justin Wu: Thank you, Jason.
        Thought: A weary smile.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
->Do you need my affirmation?
    Justin Wu: ...
    Justin Wu: ...No. I stand by my decision.
<<#EndSimple>>
<<PlayUntil(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站左看返回)>>;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_正站右看返回)>>
<<#Wait(3)>>
Justin Wu: ...
Justin Wu: I shouldn't have asked you, Jason.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站左看)>>
Justin Wu: Sorry.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_正站右看)>>
Justin Wu: ...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站左看返回)>>
Justin Wu: Maybe...
Justin Wu: Maybe I'm just trying to offload my guilt onto anyone who happens to be nearby.
Justin Wu: I've always wanted to do this. Now that it's done, all I feel is...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站低頭)>>
Justin Wu: Fear.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->The whole Ops team is on your side.
    Justin Wu: ...
    Justin Wu: I guess that's the most encouraging thing I'd like to hear right now.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站低頭轉左看)>>
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Come on, don't be so pessimistic.
    ->Then show me some spirit! Get encouraged!
        Justin Wu: Haha...
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Justin Wu: I mean it.
    Justin Wu: Thank you, Jason.
->You'll get used to it soon enough.
    Justin Wu: That's what I'm afraid of...
    Justin Wu: Getting used to making decisions that could hurt people.
    Justin Wu: That would be horrible...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站低頭轉左看)>>
    Justin Wu: Or so it seems to me, here and now.
->To fear is human.
    Justin Wu: You mean I don't need to worry about it.
    Justin Wu: Do you?;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站低頭轉左看)>>
    Justin Wu: Haha...
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: ...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_正站左看返回)>>
Justin Wu: Here we are. Let's go.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_正站右看返回)>>
<<#End>>

title: 主動安慰鄭緯
body:
//玩家可以按e跟鄭緯對話
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Are you OK?
    Wayne Zheng: ...
    Wayne Zheng: No.
->You should cooperate with Justin.
    Wayne Zheng: Like hell I am.
->I'm sorry.
    Wayne Zheng: For what?
    Wayne Zheng: Isn't this what you wanted?
<<#EndSimple>>
Wayne Zheng: I shouldn't have come. I thought maybe he'd listen for once...
Wayne Zheng: I should have seen this coming and taken actions...
<<#Simple(8)>>
->You're not thinking straight.;<<Set($MCJudgeAWei,1)>>;//紀錄下次遇到阿緯他會用這句反擊主角
->You're making a fool of yourself.;<<Set($MCJudgeAWei,2)>>;//紀錄下次遇到阿緯他會用這句反擊主角
    Wayne Zheng: ...
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Wayne Zheng: You have made your choice, Jason.
Wayne Zheng: Now leave me alone.
Thought: He won't listen to me...
<<#End>>

title: 操作路線_告知要去說服采欣
body: Justin Wu: Sorry for taking up more of your time. <<#Simple(8)>> ->It's nothing. ->What's up? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: I need you to get Cathy on our side. We need her help. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Help with what? ->Wait. Why? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: To make a report as compelling as can be, I need data from the Screening division.;<> Justin Wu: Of course Wayne won't give me anything. Justin Wu: Now I need someone within Screening to get what I need.;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->You're asking her to betray her colleagues. Justin Wu: ...That is one way of putting it.;<> Justin Wu: Although I'd rather not see it that way. Justin Wu: We're working towards the greater good. This is above loyalty and betrayal.;<> ->Do we absolutely need this data? Justin Wu: We need anything and everything to make the report more convincing. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: I need Screening data for an aftermath plan.;<> Justin Wu: As part of the closure process, we need to unrack juicers in waves, ahead of contract terms. Justin Wu: To plan for their compensation and medical assistance, we need crucial data from Screening. Justin Wu: This section is critical if we want the directors to actually buy our report.;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->Because they will want a precise cost projection. Justin Wu: They will want to see a figure that is not too high. ->Because you want to make sure every juicer is fairly treated. Justin Wu: Yes. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: I need to make a plan that ensures juicer benefits, without sending the overall cost through the roof. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Can't we do it without Screening's help? Justin Wu: Right. They are in charge of juicer management.;<> ->But won't the juicers fight us for as much payout as possible? Justin Wu: ...They will.;<> Justin Wu: This will be a tough challenge. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: We need the complete data on personal profiles, contract terms, pre-racking physical metrics, so on and so forth.;<> Justin Wu: Cathy once told me she manages the Screening database. Justin Wu: That means she has access to everything we need. Justin Wu: Now I need you to convince her to work with us.;<> <<#Simple(10)>> ->Why don't you just ask her yourself? ->Why me? Justin Wu: I don't have time for that. Justin Wu: Of the entire Ops team, you're the only one who's relatively welcomed in the Screening office. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Or is it because there's nothing else I can do? Justin Wu: That is one of the reasons. Thought: My feelings, man. ->What about Jesse? Justin Wu: Her personality is not very compatible with the task.;<> Justin Wu: She's kinda... Justin Wu: The opposite of diplomatic?;<> Thought: Well said.;<> Justin Wu: Plus, I have more important work for her. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->I see. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: The aftermath plan will make our report more convincing, but that's not the fundamental goal. Justin Wu: The bottom line is that we must ensure every juicer is treated properly and fairly. Justin Wu: Can I leave this to you? <>;//控制是否已經答應，1等於答應 <>;//控制是否已經問過問題，1等於問過問題 <> <> <> <<@俊恩問要不要幫忙說服采欣>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sure, you can count on me.;<> Justin Wu: Thank you.;<> //if沒問問題。 <<#If($DLStateFloat2=0)>> Justin Wu: I didn't expect you to agree so easily. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You thought I'd refuse? Justin Wu: I was expecting questions. ->We're beyond the questioning phase. It's do or die now. Thought: He's smiling. Justin Wu: Agreed. Justin Wu: I'm glad to have you on our side. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Any questions before you go? <<@答應說服采欣前問俊恩各種問題集合2>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Is it really OK to ask Cathy to betray her team? ($DLStateFloat4) <<#GoTo(@要求采欣背叛同事真的好嗎？)>> ->You seriously believe Silencio is beyond fixing? ($DLStateFloat5) <<#GoTo(@你覺得寂靜真的不可能被修復嗎？)>> ->I don't have any questions. Justin Wu: Good. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> <<@問完決定幫俊恩說服采欣問題>> Justin Wu: Cathy should be in the Screening office.;<> Justin Wu: Maybe take her to somewhere with more privacy. Justin Wu: Oh, it's not that urgent.;<> Justin Wu: Feel free to take your time. Justin Wu: Let me know when it's done. <<#End>> ->I don't want to do this. ($DLStateFloat6) <> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Why? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I don't want to shut down the farm. Justin Wu: After everything, now you're telling me that!?;<> Thought: He's stupefied... Justin Wu: If you still don't think this place must be shut down, well... Justin Wu: ...;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Are you angry? Justin Wu: Yes. Justin Wu: You've seen everything and you choose to do nothing about it. ->Sorry. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: I'm not asking you. Consider it an order from your acting division lead. Justin Wu: Feel free to disagree with me. An order is an order. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Fair enough. Justin Wu: Yeah. ->You're forcing me against my will. Justin Wu: That I am. Welcome to the adult world.;<> Justin Wu: I don't want to, but for the greater good, I have to. Justin Wu: Sorry, Jason.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Oh, it's not that urgent. Justin Wu: Feel free to take your time. <<#End>> ->I don't trust Yan or the unnamed director. Justin Wu: Do you have any better alternative? Thought: He's expecting an answer. This is not a rhetorical question. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not yet. ->We should find one, together. Justin Wu: In other words, you don't have any. Justin Wu: ... ->What about you? Justin Wu: Of course I don't. Justin Wu: Or else I wouldn't do what I'm doing right now. <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Every action has risks, Jason. Take them, or we can never have anything done. Justin Wu: Any other questions? <<#GoTo(@俊恩問要不要幫忙說服采欣)>> ->I still think you have a chance with Wayne. Justin Wu: Impossible. Justin Wu: This is crystal clear. //if玩家剛剛在電梯有說覺得啊偉峻嗯之後很難和好 <<#If(SaidCantGetBackTogether=true)>> Justin Wu: Didn't you just say so? Justin Wu: I'd be lucky if he sees me as a friend at all. <<#EndIf>> Justin Wu: Any other questions? <<#GoTo(@俊恩問要不要幫忙說服采欣)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I have some questions.;<> <>;//控制這裡不繼續問問題的選項 Justin Wu: Ask away. <<@答應說服采欣前問俊恩各種問題集合>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Is it really OK to ask Cathy to betray her team? ($DLStateFloat4) <<@要求采欣背叛同事真的好嗎？>> <> <> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: This is above loyalty or betrayal. <<#Simple(6)>> ->This is betrayal no matter how you slice it. Justin Wu: ...Let me reiterate. I don't see it that way. Justin Wu: Of course, everyone is entitled to their own judgments. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Ultimately it depends on Cathy.;<> Justin Wu: I'm hoping to convince her with reason. Justin Wu: Just provide her with the whole background, and tell her that I respect her decisions.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Roger. // ->Respect her own decisions? Justin Wu: Yes. This part is critical. Don't forget it. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Cathy is sensitive. The last thing I want is to coerce her into cooperation with peer pressure.;<> Justin Wu: I'm counting on you. Justin Wu: ...Any other questions?;<> //if答應後才問 <<#If($DLStateFloat=1)>> <<#GoTo(@答應說服采欣前問俊恩各種問題集合2)>> <<#EndIf>> <<#GoTo(@答應說服采欣前問俊恩各種問題集合)>> ->You seriously believe Silencio is beyond fixing? ($DLStateFloat5) <<@你覺得寂靜真的不可能被修復嗎？>> <> <> Justin Wu: Technically, there is hope. Justin Wu: Although that will require an impossibly large budget that we will never get. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What if we ask the director for it? Justin Wu: He has no reason to approve. Justin Wu: All that the director cares about is the balance sheet.;<> Justin Wu: Fixing Silencio requires investments that will never see any real return. Justin Wu: It doesn't make any sense from his perspective.;<> ->What if we all chip in by taking less salary? Justin Wu: ...Did Wayne sell you this idea? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What idea? ->He probably did mention it. ->Nope. <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: He believes the Cellar doesn't have enough repair budget because we regularly raise our salary. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Do you raise your salary? Justin Wu: We. You're included, too.;<> ->Do we get paid that much? Justin Wu: Not very much. Justin Wu: We do have steady raises, though... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: It's in line with company regulations, written in our employment contracts.;<> Justin Wu: Waiving salary raises would be violating employee benefits. Justin Wu: Even if we do that, it's still far from enough.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> //if答應後才問 <<#If($DLStateFloat=1)>> <<#GoTo(@答應說服采欣前問俊恩各種問題集合2)>> <<#EndIf>> <<#GoTo(@答應說服采欣前問俊恩各種問題集合)>> ->I have no more questions. ($DLStateFloat3);//問過一個選項在出現 Justin Wu: Good. <<#GoTo(@問完決定幫俊恩說服采欣問題)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>>

title: 篩選路線_告知要說服賴潔
body: //到他座位以後 Wayne Zheng: Phew...;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->How are you feeling? Wayne Zheng: Not too bad. ->Justin was seriously losing it. Wayne Zheng: I've hardly ever seen him so emotional. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: He knows there's nothing he can do at this stage.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why's that? Wayne Zheng: I have the CEO and a director behind me. He can't win. Thought: Wayne doesn't seem to be very happy about it, though. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I see. // ->What if he tries to sabotage the Cellar? <<#GoTo(@問阿緯峻嗯打亂報告計畫怎辦)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->What if he tries to sabotage our efforts? <<@問阿緯峻嗯打亂報告計畫怎辦>> Wayne Zheng: He won't. Wayne Zheng: Sabotaging our efforts will make me lose, but won't let him win.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What do you mean? Wayne Zheng: I mean even if he somehow manages to stop me this time... ->If you lose, he wins. Wayne Zheng: To him, winning means shutting down Silencio for good.;<> Wayne Zheng: Even if he somehow manages to stop me this time... <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: That will leave Silencio in limbo, indefinitely.;<> Wayne Zheng: We don't agree with each other, but we both want to navigate Silencio towards the greater good. Wayne Zheng: Or so I believe. Wayne Zheng: He may disagree, though. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: I swear I respect and trust Justin's technological capabilities. Wayne Zheng: He's a good man, a capable man, since the first day I knew him.;<> Wayne Zheng: I have no doubt that he knows the Cellar better than I do, and I never questioned his knowledge. Wayne Zheng: It's him who refuses to listen.;<> Wayne Zheng: The way I see it, we represent two entirely different way of thinking. Wayne Zheng: He's the practical kind, while I plan things on a more idealistic scale. Wayne Zheng: I do agree that we all live in the here and now, but if we don't look a few steps ahead, there won't be any progress.;<> Wayne Zheng: My approach is to push the boundaries of what's possible toward the idealist goal, one inch at a time. Wayne Zheng: Justin, on the other hand, is the "if it's not happening now, it's never gonna happen" kind of guy. Wayne Zheng: When challenged, he tends to cite limitations as excuses.;<> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: He sees me as a layman with the delusion of knowing everything. Wayne Zheng: That is not true. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You should tell that to Justin. Wayne Zheng: I just tried.;<> Wayne Zheng: You saw how that worked. <> ->You still wish to work with him, right? Thought: A sad smile. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Phew... Wayne Zheng: OK. I need you to do something for me.;<> Wayne Zheng: To prepare this report, I need something from Ops. Thought: He said from Ops, not Justin.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->What could you do about it? Wayne Zheng: I have my ways. ->From Ops, not Justin. Thought: Wayne is a bit surprised by what I said. Wayne Zheng: Yes, you're right. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: I need you to get Jesse on our side. We need her help. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why her? Wayne Zheng: Because I have worked with her from time to time. ->I don't think she'd agree. Wayne Zheng: She will. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Everything I know about the Cellar, I learned it from Jesse.;<> Wayne Zheng: She is one of the few, if not the only, people in Ops who sees this issue rationally. Wayne Zheng: My plan for the Cellar is based on her knowledge, not my own wishful thinking.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why do you think she needs any convincing, then? Wayne Zheng: Offering opinions and formally taking our side are very different things. Wayne Zheng: For this report, I need her to provide me with much more information, a large part of it considered classified. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I see. // ->Sounds like you're asking her to betray her team. Wayne Zheng: Some may see it that way, although I wouldn't agree.;<> Wayne Zheng: What we're dealing with is too important to be measured by moral standards, Jason. Thought: I didn't expect that from him.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Why me? Wayne Zheng: I'm occupied, and you're the only person here who knows her. <<#Simple(10)>> ->Barely. I only met her a couple of times. Wayne Zheng: And that's a couple of times more than everyone else. ->Or is it because there's nothing else I can do? Wayne Zheng: You know what? Yes, kinda, haha... ->What about Cathy? Wayne Zheng: She's... Wayne Zheng: She's not the best person to do it, after what Justin did. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <> <> <> <<@問完決定幫阿緯說服賴潔問題>> Wayne Zheng: Anyway. Wayne Zheng: Consider convincing Jesse your next task, will you? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No problem, you can count on me. //if直接答應，有問過問題DLStateFloat起碼會減過一次 <<#If($DLStateFloat=3)>> Wayne Zheng: Oh... <<#Simple(6)>> ->What's up? Wayne Zheng: Nothing. I'm just surprised that you agreed right away. Wayne Zheng: I thought there would be questions. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: Good. <<@答應幫忙啊緯說服賴潔拉呀摁>> Wayne Zheng: I'll leave it to you, then.;<> Wayne Zheng: Jesse is in her office. I've assigned you elevator access. Wayne Zheng: I have sent her a notice. The rest is up to you. Wayne Zheng: Go, Jason. <<#End>> ->I don't think it's appropriate. Wayne Zheng: Why's that? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Fundamentally, I don't think this farm should stay. Wayne Zheng: You're in support of shutting it down? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Not that, either. Wayne Zheng: Then it means keeping the status quo. That is the worst outcome, for everyone! Wayne Zheng: You know the Cellar is full of problems, and you know that people rely on this farm... Wayne Zheng: The right thing to do is to get the Cellar working again, don't you think so? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I just can't agree with your approach. // ->I think you should establish an agreement with Ops, officially. Wayne Zheng: I would if I could. The thing is, we're running out of time. Wayne Zheng: I thought we were pretty clear on that. <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Anyway, at this stage we're out of time. <<#GoTo(@反對說服賴潔但還是得去做)>> ->Yes. It should be shut down. Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: After everything I said, everything you saw, that's what you think...? Wayne Zheng: Maybe I don't know you, after all.;<> Wayne Zheng: Or maybe you have seen something that I overlooked. Wayne Zheng: Whichever case it is, I don't have time to convince you.;<> <<@反對說服賴潔但還是得去做>> Wayne Zheng: Follow my order for now. We can continue this conversation some other day. <<#Simple(8)>> ->OK. Roger. // ->Are you angry? Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: No, just a bit disappointed. Wayne Zheng: OK, I have a huge report to write. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@答應幫忙啊緯說服賴潔拉呀摁)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I don't trust Yan or the unnamed director. Wayne Zheng: Only they can make things happen. Wayne Zheng: I don't trust them either, and I have a backup plan if refuse to provide me with the promised resources.;<> Wayne Zheng: Rest assured that I'm thoroughly prepared. Wayne Zheng: Every action has risks, Jason. Take them, or we can never have anything done.;<> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Anyway, at this stage we're out of time. <<#GoTo(@反對說服賴潔但還是得去做)>> ->I still think you have a chance with Justin. Wayne Zheng: I would if I could. The thing is, we're running out of time. Wayne Zheng: I thought we were pretty clear on that. <<#GoTo(@反對說服賴潔但還是得去做)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I have a few questions. ($DLStateFloat);//選問完問題以後此選像消失 Wayne Zheng: Ask, please. <<@答應說服賴潔前問阿緯各種問題集合>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What does Jesse feel about keeping Silencio running? ($DLStateFloat2);//選過消失 <> <> <> Wayne Zheng: She agrees the farm is in terrible shape, and that it can be saved, given enough resources.;<> Wayne Zheng: Now, backed by the CEO and a director, it could actually happen. Thought: He sounds very confident. I guess he did plenty of research.;<> <<#GoTo(@答應說服賴潔前問阿緯各種問題集合)>> ->Is this really not about the Screening team? ($DLStateFloat3);//選過消失 <> <> <> Wayne Zheng: You mean what Justin said, huh... Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Thought: He seems to be hesitating. Wayne Zheng: The Screening team is part of the reason. That much I won't argue. Wayne Zheng: Fundamentally, though, I believe I represent the best interest of everyone.;<> Wayne Zheng: Is it wrong if the greater good happens to be aligned with the lesser good? Thought: Despite saying that, I can tell he has doubts. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->It's not wrong, although it makes your cause seem somehow selfish. Wayne Zheng: You think I'm selfish. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I said seem selfish. ->No, I don't. Wayne Zheng: I see... Thought: I can't tell what he's thinking. ->Yeah, I do think that. Wayne Zheng: OK, I see.;<> Thought: He's taking it calmer than I expected. <<#EndSimple>> ->You're doing good indeed. Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Thank you.;<> Thought: He needs every bit of affirmation he can get. Thought: Or else it's gonna be an infinite loop of doubting himself. ->I don't know. Wayne Zheng: Is that so... ->The cause is good, but your approach is not. Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Huh, I see. Thought: He's taking it calmer than I expected. <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@答應說服賴潔前問阿緯各種問題集合)>> ->I'm all clear. ($DLStateFloat4);//問過一個選項在出現 <<#GoTo(@問完決定幫阿緯說服賴潔問題)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>>

title: 操作組勝利慶功宴
body: //阿凡做到這裏要討論一下大家的慶祝姿勢(看能不能都跟失敗雙贏路線同動畫) //陳家騏正站靠桌抱胸，賴羽嬪柯義偉站著討論事情 Paxton Luo: Jason! Right in time!;<>;<> Paxton Luo: The new farm manager is about to make his inauguration speech.;<> Kaylee Chen: Justin is not the manager yet, okay?;<>;<> Paxton Luo: He practically is!;<> Jesse Lai: Quiet. Let him speak.;<> <>;<> <> <<#Wait(1)>> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Hi, everyone. Justin Wu: I trust you all have heard that we have finally gained the authority to shut down this farm.;<> Justin Wu: You know what it insinuates... Justin Wu: Now, finally... Justin Wu: We can relieve people from this cycle of torture. Justin Wu: By "people", I mean the juicers, as well as us.;<> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: We thought this monstrosity called Silencio would sustain itself into eternity. Justin Wu: Thanks to our combined effort, we can finally end it!;<> Justin Wu: If not for you, it will continue to exist, and harm countless more people. Justin Wu: Please allow me to say thank you. Justin Wu: Thank you for saving this tiny corner of the world.;<> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: However, we must see reality as it is.;<> Justin Wu: What we achieved is merely a beginning. Justin Wu: There will be many, many issues that require our collected effort to resolve.;<> Justin Wu: Shutting down this farm will be a slow and painful process. Justin Wu: Regardless, it has to be done. Justin Wu: Working as a team, I have no doubt we can do it.;<> <> Paxton Luo: YEAH!;<>;<>;<> Wyatt Ke: We can do it!;<>;<> Justin Wu: Please lend me your support as ever.;<>;<>;<> Justin Wu: But for today, let's take a break. We have earned it.;<> Jesse Lai: Woo-hoo! That's some speech.;<>;<> Kaylee Chen: Almost like out of the movies! Penelope Lai: It's great!;<> Justin Wu: Jason, have you got a minute? Justin Wu: There's something I want to discuss in private.;<>;<> Kaylee Chen: Oh? Salary raise?;<> Justin Wu: No! Wyatt Ke: Haha!;<> Paxton Luo: If anyone deserves a raise, it's me!;<>;<>;<> Jesse Lai: Get out. Penelope Lai: Haha!;<>;<> Justin Wu: Let's go outside, Jason.;<>;<> <>;<> <<#End>>

title: 遇到阿緯
body:
Wayne Zheng: Jason.
Thought: He's calming down a little.
Thought: Although the spirit seems to be even lower...;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭)>>
<<Set($DLStateFloat,1)>>;//這要放在@在篩選走廊與阿緯尷尬相見之上，以免無限循環
<<@在篩選走廊與阿緯尷尬相見>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->How are you feeling?
    Wayne Zheng: ...
    Wayne Zheng: Not good.
    Wayne Zheng: Can I help you?;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭返回)>>
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->No, just checking on you.
    ->What do you want to do next?
        Wayne Zheng: ...
    <<#EndSimple>>
->I'm sorry.
    Wayne Zheng: What are you sorry for?;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭返回)>>
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->I'm sorry that Justin won the upper hand.
        Wayne Zheng: The upper hand?
        Wayne Zheng: This is never about win or lose.
        Wayne Zheng: We are talking about the life, of hundreds of thousands of people.
        Wayne Zheng: Justin didn't win, or gain an upper hand. He deprived many people of their last chance.
    ->I have no idea.
        Wayne Zheng: ...
    ->Sorry for seeing you lose control.
        Wayne Zheng: The apology is all mine. I should have got a grip.
        Wayne Zheng: However, I don't regret anything I said.
        Wayne Zheng: ...
    <<#EndSimple>>
->Wayne. ($DLStateFloat);//選過消失
    <<Set($DLStateFloat,-1)>>
    Wayne Zheng: ...
    Wayne Zheng: Bye.
    <<#Simple(3)>>
    ->Bye.
        //
    ->Wait.
        <<#GoTo(@在篩選走廊與阿緯尷尬相見)>>
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    <<PlayUntil(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭返回)>>
    <<Set($DLState,阿緯低落離開辦公室走廊)>>
    <<#End>>
<<#EndSimple>>
Wayne Zheng: You know the consequences of what Justin is doing, don't you?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Depends on your perspective.
    Wayne Zheng: You make it sound as if it's up to debate.
    Wayne Zheng: If you think about it with some empathy, this is absolutely terrible.
->I know.
    Wayne Zheng: And you choose to do nothing about it.
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->It's the necessary evil.
        Wayne Zheng: You know it's evil and you still stand by it?
        <<#Simple(8)>>
        ->Otherwise the damage would be even greater.
        ->You can't just say no to everything that comes with its innate part of evil.
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
    ->I believe this is the solution with the least damage.
        Wayne Zheng: The least damage, by involving the most people...?
        <<#Simple(8)>>
        ->The bottom line is that the Cellar won't kill anyone anymore.
        ->What else? Giving them a job with a high mortality risk?
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
->I don't think shutting down the farm is that bad an idea.
<<#EndSimple>>

Wayne Zheng: You're brainwashed by Ops, like in a day.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭)>>
Wayne Zheng: I've had enough.
Thought: He's getting angry again...
Wayne Zheng: Listen. I have a solution to keep the Cellar running without killing people.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭返回)>>
<<Set($DLStateFloat,0)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Why didn't you say so?
    Wayne Zheng: I did, many times. Justin doesn't take my proposal seriously.
->That's impossible.;<<Set($DLStateFloat,1)>>
    Wayne Zheng: Because I don't have the technological know-how? Is that what you're gonna say?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->I don't believe it. Or else you'd have brought it up a long time ago.
        Wayne Zheng: I did, many times. Justin doesn't take my proposal seriously.
    ->Yes, probably because it's impractical.
        Wayne Zheng: I see you're really part of Ops now.
    <<#EndSimple>>
->What is it?
    Wayne Zheng: The specifics don't matter anymore.
    Wayne Zheng: I proposed it to Justin many times. He doesn't take it seriously.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Wayne Zheng: Never mind. I'm giving up.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站看內)>>
Wayne Zheng: I should have taken his approach. To hell with cooperation.
Wayne Zheng: Just wrestle for control at all costs. Makes things a lot easier.
Wayne Zheng: Now I'm seriously thinking about how to ruin this deal between Justin and his director...;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站看內返回)>>
<<#Simple(10)>>
->Don't do it.
    Wayne Zheng: Tell that to Justin.
->You will make the situation worse.
    Wayne Zheng: Probably, but at least I can save this farm.
    Wayne Zheng: As long as the farm is there, I have a second chance.
->I know how you feel.
    Wayne Zheng: You know? Don't kid yourself.
    //if剛剛說不可能
    <<#If($DLStateFloat=1)>>
        Wayne Zheng: You were rejecting my plan without bothering to find out what it is.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站握拳)>>
        Wayne Zheng: How are you different from Justin, or the rest of them Ops snobs?
        <<#Simple(9999)>>
        ->You have no right to call me a snob.
            Wayne Zheng: You have no right to call my plan impractical.
            Wayne Zheng: For fuck's sake, you don't even know what it is!
        ->I just don't think you have a practical mind.
            Wayne Zheng: Yeah, you people keep saying that.
            Wayne Zheng: No one is practical, other than your esteemed selves.
        <<#EndSimple>>
        Wayne Zheng: I'm so tired of it.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站握拳返回)>>
        <<#GoTo(@跳過巧遇阿緯的好路線有提到靜一靜)>>
    <<#EndIf>>
    Wayne Zheng: You're taking Justin's side, huh?;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站抬雙手)>>
    Wayne Zheng: How are you different from the rest of them Ops snobs?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->You have no right to call me a snob.
        Wayne Zheng: No right?;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站抬雙手返回)>>
        Wayne Zheng: What right do you have to look down your nose at us?
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->The Ops team never saw you that way.
        ->That is a separate matter.
        ->You keep making judgments without bothering to learn the facts.
        <<#EndSimple>>
        Wayne Zheng: ...;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭)>>
        Wayne Zheng: Whatever.
        Wayne Zheng: I'm so tired of it.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭返回)>>
        <<#GoTo(@跳過巧遇阿緯的好路線有提到靜一靜)>>
    ->The difference is that I care about your opinions.
        Wayne Zheng: You care?;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站抬雙手返回)>>
        Wayne Zheng: Your boss doesn't.
        <<#GoTo(@阿緯不爽吳俊恩說跟他溝通有成本不值得)>>
    ->I haven't given up on communications. That's something, isn't it?
        Wayne Zheng: Communications, huh.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站抬雙手返回)>>
        <<@阿緯不爽吳俊恩說跟他溝通有成本不值得>>
        Wayne Zheng: As Justin said, communications come with a cost.
        Wayne Zheng: He doesn't think I'm worth the cost.
        <<#Simple(8)>>
        ->Some things can't be helped.
            Wayne Zheng: Like what's in front of us.
            Wayne Zheng: I'm so tired of it.
            <<#GoTo(@跳過巧遇阿緯的好路線有提到靜一靜)>>
        ->I don't agree with him.
            Wayne Zheng: Then why are you on his side?
            <<#Simple(99999)>>
            ->Because I believe he is better informed.
                Wayne Zheng: Good. Keep heeding his better-informed opinions.
                <<#GoTo(@跳過巧遇阿緯的好路線有提到靜一靜)>>
            ->I'm on the side of Ops, not Justin.
                Wayne Zheng: ...
                Wayne Zheng: Does that mean you don't think the farm should be shut down?
                <<#Simple(99999)>>
                ->Yeah.
                ->I think there are more possibilities to explore.
                    <<Set($SelectionGoRest,0)>>
                    Wayne Zheng: But Justin wouldn't agree...
                    Wayne Zheng: ...;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭)>>
                    Wayne Zheng: I need some time to calm down.
                    Wayne Zheng: And...
                    Wayne Zheng: Sorry, Jason, for unloading my frustration on you.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭返回)>>
                    <<#Simple(6)>>
                    ->It's alright.
                        Wayne Zheng: Thank you.
                    ->...
                    <<#EndSimple>>
                    Wayne Zheng: See you later, then...
                    <<#Simple(6)>>
                    ->Bye.
                    ->...
                    <<#EndSimple>>
                    <<Set($DLState,阿緯低落離開辦公室走廊)>>
                    Thought: He's totally beaten up.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外)>>
                    Thought: Hopefully he will eventually accept farm closure as a fact.
                    Frances Cheng(c): He's taking it very personally...
                    <<#End>>
                ->No, Silencio should still be shut down.
                    Wayne Zheng: I figured...
                    <<#GoTo(@巧遇阿緯他失落離開環節)>>
                <<#EndSimple>>
            ->These are two separate things.
                Wayne Zheng: They seem very much related to me.
                Wayne Zheng: Alright, I'm so tired of it.
                <<#GoTo(@跳過巧遇阿緯的好路線有提到靜一靜)>>
            <<#EndSimple>>
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
<<@跳過巧遇阿緯的好路線有提到靜一靜>>
//IF剛剛說正緯不理智
<<#If($MCJudgeAWei=1)>>
    Wayne Zheng: Like you said in the lobby, I'm not thinking straight.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭)>>
    Wayne Zheng: All this time, I've tried my best to keep a rational mind. How did that work?
    Wayne Zheng: Now Justin is burning this farm down.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭返回)>>
<<#EndIf>>
//IF剛剛說正緯在做蠢事
<<#If($MCJudgeAWei=2)>>
    Wayne Zheng: Didn't you tell me not to make a fool of myself?;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭)>>
    Wayne Zheng: That's what I've been trying not to do, making a fool of myself.
    Wayne Zheng: The result? Justin is now mopping the floor with me.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭返回)>>
<<#EndIf>>

Wayne Zheng: Since that didn't work, I'm gonna try the opposite.
Wayne Zheng: Be irrational. Be selfish. Be a jerk.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Please, Wayne, think again.
->You should give Justin another chance.
    Wayne Zheng: Enough, Jason. I'm done with him.
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Fair enough.
        //
    ->Would you consider the possibility?
        Wayne Zheng: Maybe in the future. Not now.
    <<#EndSimple>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Wayne Zheng: I can see the truth.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭)>>
Wayne Zheng: No matter what I do, Silencio can't be saved.
Wayne Zheng: Fucking great.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站低頭返回)>>
<<@巧遇阿緯他失落離開環節>>
Wayne Zheng: Bye.
<<#Simple(6)>>
->Bye.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
<<Set($DLState,阿緯低落離開辦公室走廊)>>
Thought: He's totally beaten up.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看內)>>
Thought: But really, he can't do a thing about it.
<<#End>>

title: 球球指引路
body: Quentin Qiu: Hm?;<> Quentin Qiu: Oh, it's you.;<> Quentin Qiu: What are you doing here? <> <<@球球頂樓初始問答>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What are you doing here? ($DLStateFloat) <> Quentin Qiu: Answer my question first! <<#GoTo(@球球頂樓初始問答)>> ->Are you feeling bad? Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: Do you even need to ask...? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Because of me? ->What for? Quentin Qiu: Hey! ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Answer my question first! <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm looking for Wayne. <<#GoTo(@問球球找鄭緯去哪找)>> ->I want to find a solution for the situation at hand. Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: Don't tell me you want to try Wayne again? Quentin Qiu: What's the point...? Quentin Qiu: Plus... <<#GoTo(@問球球找鄭緯去哪找2)>> ->I'm just dropping by. Quentin Qiu: As if I'd buy that... Quentin Qiu: You're looking for Wayne, aren't you? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->OK, you're right. <<#GoTo(@問球球找鄭緯去哪找2)>> ->I'm really not. Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: Huh. Never mind. Quentin Qiu: Forget about it. <<#Simple(12)>> ->Yeah. // ->Is he here? There's something I need to tell him. Quentin Qiu: Ha... Quentin Qiu: But... <<#GoTo(@問球球找鄭緯去哪找2)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Bye. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Bye. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I'm looking for Wayne. <<@問球球找鄭緯去哪找>> Quentin Qiu: I knew it... <<@問球球找鄭緯去哪找2>> Quentin Qiu: I don't think he wants to talk, to anyone. <<#Simple(8)>> ->It's about the future of this farm. ->I know, but still. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Are you sure? Quentin Qiu: Honestly, I think he really needs some alone time. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I have to see him. Please. ->I'll go away if he doesn't want to see me. Promise. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: You know what? I kinda want to have a talk with him, too...;<> Quentin Qiu: I know I'm not in the right mood, though. Quentin Qiu: Forcing a conversation right now could make things even worse. Quentin Qiu: Thank you for solving my problem, haha... Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: He's outside.;<> Quentin Qiu: Go find him if you really think it's a good idea. Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: And Jayster. <<#Simple(6)>> ->What? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: If you can't make the situation better, don't make it any worse. Please. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I won't. Quentin Qiu: Yeah. ->I can't promise. Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: And I can't stop you, can I? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You can't. ->Now I'm not sure anymore. <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: ...Think about it, then. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Bye. <> <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>>

title: 說服阿緯
body:
//這時候主角在鄭緯左後方 by凡
Wayne Zheng: Jason?;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_頂樓左看)>>
Wayne Zheng: You managed to find me.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Took me a lot of finding.
    Wayne Zheng: Because I don't want to be found.
    Wayne Zheng: Although...
->Am I interrupting?
    Wayne Zheng: Sort of, haha...
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Shall I leave you be?
        Wayne Zheng: ...
        Wayne Zheng: *Sigh* Come over here.
    ->I'll be going, then.
        Wayne Zheng: Haha, no need for that...
    <<#EndSimple>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Wayne Zheng: Now that you've found me, let's talk.
Wayne Zheng: Come over here.;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_頂樓左看返回)>>
<<#End>>

title: 說服賴潔完成程芳對話
body:
//說服完賴潔上車離開前。開始。(前個任務:離開操作組)
//說服完采欣回到走廊(前個任務:離開茶水間)



Wayne Zheng(c): Hello? Jason?
<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭)>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Yeah.
->What's up?
<<#EndSimple>>
//if成功
<<#If($ConvincedLaiJie=0)>>
    Wayne Zheng(c): Well done.
    Wayne Zheng(c): I just received a load of data from Jesse. Your effort paid off.
    Wayne Zheng(c): So, um...
    Wayne Zheng(c): Well done!
    Wayne Zheng(c): Now we have everything we need to save this farm.
    Wayne Zheng(c): That's it. I've got the report to write. Talk to you later.
    <<#Simple(6)>>
    ->Later.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndIf>>
//if失敗
<<#If($ConvincedLaiJie=-1)>>
    <<Set($DLStateFloat,-1)>>
    <<Set($DLStateFloat2,1)>>
    Wayne Zheng(c): ...
    Wayne Zheng(c): Jesse sent me a very carefully phrased letter...
    Wayne Zheng(c): Basically saying she's not helping us.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭返回)>>
    Wayne Zheng(c): Does that mean you tried and failed?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Sorry.;<<Set($DLStateFloat,1)>>
        Wayne Zheng(c): Don't be. I don't blame you.
    ->Yeah, I failed.
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Wayne Zheng(c): ...
    Wayne Zheng(c): Can I ask why she turned my request down?;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭)>>
    <<@解答阿緯為啥賴潔拒絕合作>>
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Didn't she mention it in the letter? ($DLStateFloat2);//選過消失
        <<Set($DLStateFloat2,-1)>>
        Wayne Zheng(c): No, not a word of it.
        Wayne Zheng(c): That's why I'm calling you...
        <<#GoTo(@解答阿緯為啥賴潔拒絕合作)>>
    ->I think she didn't like my attitude.
    ->Probably because I had a fight with someone in her office.
        Wayne Zheng(c): I see...
        Wayne Zheng(c): Alright. Thank you all the same.
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Wayne Zheng(c): Consider your task done. Now feel free to do what you need to.
    Wayne Zheng(c): See you later.
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->See you later.
        //
    ->Sorry, Wayne.
        Wayne Zheng(c): It's alright.
        //if剛剛有抱歉
        <<#If($DLStateFloat=1)>>
            Wayne Zheng(c): I told you, didn't I?
        <<#EndIf>>
        Wayne Zheng(c): I don't blame you.
        Wayne Zheng(c): Don't dwell on it.
        Wayne Zheng(c): See you later.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndIf>>
<<CloseCommunicator()>>
Thought: He hung up.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭返回)>>

Frances Cheng(c): I'm back!
Frances Cheng(c): J? How are you doing? Any update while I was gone?;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Nothing.
    Frances Cheng(c): Good to know.
->I had some stressful talks.
    Frances Cheng(c): Wow...
    Frances Cheng(c): Will any of it affect our operation?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Nope.
        Frances Cheng(c): Good.
    ->You didn't ask about my feelings.
        Frances Cheng(c): Haha, don't be sad. You're the good boy.
    <<#EndSimple>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Frances Cheng(c): 23 asked me to do some preparation.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->What preparation?
    Frances Cheng(c): Turning on a few devices.
->Are they back yet?
    Frances Cheng(c): No, but should be soon enough.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Frances Cheng(c): Now we have to wait for contract signing.
Frances Cheng(c): That's it. Keep in touch!
<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭返回)>>
<<#End>>

title: 說服采欣完成程芳對話
body: //說服完賴潔上車離開前。開始。(前個任務:離開操作組) //說服完采欣回到走廊(前個任務:離開茶水間) Frances Cheng(c): J? How are you doing? Any update while I was gone? <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Nothing. Frances Cheng(c): Good. ->I had some stressful talks. Frances Cheng(c): Wow... Frances Cheng(c): Will any of it affect our operation? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nope. Frances Cheng(c): Good. ->You didn't ask about my feelings. Frances Cheng(c): Haha, don't be sad. You're the good boy. <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng(c): I left for a while. 23 asked me to do some preparation. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What preparation? Frances Cheng(c): Turning on a few devices. ->Are they back yet? Frances Cheng(c): Not any time soon, it seems. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng(c): Hold on. Justin Wu from Ops is calling you. <> Frances Cheng(c): Let me patch him in. Frances Cheng(c): ... Justin Wu(c): Jason? How is it going? <> <> <> <<#If($ConvincedTsaiShin=0)>> <> <<#EndIf>> <<#If($ConvincedTsaiShin=-1)>> <> <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Cathy agreed to help. ($DLStateFloat);//只出現上兩個或下兩個 ->It's done. ($DLStateFloat);//只出現上兩個或下兩個 Justin Wu(c): Great. I'll wait for her data then. Justin Wu(c): Well done. You have helped a lot. Justin Wu(c): Is there anything that I should know? I'll work on the report if not.;<> <<@俊恩確認采欣說服後問題>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nothing. Just focus on the report. Justin Wu(c): I will. ->I told her that you will take good care of the Screening staff. ($DLStateFloat4) <> Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): To the best of my ability, yes. Justin Wu(c): ...Did you tell her there will be people losing their jobs? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No need. She's well aware of that. ->I probably forgot to. ->I don't remember if I did. ->I think I did. <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): ...Okay. Justin Wu(c): I will contact her separately, about my plan for the Screening staff. Justin Wu(c): Thank you for letting me know. Justin Wu(c): Anything else? <<#GoTo(@俊恩確認采欣說服後問題)>> ->Are you aware that you're using Cathy? ($TsaiShinFeltUsed);//有選才會出現 <> Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): What do you mean? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You forced a request that she couldn't possibly refuse upon her. Justin Wu(c): I asked for her consent. Justin Wu(c): She could have said no if she wanted to. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Still, you put her in a very difficult position. Justin Wu(c): She has to act against the collective will of her colleagues, yes. But— <<#Simple(6)>> ->She has to betray her colleagues. Justin Wu(c): ...Let me remind you again, that depends on your perspective. <<#Simple(6)>> ->From her perspective, this is betrayal. Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): OK. I accept that. Justin Wu(c): Still... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): She made her own decision. I shall not be responsible for it. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I didn't say you're responsible. ->You're right. // ->This is not about who's responsible. Justin Wu(c): It's not...? <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): ...You lost me then. Justin Wu(c): I don't know how it could have been done in a better way. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Tell Cathy that you're sorry, for putting her in a difficult position. Justin Wu(c): ...I don't understand. How does that matter? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->It will make her feel better. Justin Wu(c): ... ->So that she knows you care about her feelings, that you're not using her. Justin Wu(c): I never wanted to use— Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): I— <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): I see. Justin Wu(c): Thank you for pointing it out. I haven't realized. Justin Wu(c): Empathy is not my strong suit... Justin Wu(c): I will contact her. That much I promise. Thought: He sounds a bit disappointed in himself. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Okay, you're right. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): Jason, I have tried my utmost best to handle the situation. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I know. Justin Wu(c): Yeah. ->Maybe you could do more. Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): ...Maybe. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->It's nothing. Forget about it. Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): OK, I see. <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): Anything else I should know? <<#GoTo(@俊恩確認采欣說服後問題)>> <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): Once I receive Cathy's data, I need time to analyze it. Justin Wu(c): Oh, I've made an appointment with the CEO. We are meeting up in the entrance lobby. Justin Wu(c): There's quite some time before the meeting, but you can wait for me there. Justin Wu(c): That's it for now. Justin Wu(c): See you later. ->Sorry, I failed. ($DLStateFloat2);//只出現上兩個或下兩個 ->Cathy refused to help. ($DLStateFloat2);//只出現上兩個或下兩個 Justin Wu(c): ...;<> Justin Wu(c): ... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Justin? ->I'm sorry. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): ... Justin Wu(c): Don't feel sorry. I'm not blaming you. Justin Wu(c): I was searching for an alternative solution. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Have you got any new ideas? Justin Wu(c): No. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu(c): I wasn't expecting Cathy to say no. Justin Wu(c): Now I must change the plan asap. Thought: ... Justin Wu(c): Oh, I've made an appointment with the CEO. We are meeting up in the entrance lobby. Justin Wu(c): There's quite some time before the meeting, but you can wait for me there. Justin Wu(c): That's it for now. Justin Wu(c): See you later. <<#EndSimple>> Thought: He hung up.;<> //if開導吳俊恩采欣心情 <<#If($DLStateFloat3=0)>> Frances Cheng(c): Wow, J... Frances Cheng(c): Since when did you become a master therapist? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Am I a master therapist? Frances Cheng(c): You are. What you said to Justin... ->I live and learn. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng(c): ... Frances Cheng(c): I see that you have changed a lot over the course of just a few days. Frances Cheng(c): Oh! <<#EndIf>> Frances Cheng(c): He did say there's some time before his meeting with Yan?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Sounds like so. Frances Cheng(c): I see... ->What's up? Frances Cheng(c): Nothing. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng(c): I'm checking your schedule, in case there's any conflict with contract signing. Frances Cheng(c): So far everything looks fine. No worries. Frances Cheng(c): Now you have some free time. Frances Cheng(c): If there's nothing else to do, go wait for Yan at the 1F lobby. <> <<#End>>

title: 跟隆生確認怎麼簽約
body:
//龍昇做仔椅子上

//這裡要描寫隆生有點無情，他只關心任務，工廠怎麼樣了她不太在乎。(前面在辦公室沒有人)
//可以主動問隆生錯過慶功宴，或是問說知不知道董事會對寂靜的處置。

Lawrence Chang: Hey, Jason.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐二低頭轉右看)>>;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Hey.
    //
->How's it going?
    Lawrence Chang: I don't know, but Ms. Cheng told me Nin will be back soon.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
//if這裡可以加操作組獨有的對話，主要先問等等要簽約的人掌怎樣

//


Nin Situ(c): We...;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐二右看返回)>>
23(c): We're back!;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭返回)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->23?
    23(c): Yes it's me!
->Took you guys a while.
    23(c): I tried. Things don't always happen to the plan.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
23(c): Looks like F has been handling everything well. I knew she could do it!
Frances Cheng(c): Thank you. I tried, too, haha...
//if有走葉慈恩路線
<<#If($KnowTimeGapUseComputer=0||$KnowTimeGapUseComputer=-3)>>
    23(c): Oh? You found one more contract candidate?
    23(c): Eh...
    //if找失敗
    <<#If($KnowTimeGapUseComputer=-3)>>
        23(c): Then you cancelled it?
        Frances Cheng(c): Somebody noticed us. Just in case, I undid every change I made...
        23(c): I see. It's a shame.
        23(c): Still, that was some superb job.
        23(c): Nicely done, F!
    <<#EndIf>>
    //if成功
    <<#If($KnowTimeGapUseComputer=0)>>
        Frances Cheng(c): Did I do it right? I think I did it clean enough...
        23(c): Don't worry!
        23(c): Well done!
        23(c): Let me see. The extra candidate you found is...
        23(c): Simone Lo?
        Frances Cheng(c): Uh-huh.
        23(c): We have one extra shot. Thanks, F!
    <<#EndIf>>
    Frances Cheng(c): Haha...
<<#EndIf>>

Lawrence Chang: Hey! Jason and I deserve a pat on the back, too!;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐二右看)>>;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭)>>
<<#Simple(6)>>
->Yeah.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
23(c): ...
23(c): Now, let's talk about what's next.
Lawrence Chang: Oi!! Did you hear me?;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐二右看返回)>>
23(c): Hee hee.

Nin Situ(c): OK, team. We have critical tasks upcoming.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐二低頭)>>
Nin Situ(c): Get the information we need out of these parents.
23(c): To the contract rooms!;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約正坐二低頭返回)>>
<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭返回)>>
<<#End>>

title: 車上會合
body: Nin Situ: 23, drive. //車子開始晃動離開。 <<#Simple(6)>> ->Hold on. Where are we going now? Nin Situ: An abandoned construction site in Evacuation Zone 6. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Nin Situ: I transported Frances to another safehouse. Nin Situ: Otherwise we can't spare any people to protect her. All hands on deck. //if有跟顏聖合透漏芳是女友 <<#If($CurseBossHurtFrances=0)>> 23(c): You told Yan that F is your girlfriend.;<> 23(c): Now your apartment isn't safe anymore. Nin Situ: We do have precautions, but given the situation, it's not enough. Nin Situ: Don't make similar mistakes again, Jason. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I won't. Nin Situ: Good.;<>;<> ->It's Yan's fault. Nin Situ: Ultimately it's his fault.;<> Nin Situ: But it's no excuse for you to let slip like that.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Hey, wait... <<#EndIf>> Lawrence Chang: So, what's the situation now, Nin?;<> Nin Situ: The guy who attacked you is from a gang in the Shithole. White Scorpion. Nin Situ: Following that lead, 23 found out their involvement in our human trafficking case.;<> Nin Situ: Helper chimed in. He pinpointed the location of the children. Nin Situ: He also found out that today is Vision's pickup date. Lawrence Chang: Pickup date?;<> Yaron Kun(c): Vision's gonna pick the children up from the gang.;<>;<> Yaron Kun(c): Can't find a better opportunity to catch them red-handed, eh? Nin Situ: We need to be there before Vision does, and extract the children.;<> Yaron Kun(c): Then catch Vision's people when they finally show up. Nin Situ: No. We evacuate once the children are secured. Can't afford to stay.;<>;<> Yaron Kun(c): Do I need to remind you this opportunity won't come twice? Nin Situ: We don't know how much a threat Vision's pickup team is. Yaron Kun(c): What threat? We have you. //這裡做一個反諷，之後反而是司徒寧腦衝 Nin Situ: We already know they have hired mercenaries.;<> Nin Situ: We could be vastly outnumbered. I'm not bulletproof. Nin Situ: Do you really want to take the risk? Yaron Kun(c): However... Nin Situ: Do you have a death wish?;<> Yaron Kun(c): ... Yaron Kun(c): ...Okay, okay. You're the boss. //if情報姊所 <<#If($GetIntel>0)>> //if是透過駱思敏 <<#If($GetIntel=1)>> 23(c): I ran a search on the name Simone Lo gave us, the one who took her daughter.;<>;<> 23(c): Following that person, I located their transaction venue and checked nearby surveillance camera footage.;<> 23(c): There's a peculiar van nearby, presumably keeping watch for the transaction. 23(c): Preliminary search suggests it's owned by Chameleon and his gang.;<> Nin Situ: Preliminary search based on what?;<> 23(c): Based on one of the passengers coming out for a cigarette break. 23(c): Jeremy Chen. Mercenary, wanted fugitive. 23(c): Plus, the make and model of the van says it all. Nin Situ: The model is?;<> <<#EndIf>> //if士透過陳聲紋 <<#If($GetIntel=2)>> 23(c): The van we learned about in the contract room. 23(c): Remember? Shawn Chen mentioned it.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Of course. 23(c): Good. ->Mentioned what? 23(c): He said he saw a strange van at the venue of transaction, remember? <<#EndSimple>> 23(c): I checked the log footage of all surveillance cameras near the venue, and identified the vehicle.;<> <<#EndIf>> 23(c): RX84.
