Lightweight armored vehicle. Nin Situ: Military grade?;<> 23(c): Yes, commonly used by foreign military organizations. 23(c): It's forbidden in this country. Can't buy it anywhere, not legally.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Why's that? Nin Situ: It comes with four homing turrets. Lawrence Chang: No shit.;<>;<>;<> Nin Situ: It's most often used by dignitaries for convoys. Nin Situ: Not in our country, though.;<> 23(c): The government has a tight leash on homing weapons.;<>;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> 23(c): Simply put, you can't get any RX84 here, except for by smuggling. Nin Situ: Naturally, they are very rare. We can locate them with ease through the public surveillance network... Nin Situ: 23? 23(c): Located. Currently on Highway 9. 23(c): Current speed approximately 50 kmph. The traffic isn't ideal. Nin Situ: Highway 9...;<> Nin Situ: The area is a Tier 3 zone, if my memory serves. They don't have much priority in flops supply. 23(c): Correct. That means the traffic coordination system doesn't work very well. It should slow them down for a while. Nin Situ: Good.;<> Nin Situ: Can we tell how well they're armed? 23(c): Afraid not. The vehicle has reflective coating and signal jammer. Can't detect what's inside. 23(c): Can't say I'm surprised, otherwise the police would be after them already. Nin Situ: Right.;<> 23(c): Judging by the sagging of its suspension system, though...;<>;<> 23(c): There must be seven or eight mercenaries armed to the teeth, or one compact mecha suit. 23(c): Whichever case it is, it's beyond you. Yaron Kun(c): Beyond us? Between she and I, we can— 23(c): Take down a fleet of five vans?;<> Yaron Kun(c): What the...!?;<>;<> 23(c): There are five of them, all heading towards the same destination. Yaron Kun(c): The fuck? Mobilizing a small army just to pick up a few kids? Nin Situ: They do care a lot about these kids for sure.;<> Nin Situ: What's their ETA? 23(c): ETA in 2 hours, at least. Nin Situ: Good. We have time.;<> Nin Situ: We must move fast. Avoid contact at all costs. Nin Situ: Jason, Lawrence, you have done great to extract intel.;<> Nin Situ: That was significant help. Lawrence Chang: No biggie!;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Thank you. ->We just followed your plan. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <>;<> <<#EndIf>> Yaron Kun(c): Hey folks, I'm on site. Yaron Kun(c): First, some quick recon.;<> Nin Situ: Don't engage until we arrive.;<> Yaron Kun(c): Roger that. Thought: He cut off the comms. Nin Situ: Damn this guy...;<>;<> Lawrence Chang: He won't compromise the operation, will he? 23(c): He doesn't have a reason to.;<> 23(c): Or at least, I don't see any reason, for now... Nin Situ: Listen, Jason and Lawrence.;<> Nin Situ: There's one thing you should know. Nin Situ: Now I'm positive that our operation is already compromised. Nin Situ: The man earlier today is proof. Lawrence Chang: ...Are you suggesting there's a mole?;<> Nin Situ: Yesterday at the Flop Core, Vision clearly knew our plan. Lawrence Chang: Maybe they had a hunch. Knowing our plan? Impossible.;<> Nin Situ: They deployed mercenaries, Lawrence. No one does that on a hunch. Lawrence Chang: It's the Flop Core. They can't afford any risk! It's reasonable. Lawrence Chang: ...I don't get it. Why can't you just trust us, Nin?;<> 23(c): Lawrence... Lawrence Chang: Why are you so paranoid?;<> 23(c): Stop it... Lawrence Chang: We've been working together for so long.;<> 23(c): LAWRENCE!!! Lawrence Chang: ...;<> 23(c): Nin's not suspecting you. There is no mole.;<> 23(c): ...We just discovered a miniature bug in Frances's leg. Lawrence Chang: What!?;<> Thought: What!? 23(c): It must have been lodged there when Chameleon shot her. 23(c): That explains why her wound wasn't so bad. It's not a regular bullet. Lawrence Chang: But... Nin Situ: But you should have already examined her wound.;<> Nin Situ: She was in your charge. Lawrence Chang: No way. I couldn't possibly miss a bug... Nin Situ: The bug is there. It's a fact. Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Lawrence Chang: I didn't... Nin Situ: It's OK, Lawrence. I trust you. Nin Situ: You just overlooked.;<> Nin Situ: The point is we have identified the source of compromise and fixed it. Lawrence Chang: ... Nin Situ: Lawrence, sometimes for others to trust you, you must trust them first.;<> Lawrence Chang: ...;<> Nin Situ: If I didn't trust you, I wouldn't allow you on this operation. Nin Situ: And of course I wouldn't have you on this rescue mission. Lawrence Chang: ...Yeah. Lawrence Chang: Sorry, I was acting like a brat.;<> Nin Situ: You are young. //車子晃動 23(c): Everyone.;<> 23(c): We have arrived. Nin Situ: Let's go.;<>;<> <<#End>>

title: 大廳陪嚴勝和
body: //顏聖合翹腳看螢幕 Shane Yan: Jason Dai, is it?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes. ->We just met earlier today. <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: ...;<> <<#If($AgreedWiretap=0)>> Thought: Hey, I should activate the bug... Thought: Yuna Hsu told me to record every single conversation with Yan. <<#Simple(12)>> ->[Activate the bug];<> <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //IF篩選 <<#If($MCBelongSelectDepartment=1)>> Shane Yan: Did Wayne say when he will be here? <<#EndIf>> //IF操作 <<#If($MCBelongControlDepartment=1)>> Shane Yan: Did Justin say when he will be here? <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: Okay. ->I thought he must have told you? Shane Yan: No.;<> Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: I see he didn't tell you, either.;<> ->Why ask? Shane Yan: ...;<> Shane Yan: We have an appointment, and he leaves me waiting. Shane Yan: Isn't that a good enough reason for asking?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah. ->I don't know how much longer he needs. <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: ... <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: ... <<#Simple(6)>> ->May I know if the board accepted his report? Shane Yan: Let's talk about that when he's here, shall we? ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#Wait(4)>> //執行長繼續看自己的平板，尷尬幾秒。 <> //if有說成芳是女友 <<#If($CurseBossHurtFrances=0)>> Shane Yan: Say, the world is a small place, isn't it? Shane Yan: Here you are, Frances's boyfriend, working on the same farm. Shane Yan: I wonder if she gave you any help in the recruitment process?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She didn't. // ->None of your business. Shane Yan: Oh, I beg to differ. Shane Yan: As the CEO, farm supervisor possibly abusing power is within my legitimate concern. Shane Yan: ... ->Maybe? I don't know. Shane Yan: Is that so? I will need to look into it, then. Shane Yan: ... <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: Hahahahaha!;<> Shane Yan: Don't be so nervous. I'm kidding. Shane Yan: I know Frances too well. She will never do something like that. Shane Yan: She's something of a miracle in this industry, a senior executive with a righteous soul.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->She earned the position fair and square. Shane Yan: Of course she did.;<> Shane Yan: Although her personality must have made the process extra hard. <<#GoTo(@嚴勝和說成芳做這行會特別辛苦)>> ->Why's that? Shane Yan: I presume you mean the part about a righteous soul?;<> Shane Yan: Haha... <<@嚴勝和說成芳做這行會特別辛苦>> Shane Yan: I'm not proud of it, but... Shane Yan: Exploitation is an innate part of this industry.;<> Shane Yan: That much I must admit, even as a beneficiary of it. Shane Yan: Imagine this room, where everyone is equally soiled. No one feels anything wrong about having a bit of soot on their face.;<> Shane Yan: Then enters this person, sparkling clean... Shane Yan: Suddenly everyone starts to feel uncomfortable.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Is that a legit reason to kill her? Shane Yan: What? Shane Yan: Oh, haha...;<> Shane Yan: Of course not. Thought: I wanna bash this fucker's brains in.;<> Shane Yan: You accused me of the same thing earlier today.;<> Shane Yan: I see you have this misconception that I am involved in Frances's accident. How unfortunate. Thought: ...;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#EndIf>> Shane Yan: Oh. Shane Yan: I asked you about Ms. Yuna Hsu. Shane Yan: I'm a bit concerned about her investigation here at Silencio.;<> Shane Yan: As I said, there are rumors of her employing unorthodox measures during her work. Shane Yan: Being the big boss of all Vision farms, I wouldn't want the IIB to harass my employees. Thought: What's he getting at...? Shane Yan: I know how it feels. I've been a victim of her harassment myself. Haha. <<#Simple(8)>> ->She has no power over you. Shane Yan: No. The IIB is directly under the board. They have power over all branches, divisions, and positions. Shane Yan: They can interrogate me if they want to. ->Harassment? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: My secretary was once accused of corporate espionage. Yuna listed me as a possible accomplice and conducted investigations way beyond reasonable.;<> //if有答應竊聽 <<#If($AgreedWiretap=0)>> Thought: Guess what? She's doing the same thing now. Thought: I'm carrying a bug for her. <<#EndIf>> Shane Yan: For a while, I thought she's hell-bent on using me as the scapegoat. Shane Yan: Ah, memories from the bad old days. Shane Yan: You're quite the wicked devil. Won't you say so, Yuna?;<> Thought: ...!? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Who are you talking to? ->What? Is she here with us? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Shane Yan: Hahahahaha! Relax. I'm joking.;<> Shane Yan: No. It's just that I realized if she were still after me right now, she'd have you wear a bug. Shane Yan: And if she did that, I should send her my regards.;<> Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: Honestly.;<> Shane Yan: I appreciate her motivation. Her ambition. <> <<#End>>

title: 阿緯抵達
body: //啊委從畫面走過來 <> Wayne Zheng: Mr. Yan! Shane Yan: Hey, Wayne. Shane Yan: It sure took you a while. Wayne Zheng: I'm terribly sorry! Shane Yan: Ha, don't be!;<>;<> Shane Yan: I was having a pleasant conversation with Jason. Thought: ... Shane Yan: Now that you're here, I will make the big announcement.;<> Wayne Zheng: ...Please do. //if說服賴潔成功 <<#If($ConvincedLaiJie=0)>> Shane Yan: Congrats. Your report was well accepted. Shane Yan: We have done a preliminary survey. Over half directors are in agreement with keeping Silencio.;<> Shane Yan: Your report is the cornerstone of such agreement. Very well done. Shane Yan: It's especially impressive that you offered detailed data and analysis on the performance of the Cellar.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Thank you.;<> Shane Yan: I take it that you are working with someone within Ops?;<> Wayne Zheng: Yes, sir.;<> Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: Good.;<> Shane Yan: Now, I agree to make you the manager of this farm. Shane Yan: The approval and handover process will take about a month, but...;<> Shane Yan: During this period, you are the de facto leader of Silencio. Shane Yan: Congratulations, Mr. Zheng, the new manager.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Thank you, sir!;<> Shane Yan: Don't thank me. It's not an easy job.;<>;<> Shane Yan: To put it bluntly, you are facing a crappy start. Shane Yan: Silencio is still an outdated piece of junk. Your job is to figure out how to enable it to create more value for Vision.;<> Shane Yan: I wish you good luck. Wayne Zheng: You can count on me, sir!;<>;<> <> Shane Yan: Good.;<>;<> Shane Yan: Now I must report to the board. Our meeting is concluded.;<> Wayne Zheng: Sure. Shane Yan: Oh. Shane Yan: Jason is a good man. You'll want to keep him around.;<> Shane Yan: We just had some quality time. Haha! Wayne Zheng: Haha, I sure will!;<>;<> Shane Yan: I'm expecting good news from you. Wayne Zheng: I will deliver. Have a good day, Mr. Yan.;<> <> <<#End>> <<#EndIf>> //if說服賴潔失敗 <<#If($ConvincedLaiJie=-1)>> Shane Yan: I'm sorry to announce that your report has fallen short of expectations. Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Shane Yan: It's good, but not good enough.;<> Shane Yan: Especially, the part regarding the Cellar is too vague, not backed by any real data.;<>;<> Shane Yan: Assumptions without factual or professional backing... Shane Yan: They undermine the credibility of a report, as everyone knows. Wayne Zheng: ...Yes, sir.;<>;<>;<> Shane Yan: However, this is not exactly surprising. Wayne Zheng: Huh?;<>;<> Shane Yan: A report from the perspective of Screening alone can't be too comprehensive. Shane Yan: It's my fault to put too much expectation in it. Wayne Zheng: ...;<>;<> Shane Yan: Cheer up, Wayne.;<> Shane Yan: Your report was helpful to the board.;<> Wayne Zheng: About my promotion...;<>;<> Shane Yan: Let me finish, OK? Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: Sorry for interrupting. Shane Yan: It was helpful to the board, but not to me.;<> Wayne Zheng: ... Shane Yan: It convinced the board to keep Silencio, but failed to win us additional funding.;<> Shane Yan: The board doesn't believe that Silencio can be reshaped into a marketing asset of Vision Corp's.;<> Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: I tried to vouch for you, proving that you have what it takes to revitalize this farm.;<> Shane Yan: However, your report suggests your influence is confined to your own division.;<> Shane Yan: In other words, you're a great division lead, and that's it.;<> Shane Yan: They don't see any potential to reach any higher in you. Shane Yan: There, I have said it. Shane Yan: Now you may ask questions. Wayne Zheng: There's no need. I—;<>;<> Shane Yan: Ask, please. Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: ...Will you make me the farm manager?;<> Shane Yan: No.;<> Shane Yan: There will be no promotion. Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Shane Yan: Although you may keep your position as the division lead of Screening.;<> Shane Yan: You're doing your job well, then continue to do it well.;<> Wayne Zheng: ...Yes, sir.;<> Shane Yan: Good.;<> Shane Yan: Our meeting today is done. <> Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: Hey.;<>;<> Shane Yan: Cheer up. You've got what you wanted.;<> Shane Yan: Silencio is saved. It will continue to exist as the eyesore that it is.;<> Shane Yan: All thanks to you. Shane Yan: Goodbye.;<> Wayne Zheng: ... <<#Simple(6)>> ->Evening. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#End>> <<#EndIf>>

title: 篩選組勝利慶功宴
body:
Quentin Qiu: Hey! Jayster!;<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局右看)>>;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_好結局右看)>>
Quentin Qiu: Where have you been?;<<Play(曹泰銘,MBU_曹泰銘_斜坐右看)>>;<<Play(吳晉峰,MBU_吳晉峰_轉身右看)>>
Quentin Qiu: We've ordered pizza!
<<#Simple(6)>>
->Pizza? When?
    Cathy Hsieh: QQ called the pizzeria before the announcement!;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_好結局右看返回)>>
    Quentin Qiu: No need to wait! I knew it!;<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局右看返回)>>;<<Play(吳晉峰,MBU_吳晉峰_轉身右看返回)>>
    Quentin Qiu: Wayno can't possibly fail.
    Tyler Cao: Haha.
    Cathy Hsieh: That's not what you said!
    Jeff Wu: What did he say, then?
    Cathy Hsieh: He said a nice slice of pizza helps to save morale if Wayno screws it up.
    <<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局右看)>>
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->You'll have pizza either way.
        Quentin Qiu: Why not? It's pizza!    
    ->Well said! Hahaha!
        Quentin Qiu: The Jayster knows me!
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    <<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_好結局右看)>>
    <<Play(吳晉峰,MBU_吳晉峰_轉身右看)>>
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
<<Set($DLState,阿緯走來招呼大家注意他)>>
<<#Wait(1)>>
Wayne Zheng: Attention, everyone!
Wayne Zheng: I have an announcement to make!
<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站拿杯)>>
Tyler Cao: Insert fanfare!
Quentin Qiu: Here be drumroll!;<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局右看轉左)>>
Cathy Hsieh: Shut up and listen!;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_好結局右看返回)>>
<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局左看轉右)>>
Wayne Zheng: Ahem!
<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_好結局右看)>>
Wayne Zheng: What we have achieved is but one milestone, with many more to come.
Wayne Zheng: We have bought this farm more time.
Wayne Zheng: But fundamentally, it's still the same old farm, with many issues waiting to be addressed.
Wayne Zheng: Going forward, we will face a mountain of challenges.
Wayne Zheng: Do not pat yourself on the back yet, because the real work is just beginning.
Wayne Zheng: ...
Wayne Zheng: However.
Wayne Zheng: That. Is. For. Tomorrow!
Wayne Zheng: Enjoy yourselves today and celebrate! You have earned it!;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站拿杯舉杯)>>
Quentin Qiu: Yay!!!;<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局右看歡呼)>>
Jeff Wu: Well said, Wayne!;<<Play(鄭緯,MBU_鄭緯_側站拿杯舉杯返回)>>
Cathy Hsieh: Pizza!;<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局右看歡呼返回)>>;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_好結局右看舉杯)>>
Wayne Zheng: Jason.;<<Play(吳晉峰,MBU_吳晉峰_轉身右看返回)>>
Wayne Zheng: Come to my desk for a moment.;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_好結局右看舉杯返回)>>;<<Play(曹泰銘,MBU_曹泰銘_斜坐右看返回)>>
<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局右看返回)>>
<<PlayUntil(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_好結局右看返回)>>
<<#End>>

title: 徐媛收尾
body: Thought: ... Thought: How much longer do I have to wait...? Thought: What's taking Hsu so long... Yuna Hsu: What's taking Hsu so long?;<>;<> Thought: ...!? Yuna Hsu: That is what you're thinking. Am I right?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No. Yuna Hsu: I can't always be right, huh. ->Yep. Yuna Hsu: Of course. ->I was only halfway through that thought. Yuna Hsu: Ha, were you? Thought: Her smile is more like a formality. <<#EndSimple>> Yuna Hsu: Hand it over. //if沒答應 <<#If($AgreedWiretap=-1)>> Yuna Hsu: My bug. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I didn't agree to wear it, remember? ->Huh? What bug? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Yuna Hsu: In your sofa, between cushions. Thought: ...What?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why did you ask me if you're already planted a bug? Yuna Hsu: Wedged between cushions, I'm not expecting much out of it. ->When did you plant this? Yuna Hsu: When you refused to cooperate. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //if有答應 <<#If($AgreedWiretap=0)>> Yuna Hsu: The bug you're wearing. <> //if都有開好 <<#If($ActivateWiretapCount=2)>> Yuna Hsu: You did a great job. <<#EndIf>> //if只開一次 <<#If($ActivateWiretapCount=1)>> Yuna Hsu: Half-baked job.;<> Yuna Hsu: Didn't I ask you to record every single conversation with Yan? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I forgot. Yuna Hsu: ...;<> Yuna Hsu: Luckily, I didn't expect too much out of you to begin with. ->There are stuff that I wouldn't like you to hear. Yuna Hsu: Is that so?;<> Yuna Hsu: Unfortunately, I will hear it anyway. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //if都沒開 <<#If($ActivateWiretapCount=0)>> Yuna Hsu: Can you ever keep a promise, Dai? <<#Simple(8)>> ->What are you talking about? Yuna Hsu: You didn't activate the bug at all. ->You mean I didn't activate the bug? Yuna Hsu: What else could it be? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Yuna Hsu: Mind telling me why you didn't? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I tried, but it didn't work. ->I changed my mind. <<#EndSimple>> Yuna Hsu: ... Yuna Hsu: Good. <<#EndIf>> Yuna Hsu: Now, the other bug. Yuna Hsu: In your sofa, between cushions. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why did you ask me if you're already planted a bug? Yuna Hsu: Wedged between cushions, I'm not expecting much out of it. ->When did you plant this? Yuna Hsu: This morning. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#EndIf>> Yuna Hsu: Always have a failsafe. That's how I do everything. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Did you get what you need? Yuna Hsu: Yes, more than enough. ->What now? Yuna Hsu: Now?;<> Yuna Hsu: Now I'm proceeding to the next step. Yuna Hsu: But it doesn't involve you. Your part is done.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> //if早上跟執行長對話有牽扯徐媛 <<#If($PlaySillyBackfire=0)>> Yuna Hsu: You're not a very smart guy.;<> Yuna Hsu: He asked you about the Flop Core, and you played dumb? Yuna Hsu: Don't you know it could blow my cover?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->How could I know that he's aware of our meeting? ->Sorry, I thought he'd buy it. <<#EndSimple>> Yuna Hsu: My bad. There's no point blaming an amateur. <<#EndIf>> //if竊聽有完成+也有給情報 <<#If($ActivateWiretapCount=2&&$ToldYuanIntel=2)>> Yuna Hsu: I suppose you have no idea what's really happening...;<> Yuna Hsu: Still, you have done me a massive favor. Yuna Hsu: Now you have earned my respect, Dai.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Um, thank you? Yuna Hsu: You're welcome. ->I didn't have your respect before? Yuna Hsu: Well, you said it, not me. ->I want more than respect. Yuna Hsu: Be grateful for what you have. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Yuna Hsu: You will need this respect in the future. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What's that supposed to mean? Yuna Hsu: You will know, eventually. ->Thank you, then. Yuna Hsu: Good. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Yuna Hsu: We should part ways here. Yuna Hsu: We will not meet again. Once I leave this farm, there's no reason to come back.;<> Yuna Hsu: I have found everything I need to know. Yuna Hsu: The farm itself doesn't deserve my attention, now that I've proven it's unimportant in Vision's hierarchy of power. Yuna Hsu: Not just unimportant. It's nothing.;<> //if雙贏 <<#If($FabWinWin=0)>> Yuna Hsu: Still, color me surprised by what you did. Yuna Hsu: You managed to have Screening and Ops work together to uncover the truth. Yuna Hsu: Silencio, this old piece of junk, was slated for an ugly ending. Yuna Hsu: You, together with Wayne Zheng and Justin Wu, managed to get it a second wind.;<> Yuna Hsu: Not only that. The board even allocated additional funding. Yuna Hsu: Maybe this farm will live on. Yuna Hsu: For how long, though? I cannot say.;<> Yuna Hsu: Nonetheless, you have done an extraordinary job today. Yuna Hsu: You are an interesting man, Dai. Yuna Hsu: Maybe I'll invite you to my team someday. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Sorry, not interested. Yuna Hsu: Don't say it so soon.;<> Yuna Hsu: Given the right circumstances, you will develop an interest, one way or another. I will see to that. <> ->Maybe. Thought: A smile. Thought: One that feels creepy somehow... ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@跳過徐媛非雙贏的反應)>> <<#EndIf>> //if篩選組勝力 <<#If($ConvincedLaiJie=0&&$MCBelongSelectDepartment=1)>> Yuna Hsu: Silencio will undergo a slow and painful death. Yuna Hsu: No one could stop it. <<#Simple(8)>> ->The Screening team will prevent that. Yuna Hsu: They may postpone the death, not avoid it. ->Why's that? Yuna Hsu: Sorry, I can't be bothered to explain. Yuna Hsu: Just know that I speak the truth. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //if操作組勝力 <<#If($ConvincedTsaiShin=0&&$MCBelongControlDepartment=1)>> Yuna Hsu: Silencio will die a nasty death, leaving behind a huge loss to the company. Yuna Hsu: The loss will be recovered by exploiting juicers, one penny at a time. <<#Simple(8)>> ->The Ops team will prevent that. Yuna Hsu: Oh, they will try, no doubt.;<> Yuna Hsu: But they won't succeed. <> ->Why's that? Yuna Hsu: Sorry, I can't be bothered to explain. Yuna Hsu: Just know that I speak the truth. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> <<@跳過徐媛非雙贏的反應>> Yuna Hsu: Anyway, now I have found the key to the truth. Yuna Hsu: Keep pursuing your investigation, and you may eventually find it, too. <<#Simple(8)>> ->The key to the truth? Yuna Hsu: The one thing that the board of directors truly wants to keep secret. ->What is it? Yuna Hsu: Go find out for yourself. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Yuna Hsu: ...;<>;//徐媛看相別的地方 <<#End>>

title: 慶功宴結束程芳想法
俊恩抵達
body: <> Justin Wu: Mr. Yan. Shane Yan: Hey, Justin. Justin Wu: Sorry for the delay. The elevator ride took a while. Shane Yan: No worries. Shane Yan: I know how long the ride is, not counting waiting time.;<>;<> Shane Yan: It's quite alright, though. Jason has been keeping me company. Haha! Shane Yan: I was say—;<> Justin Wu: Let's cut to the chase, sir.;<> Justin Wu: ... Shane Yan: Has anyone complained about your conversational skills?;<>;<> Justin Wu: Yes. Is that relevant?;<> Shane Yan: No. Haha... Shane Yan: Good. Let's cut to the chase.;<> //IF操作勝利 <<#If($ConvincedTsaiShin=0)>> Shane Yan: Simply put. Shane Yan: Congrats. Your report was well accepted.;<> Shane Yan: We have done a preliminary survey. Over half directors are in agreement with keeping Silencio. Shane Yan: Your report is the cornerstone of such agreement. Very well done. Shane Yan: Your plan for post-closure juicer management is especially impressive.;<> Justin Wu: Thank you.;<> Shane Yan: I trust you have access to the Screening division's data?;<>;<> Justin Wu: Yes.;<> Shane Yan: How? They have no reason to cooperate with you.;<> Justin Wu: Jason helped me to convince one of their staff members.;<>;<> Shane Yan: Ah, Jason. Well done. Shane Yan: ...;<>;<> Shane Yan: Good.;<> Shane Yan: Now, I agree to make you the manager of this farm. Shane Yan: The approval and handover process will take about a month, but... Shane Yan: During this period, you are the de facto leader of Silencio. Shane Yan: Congratulations, Mr. Wu. Justin Wu: Thank you.;<> Shane Yan: Don't thank me. It's not an easy job.;<> Shane Yan: To put it bluntly, you are facing a crappy start. Shane Yan: Silencio is barely alive, but it's still a functioning farm.;<> Shane Yan: To finally shut it down, you will need to navigate a messy web of stakeholders. Shane Yan: I wish you good luck. Justin Wu: You can count on me, sir!;<>;<> Shane Yan: Good.;<> Shane Yan: Now I must report to the board. Our meeting is concluded.;<> <> Justin Wu: Sure.;<> Shane Yan: Oh.;<> Shane Yan: Jason is a good man. You'll want to keep him around. Shane Yan: We just had some quality time. Haha!;<> Justin Wu: I will. Shane Yan: I'm expecting good news from you.;<> Justin Wu: I will deliver. Have a good day, Mr. Yan. <<#End>> <<#EndIf>> //if說服采欣失敗 <<#If($ConvincedTsaiShin=-1)>> Shane Yan: I'm sorry to announce that your report has fallen short of expectations. Justin Wu: ...;<> Justin Wu: I see.;<> Shane Yan: It's good, but not good enough.;<> Shane Yan: Especially, the part regarding the handling of existing juicers is too vague, not backed by any real data.;<>;<> Shane Yan: Assumptions without factual or professional backing... Shane Yan: They undermine the credibility of a report, as everyone knows.;<> Justin Wu: ...Yes, sir.;<> Shane Yan: However, this is not exactly surprising. Justin Wu: ...?;<> Shane Yan: A report from the perspective of Ops alone can't be too comprehensive.;<> Shane Yan: It's my fault to put too much expectation in it. Justin Wu: ...;<> Shane Yan: Cheer up, Justin.;<> Shane Yan: Your report was helpful to the board. Justin Wu: About my promotion...;<> Shane Yan: Let me finish, OK?;<>;<> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Sorry for interrupting.;<>;<> Shane Yan: It was helpful to the board, but not to me.;<> Justin Wu: ... Shane Yan: It convinced the board to keep Silencio, but failed to win us additional funding.;<> Shane Yan: The board does not want to pay for the collateral damages as a result of full shutdown.;<> Shane Yan: They would rather see a constant but lesser loss by keeping the farm running as-is. Shane Yan: Your plan about handling existing juicers failed to convince them. Justin Wu: ...;<> Shane Yan: I tried to vouch for you, proving that you have what it takes to lead this farm. Shane Yan: However, your report suggests your influence is confined to your own division. Shane Yan: In other words, you're a great division lead, and that's it.;<> Shane Yan: They don't see any potential to reach any higher in you. Justin Wu: ...;<> Shane Yan: There, I have said it.;<> Shane Yan: Now you may ask questions. Justin Wu: ...Will you make me the farm manager? Shane Yan: No.;<> Shane Yan: There will be no promotion. Justin Wu: ... Shane Yan: However, you will be formally appointed as the lead of Ops.;<> Shane Yan: The previous lead will be removed in a peaceful way. Rest assured. Shane Yan: Listen, Justin.;<> Shane Yan: You're doing your job well, then continue to do it well. Justin Wu: ...Yes, sir. Shane Yan: Good.;<> Shane Yan: Our meeting today is done.;<> <> Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: Hey.;<>;<> Shane Yan: Cheer up. Silencio won't last forever.;<>;<> Shane Yan: Before the end of its days, though, it will keep causing trouble. Shane Yan: All thanks to you.;<> Shane Yan: Goodbye. Justin Wu: Goodbye, sir. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Bye. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>> <<#EndIf>>

title: 阿緯俊恩和解2
body: //技術派希望技術在萬象內部的地位繼續很高，紅盒實驗室的預算繼續很高。所以他們迫不急待要把寂靜廢氣然後讓深海取而代之。證明他們的能力。 //政治派希望把寂靜塑造成某種形象功廠，不賺錢但可以收容很多人，讓他們跟政府很好談。 //技術派騙局是他們會為了減少賠償壓榨勞工，這個政委會指出來源的合約就有這個漏洞。 //政治派騙局是他們只注重人數，所以會強硬施壓讓人數過量，然後待遇品質嚴重下降。 //如果使用新技術來達成高人數，在布要求賺錢的情況下有可能同時達成雙方利益。 //可是這種等級的合作很複雜，涉及很多未知數，代表今天之內不會有結果。 //所以只能要求延勝和去跟董事們談，對它的好處就是不用得罪任何一方 Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: I'll get straight to the point.;<> Justin Wu: Thanks to Jason, I realized your naïve plan could actually work.;<> Justin Wu: Provided that the board does provide additional funding like you hoped... Wayne Zheng: I'm glad you've come to your senses.;<> Justin Wu: Come to my senses? Justin Wu: What's that supposed to mean?;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: You didn't even want to consider my proposal. Now you admit it's practical. Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Let me finish.;<> Justin Wu: I said it could work. The chance isn't very good. Wayne Zheng: Huh? Justin Wu: You said you could bargain for funding to fix the Cellar?;<> Justin Wu: How much funding are you talking about? Got any idea?;<> Wayne Zheng: I do. It's about—;<> Justin Wu: Take that number and multiply it by three.;<> Wayne Zheng: What? Why...?;<> Justin Wu: Have you factored in the difficulty of performing fixes and upgrades without unracking all juicers?;<> Justin Wu: Even if we have unlimited funding, the process will take decades.;<> Justin Wu: This is why I refused to even consider your plan. Justin Wu: The whole thing is based on an unrealistic assumption. Justin Wu: I told you about it over and over again, but you refuse to listen.;<> Justin Wu: Fixing this farm up is practically impossible. Plain and simple. //if篩選 <<#If($MCBelongSelectDepartment=1)>> Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: Why have you come, then?;<> Justin Wu: Because you have a chance to do what I can't—securing funding from the board.;<> Wayne Zheng: So...? Justin Wu: So we have money to terminate all existing contracts, and shut down—;<> Wayne Zheng: Geez...;<> Justin Wu: And shut down the farm without compromising the juicers' benefits. Wayne Zheng: Justin... Justin Wu: Nobody will die in the Cellar anymore. Good ending.;<> Wayne Zheng: What the hell are you talking about?;<>;<> Justin Wu: A reasonable, practical, actionable plan. Wayne Zheng: The only reason why the board would provide any funding is that they want us to take in more people!;<> Wayne Zheng: Reduce the unemployment rate and gain political leverage. That is the whole point!;<> Wayne Zheng: You want to take the money, shut down the farm, and send hundreds of thousands of people out there without a job!? Wayne Zheng: Why would the board allow that?;<> Justin Wu: Silencio will cease to cause injury and death. Good PR story.;<>;<> Justin Wu: I believe it's good for Vision's public image. Wayne Zheng: Injury and death are never an issue, because—;<> Justin Wu: ...The hell did you say?;<> Wayne Zheng: Let me finish.;<> Wayne Zheng: Because Vision is more than capable of eliminating negative publicity. The board never saw it as a problem.;<> Justin Wu: So you agree to let them do that?;<> Wayne Zheng: No! Listen to me.;<> Justin Wu: I'm listening.;<> Justin Wu: Remind me if I'm wrong, Jason. I thought he said injury and death are not a problem?;<>;<> Justin Wu: Doesn't it mean he's OK with deaths?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah, I think that's what he means.;<>;//扣一成功率 Justin Wu: See?;<> Wayne Zheng: Not you too, Jason...;<> ->He means to say it's not a problem to Vision. Wayne Zheng: Right!;<>;<> Justin Wu: ... ->Don't ask me. I have no idea.;<>;//扣一成功率 Wayne Zheng: Don't tell me that you don't get it, Jason?;<>;<> <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: I mean Vision Corp doesn't care if people are injured or killed in the Cellar.;<> Wayne Zheng: As a result, the board won't pay a cent to fix a non-issue.;<> Wayne Zheng: They are evil. I agree with you. It's a fact. Wayne Zheng: What we're doing is to find a way and use their evil for the good.;<> Wayne Zheng: If played right, we will secure funding from the board. Justin Wu: ...Maybe.;<> Justin Wu: But your goal is just to provide jobs to more people, isn't it?;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Just to provide jobs? Justin Wu: Don't you think stopping the casualty is a higher priority?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Wayne's goal deserves high priority. Justin Wu: Mine deserves higher. <<@跟俊恩解釋阿偉目標也重要段落>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Even if so, you still should consider his plan. Justin Wu: ... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He recognizes your concerns, so you must consider his. Justin Wu: ...;<> Justin Wu: Okay.;<> Justin Wu: I apologize. I've been thinking inside my own box.;<> Justin Wu: I should have taken your perspective into consideration. Sorry.;<> Wayne Zheng: ...Thank you. ->Do you think your goal is the only priority? Justin Wu: What are you talking about?;<>;<> Justin Wu: I never said— Wayne Zheng: Okay, okay... Wayne Zheng: Jason just wants you to occasionally see things from my perspective for a change.;<>;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@篩選路線_檢查合作成功數值)>> ->Right. We're talking about life and death, after all. <> Wayne Zheng: Jason... <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <>;//扣一成功率 ->People can't survive without a job. Justin Wu: At least it's not immediate death. <<#GoTo(@跟俊恩解釋阿偉目標也重要段落)>> ->Right. We must save lives. Justin Wu: See? That's what I call reasonable thinking.;<> Wayne Zheng: *Sigh* <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <>;//扣一成功率 <<@篩選路線_檢查合作成功數值>> Wayne Zheng: Justin, I have been trying so hard to achieve what we both want. //if數值不夠 <<#If($DepartmentCoopSuccess<-1)>> Wayne Zheng: Now I see that you only care about your own goal.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: No matter how many times I repeat myself. Justin Wu: Because my goal is the only one that matters.;<> Wayne Zheng: We have nothing to talk about, then.;<> Justin Wu: That I do agree with.;<> Justin Wu: This conversation is a mistake. I should be going.;<> <> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Don't go just yet. Justin Wu: I've wasted enough time.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: And that I do agree with, too. Wayne Zheng: I've wasted more time than he deserves.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <>;<> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Dammit!!!;<> Wayne Zheng: He will die a stubborn old man. Wayne Zheng: Now we must switch back to the original plan. He only slowed us down. Wayne Zheng: It's almost time to meet Mr. Yan.;<> Wayne Zheng: I must go back to the office. Need to finish some critical work.;<> Wayne Zheng: Go to the lobby, Jason. Keep Mr. Yan occupied if he's already there. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What should I do? Wayne Zheng: Entertain him. ->Sure thing. Wayne Zheng: Appreciated. ->Sorry. I shouldn't have asked Justin to see you. Wayne Zheng: No problem. You didn't know he's so stupidly stubborn. Wayne Zheng: Geez... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: See you later. //等整尾離開，這裡塞一段程芳跟主角檢討 <> <<#Wait(2)>> Frances Cheng(c): J... Frances Cheng(c): You have tried. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Not hard enough. ->Yeah, there's only so much I can do. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng(c): ... Frances Cheng(c): You know it's not your fault, right? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I do, Fran. Frances Cheng(c): Good. ->I still hope it could have ended differently. Frances Cheng(c): ...Yeah. <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>> <<#EndIf>> Justin Wu: So, what's your plan...?;<> Justin Wu: I'm trying to take your perspective, and I still don't think it's OK to send people to the Cellar only to be killed. Wayne Zheng: I totally agree with you.;<> <<#EndIf>> //if操作 <<#If($MCBelongControlDepartment=1)>> Wayne Zheng: Wait, Justin.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Could you think it over again? Justin Wu: What's that supposed to mean? Wayne Zheng: I mean...;<> Wayne Zheng: ...Maybe you're overestimating the situation? Wayne Zheng: Maybe it's hard but not that hard. Justin Wu: ...;<>;<> Justin Wu: Within Ops, we have been discussing the repair of the Cellar for years. Justin Wu: I'm absolutely positive. Justin Wu: With so many juicers racked up, the repair process will be significantly slower. Justin Wu: Time is money. You know what I mean?;<> Wayne Zheng: I do!;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Maybe this delay won't be that long?;<> Wayne Zheng: I've done some preliminary research, and the outlook doesn't seem to be so bad. Justin Wu: ...;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->The Ops team knows better.;<>;//+1 Wayne Zheng: Sure. Although I have done my homework, too... ->Maybe the Ops team got it wrong? <> Thought: Justin's face suggests he can't believe I said that... Justin Wu: What the hell have you people been smoking...? <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: If we miss this opportunity, Justin...;<> Justin Wu: I know what you're about to say. No means no. Justin Wu: My responsibility is to make you see reality as it is. Wayne Zheng: *Sigh*;<> Wayne Zheng: Let me put it this way. I don't think the situation is hopeless.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Your team keeps using reality and practicality as excuses. Wayne Zheng: Why don't you try to think outside the box for once?;<> Wayne Zheng: Don't you get that feeling, Jason?;<> Wayne Zheng: In their world there is only certainty, no such thing as possibility. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->The Cellar is their domain. Their opinion should have more weight.;<>;//+1 Wayne Zheng: Yes, should have more weight.;<> Wayne Zheng: But not all the weight. <<@阿偉同意應該以操作組為主>> Wayne Zheng: I have done plenty of research. Can't you people just hear me out? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm sorry but it takes time, and time is money.;<>;//+1 Wayne Zheng: ... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Trust me. The Ops team wants to fix the Cellar more than anyone else does.;<>;//+1 Wayne Zheng: I do believe so, but... ->Do we have to spend time on every random opinion?;<>;//+1 Wayne Zheng: Of course not. But mine is backed by research... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Right. We should explore every avenue. Justin Wu: Jason...;<>;<> Justin Wu: I thought you have heard this more than enough times.;<>;<> Justin Wu: Discussing and exploring ideas take time. Justin Wu: Time that we don't have.;<>;<> Justin Wu: Each of us have a domain. Don't cross the line. It's called teamwork. <>;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Not all possibilities are worth exploring.;<>;//+1 Wayne Zheng: Sure. You are the professionals, and you should be the judge of that. Wayne Zheng: But at least give my opinion a serious look before writing it off, will you? <<#GoTo(@阿偉同意應該以操作組為主)>> ->He's right. We can always use new perspectives. Justin Wu: Jason...;<>;<> Justin Wu: I thought you have heard this more than enough times.;<>;<> Justin Wu: Discussing and exploring ideas take time. Justin Wu: Time that we don't have.;<>;<> Justin Wu: Each of us have a domain. Don't cross the line. It's called teamwork. <>;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> //IF數值不夠，預設0，至少要+2 <<#If($DepartmentCoopSuccess<1)>> Justin Wu: I've heard enough.;<>;<> Justin Wu: After everything, you still refuse to recognize my professional judgment.;<> Wayne Zheng: ... Justin Wu: I can't work with you when you can't show the slightest trust.;<> Wayne Zheng: The feeling is mutual.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: But there is too much at stake. We must work together. Don't be so stubborn. Justin Wu: Stubborn? I could say the same about you.;<>;<> Justin Wu: The only stubborn head in this room is you.;<> Justin Wu: You reject reality. Worse still, you wanna drag us into your imaginary fairyland. Justin Wu: I'm done talking with you.;<> Justin Wu: ... Wayne Zheng: ...;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Typical you. Wayne Zheng: Refusing to communicate at the first sign of confrontation. Justin Wu: I've had enough of that, too.;<> Justin Wu: What you want is not communication.;<>;<> Justin Wu: It's subjugation, forcing everyone to agree with you. Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: If you say so. This meeting is over.;<> <> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Don't go just yet. Justin Wu: No, we're done.;<> Wayne Zheng: That I do agree with.;<> Wayne Zheng: I've wasted more time than he deserves.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> Justin Wu: ...;<> Justin Wu: That's Wayne for you. Forever a dreamer.;<> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Now we must switch back to the original plan. He only slowed us down.;<>;<> Justin Wu: It's almost time to meet Mr. Yan. Justin Wu: I must go back to the office. Need to finish some critical work.;<> Justin Wu: Go to the lobby, Jason. Keep Mr. Yan occupied if he's already there. <<#Simple(8)>> ->What should I do? Justin Wu: Entertain him. ->Sure thing. Justin Wu: Appreciated. ->Sorry. I shouldn't have asked Wayne to see you. Justin Wu: He lives in his little bubble. Justin Wu: Not your fault. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: See you later. <> //等吳俊恩離開，這裡塞一段程芳跟主角檢討 <<#Wait(2)>> Frances Cheng(c): J... Frances Cheng(c): You have tried. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Not hard enough. ->Yeah, there's only so much I can do. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng(c): ... Frances Cheng(c): You know it's not your fault, right? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I do, Fran. Frances Cheng(c): Good. ->I still hope it could have ended differently. Frances Cheng(c): ...Yeah. <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>> <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Okay...;<> Wayne Zheng: You're right. I should trust your professional opinion. Wayne Zheng: It's just that... Wayne Zheng: I really hope this could somehow work out.;<> Justin Wu: I hope the same, Wayne.;<> Wayne Zheng: ...There's simply no way to perform any work without unracking everyone? Justin Wu: Correct. Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Then I have this idea. I told Jason earlier.;<> Wayne Zheng: It could be salvaged, I think, if we improvise a little.;<> Justin Wu: What's your plan...? <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: Maybe we've been aiming unnecessarily high this whole time. What if we don't need to fix the whole Cellar?;<> Wayne Zheng: If the goal is just to avoid further casualty, we can simply use the Cellar in a smarter way. Wayne Zheng: The Cellar is not running at full capacity, meaning we can move racks around. Wayne Zheng: Move all contributors out of the most damaged zones, then bring local systems offline for repair and maintenance. Wayne Zheng: That will save a lot of time and money.;<> Justin Wu: ...Correct.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Repeat and rotate. Work on a couple of zones at a time. That should do the trick. Justin Wu: Theoretically, yes. Wayne Zheng: That's my plan. What do you think?;<> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: ...You said the board wants us to take in more people?;<> Wayne Zheng: Yes.;<> Justin Wu: How many? Got a ballpark figure? Wayne Zheng: Up to 90% of Silencio's full capacity. Justin Wu: ...What!?;<>;<> Justin Wu: That's impossible. Justin Wu: Old farms like Silencio are supposed to run at 70% capacity maximum.;<> Justin Wu: The rest 30% must be reserved for redundancy. We need even more redundancy for your rotate plan to work. Justin Wu: More importantly...;<> Justin Wu: The life support system can't handle 90% capacity. Justin Wu: Air ventilation, radiation and temperature control. A system overload could kill a ton of people.;<> Justin Wu: Even if that doesn't happen, they will suffer permanent damage over time. Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Justin Wu: Will they accept lower capacity?;<> Wayne Zheng: ...I think not.;<> Justin Wu: Then it's a dead end. Wayne Zheng: ... Thought: Wayne looks angry... Wayne Zheng: They knew it... Justin Wu: Who? Wayne Zheng: This is all part of their plan... Wayne Zheng: They planned to get rid of the "useless" part of the population all along.;<> Wayne Zheng: This unreasonable demand to take in more contributors despite our physical limitation... Wayne Zheng: They didn't care about side effects. All it takes is a bit of censorship and some PR campaigns.;<> Wayne Zheng: How hard is it to silence thousands of slum dwellers, huh? Wayne Zheng: FUCK THIS!!;<>;//崩潰敲桌低頭 Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: They almost had me. Justin Wu: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: Thank you for the wakeup call... Wayne Zheng: Or else I'd be working happily as their minion to kill thousands upon thousands... Justin Wu: ... Wayne Zheng: Sorry, Justin. Wayne Zheng: I admit I wasn't paying enough attention to your warnings... Justin Wu: ...Yeah. Justin Wu: What now?;<> Justin Wu: My way is a dead end, yours is no-go...;<>;<> Justin Wu: Maintain the status quo? Is that the best we can do? Wayne Zheng: It's not sustainable...;<> Wayne Zheng: ... Justin Wu: ...;<> Frances Cheng(c): Instead of repairing everything, what if we just update the Cellar's life support system to match those of new farms?;<> Frances Cheng(c): Ask them, J.;<> <> <<#Simple(10)>> ->What if we just update the Cellar's life support system to match those of new farms? <<@配合程芳問能不能更新系統>> Justin Wu: That...;<> Wayne Zheng: Will not work...?;<> Wayne Zheng: I mean, if it works, we should have done it a long time ago... Justin Wu: Wait. That could actually work!;<>;<> <<@篩選路線解釋幫地窖安裝新系統橋段>> Justin Wu: We did consider it a long time ago, but eventually gave it up.;<> Justin Wu: Because it takes a ton of money. Wayne Zheng: But now we could get massive funding!;<>;<> Justin Wu: Yep, although this update will cost about 50% more than repairing the old system.;<> Wayne Zheng: I think it's still within a reasonable range. Leave the negotiation to me.;<> Justin Wu: Great. I will ask my team to verify the technical feasibility.;<>;//看手機 Wayne Zheng: Well done, Jason! You solved one heck of a problem!;<> Justin Wu: To pull that off, though, we will need technical support from HQ.;<> Wayne Zheng: Is that going to be a problem?;<> Justin Wu: As far as I know, the tech team at HQ predominantly want to see this farm shut down. Wayne Zheng: The Red Room team?;<> Justin Wu: Yeah. Thought: Yuna Hsu did mention something about it yesterday... Thought: There are two factions in HQ, wrestling for dominance.;<>;<> Thought: One wants to double down on technological innovation and increase the profit of flop farming. Thought: The other plans to trade profit for political leverage, so that Vision doesn't need to solely rely on flop farming. Thought: Both believe they represent the right way forward...;<> Wayne Zheng: Factions, huh... Wayne Zheng: We must pick a side and fight against the other. Wayne Zheng: ... Frances Cheng(c): You know what? Upgrading the Cellar could actually serve both sides. Thought: ...What?;<> Frances Cheng(c): It depends on how we package the idea. Frances Cheng(c): I'll explain to you, J. Please forward the message to Wayne and Justin. Frances Cheng(c): An upgraded Cellar holds more contributors, which obviously earns Vision the political leverage some board members want.;<> Frances Cheng(c): To make that a reality, HQ's technical team will be heavily involved. Frances Cheng(c): In doing that, the other faction builds a convincing case that technological advancement is the backbone of Vision. <> Wayne Zheng: A small win for each side, and no one has the upper hand...;<>;<> Justin Wu: Excuse me? Wayne Zheng: I get it! I think I can navigate between two factions!;<>;<> Frances Cheng(c): Looks like they don't need me, J. Frances Cheng(c): I see that Silencio has a wise new manager now. Frances Cheng(c): Ms. Yeh's hard work is paid off. <> //IF篩選組 <<#If($MCBelongSelectDepartment=1)>> Wayne Zheng: Justin, could you please give me a preliminary feasibility report on upgrading the Cellar?;<> Justin Wu: We don't have time for anything fancy. But if repurposing old data is not a problem, yeah, I can.;<> Justin Wu: Let me check first.;<> Justin Wu: I've got to go back to the office. //吳俊恩離開 <>;<> Wayne Zheng: Me, too. Need to revise my previous report.;<> Wayne Zheng: It's almost time to meet Mr. Yan. Wayne Zheng: Go to the lobby, Jason. Keep Mr. Yan occupied if he's already there. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What do you need it for? Wayne Zheng: Um, just keep him busy with whatever...? ->Sure thing. Wayne Zheng: Thanks a bunch! <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: See you later. <<#EndIf>> //IF操作組 <<#If($MCBelongControlDepartment=1)>> Wayne Zheng: Justin, could you please give me a preliminary feasibility report on upgrading the Cellar?;<> Justin Wu: I think I can just repurpose my original report.;<> Justin Wu: We don't have much time, though. Mr. Yan's appointment is coming up. Justin Wu: Let me check first.;<> Justin Wu: I've got to go back to the office. //吳俊恩離開 <>;<> Wayne Zheng: I need to invent a makeshift proposal, too.;<> Wayne Zheng: ...Can we really make it? Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Go to the lobby, Jason. Keep Mr. Yan occupied if he's already there. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What do you need it for? Wayne Zheng: Um, just keep him busy with whatever...? ->Sure thing. Wayne Zheng: Thanks a bunch! <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: See you later. <<#EndIf>> //等鄭緯離開，這裡塞一段程芳跟主角檢討 <> <<#Wait(3)>> Frances Cheng(c): It worked!;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Right, all thanks to you. Frances Cheng(c): And you, too! ->What worked? Frances Cheng(c): You made them work together, dummy! Frances Cheng(c): Haven't you realized? Haha. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng(c): Great! This is the best outcome possible! <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why are you so excited? Frances Cheng(c): Because, um... Frances Cheng(c): I feel guilty for not being there in Silencio's most crucial moment. Frances Cheng(c): If the farm collapses, I will feel liable. Frances Cheng(c): Because I should have been there taking leadership. <<#Simple(8)>> ->This is not your fault at all. ->Don't look at it like that. Frances Cheng(c): I know. Still, can't help it. Haha... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng(c): ... Frances Cheng(c): J... Frances Cheng(c): You didn't have to do any of this, but you did it nonetheless. Frances Cheng(c): Well done! <<#Simple(8)>> ->Yeah. Frances Cheng(c): Come on! Show me some spirit! Frances Cheng(c): Haha. ->I was just doing the right thing. Frances Cheng(c): And you have done it. Congrats. Thought: She sounds... Thought: Appreciative? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng(c): Wayne solved the final piece of the puzzle on his own. He will make a great leader. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Yeah. // ->The solution that you didn't get to finish, what's the last half? Frances Cheng(c): You will know soon enough. Hee hee. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng(c): ... Frances Cheng(c): I guess he will be the next manager. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Wayne? Frances Cheng(c): Right. ->Justin? Frances Cheng(c): No, I mean Wayne. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Frances Cheng(c): Now, go to the entrance lobby. Frances Cheng(c): They asked you to buy them some time, remember? <> <<#End>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: What if we just update the Cellar's life support system to match those of new farms?;<>;<> Frances Cheng(c): Ah, they found the solution on their own.;<> <> Justin Wu: That way, we can take in more people.;<> Justin Wu: The new system also helps to reduce accidents. Wayne Zheng: That is neat. Why didn't you say so before?;<> Justin Wu: Because it's impossible to pull off.;<> <<#GoTo(@篩選路線解釋幫地窖安裝新系統橋段)>> //

title: 雙贏路線阿緯俊恩頂樓2
body: Wayne Zheng: Say, do you know that...;<>;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: This helipad is dedicated to the CEO's personal use. <<#Simple(8)>> ->For real? Wayne Zheng: Yeah, he has his own helipad. ->I know. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: So what? Wayne Zheng: Sitting on his helipad like we own the place feels... Justin Wu: Feels great.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Haha!;<> Wayne Zheng: Remember to run like hell when you hear his carrier coming, boys!;<> Justin Wu: Definitely will do! Haha!;<>;<> <> <<#End>>

title: 篩選組失敗結局
body: Tyler Cao: Jason, you're back?;<> Quentin Qiu: Hey.;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Hey. // ->How are you guys doing? Tyler Cao: Same old same old, haha... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: I thought we could change things...;<>;<> Cathy Hsieh: It's not so bad. At least we didn't screw up anything! Tyler Cao: Right, Ops won't get the farm closure they want.;<>;<> Tyler Cao: We have kinda bought us more time?;<> Quentin Qiu: I don't feel like we have achieved anything! Tyler Cao: Well, we have tried our best.;<> Quentin Qiu: Hey, where's Wayno?;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: He should have come back before Jayster, eh?;<> Tyler Cao: Right.;<> Cathy Hsieh: I guess he wants some quiet time. Quentin Qiu: To cope with the defeat? Tyler Cao: Is he that fragile?;<> Cathy Hsieh: Hard to say. He was betting heavily on this chance...;<> Tyler Cao: Yeah... Quentin Qiu: I hope he's alright. <> Wayne Zheng: Huh?;<> Wayne Zheng: Why aren't you people working? Cathy Hsieh: Wayne!?;<> Quentin Qiu: Damn! We thought you were somewhere entertaining suicidal thoughts! Wayne Zheng: Suicidal!?;<> Wayne Zheng: Come on! Do you really think I'm that easily crushed? Quentin Qiu: Well, who knows? Haha!;<> Tyler Cao: Where did you go?;<> Wayne Zheng: I made some phone calls. There were arrangements to be made.;<> Wayne Zheng: Then I went to order a bunch of milk tea for everyone. The delivery guy could be here any minute.;<> Cathy Hsieh: Wow! Wayne Zheng: You have earned it, guys! The report was excellent work.;<> Quentin Qiu: Milk tea? From which store?;<> Tyler Cao: Beggars can't be choosers.;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: This beggar needs to adjust his anticipation level!;<>;<> Tyler Cao: Cheeky...;<> Wayne Zheng: Haha, it's your favorite store.;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: Sweeet! You know me, Wayno!;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: If everything's alright, I'll be off to work.;<> Cathy Hsieh: ...;<> Cathy Hsieh: Uh, wait, Wayne!;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Hm?;<> Cathy Hsieh: ...Are you really OK? Wayne Zheng: ...;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: I am.;<> Wayne Zheng: I know some of you are not convinced, but I'm dealing with it exceptionally well. Tyler Cao: Exceptionally well? Wayne Zheng: Yeah.;<> Wayne Zheng: The outcome was a bummer, but then I thought... Wayne Zheng: Hey, at least we managed to save the day. Wayne Zheng: As long as we have today, there's always tomorrow.;<> Wayne Zheng: I have faith in us.;<> Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: Awww I'm on the verge of tears.;<>;<> Tyler Cao: Shut up.;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: I seriously am!;<> Tyler Cao: Oh, okay... Wayne Zheng: Haha! Seriously, we have work to do, everyone!;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Don't forget your share of milk tea, Jason!;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Thanks! ->Sure. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <>;<> Wayne Zheng: Hey, Jason. The crisis is over for now, you know... Wayne Zheng: But your job formally starts next week, remember?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Oops, totally forgot about it. Wayne Zheng: Ha! I knew it.;<> ->Yeah, I'm aware. Wayne Zheng: Great!;<> ->Huh? Why? Wayne Zheng: Ha, I figured you'd forget. Wayne Zheng: Remember the temporary hiring freeze ordered by HQ? It ends by the end of this week.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: We don't need you tomorrow for work, you hear that? Wayne Zheng: Although we need you any time as a buddy. Haha. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sure. Wayne Zheng: "Sure"? That's it? I was telling a joke, man!;<> Cathy Hsieh: It's not funny, Wayne! You should listen to yourself.;<> Wayne Zheng: Gah! Is that so? Haha...;<>;<> ->Yeah! I'm gonna be a good couch potato all day long. Wayne Zheng: Ah. Quentin Qiu: Niiice. Cathy Hsieh: I want to be a couch potato, too. ->What if I have this sudden urge that compels me to come to work? Wayne Zheng: Ha! Feel free to follow your heart then! <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Oh, Jason.;<> Wayne Zheng: Since we won't see you again until next week, you may want to spend a bit more time with everyone before you go. <> <<#End>>

title: 操作組失敗結局
body:
//胖胖在用電腦，賴羽嬪跟柯義緯面對面側站(可以的話有個姿勢比較好，在苦惱討論的感覺)，賴潔站到胖胖旁邊，陳家騏在自己座位附近正面靠桌抱胸
Thought: Everyone's going home, huh...;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局左看轉右)>>
Penelope Lai: Hey, Jason...;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局右看)>>
Penelope Lai: Justin told us to go home.
Wyatt Ke: There's nothing left to do, anyway.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Then why are you still here?
    Penelope Lai: I don't know...
    Penelope Lai: It just feels wrong to be anywhere but here at this hour...
->Where's Justin?
    Kaylee Chen: In the division lead's office, doing whatever.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Wyatt Ke: Aaaahhh we deserve better than this!;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局右看轉左)>>;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局右看轉低頭)>>
Wyatt Ke: This can't be happening...
Penelope Lai: Well, we can't do anything about it now...
Wyatt Ke: What are we gonna do next?;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局低頭轉右看)>>
Penelope Lai: ...;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局左看轉低頭)>>
Kaylee Chen: Give it a rest! I don't want to hear this!;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局右看轉左)>>;<<Play(陳家騏,MBU_陳家騏_正站插腰右看返回)>>
Wyatt Ke: Get the hell out of here then!
Kaylee Chen: What did you say?;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局低頭轉左看)>>;<<Play(陳家騏,MBU_陳家騏_正站插腰右看)>>
Penelope Lai: Calm down, you both!
Kaylee Chen: ...
Wyatt Ke: ...;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局左看轉低頭)>>
//攝影機拍胖胖
<<Set($DLState,胖胖說該做的事情基本沒變)>>
Paxton Luo: We will continue to do the same thing.;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局左看返回)>>;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_壞結局右看)>>;<<Play(陳家騏,MBU_陳家騏_正站插腰右看返回)>>
Paxton Luo: Keep the Cellar serviced, avoid casualties as much as we can.;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局低頭轉左看)>>
Paxton Luo: Screening will keep sending people down. We do our best to keep them safe.
Paxton Luo: Don't you agree?
Kaylee Chen: ...;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局左看)>>;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_壞結局右看返回)>>
Wyatt Ke: ...;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局左看轉低頭)>>
//吳俊恩走出組長辦公室。
<<Set($DLState,俊恩走出操作組辦公室收偉操作劇情)>>
<<#Wait(1)>>
<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局左看轉右)>>
Justin Wu: Packs is right.;<<Play(陳家騏,MBU_陳家騏_正站插腰右看)>>
Wyatt Ke: Justin...;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局低頭轉右看)>>;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_壞結局右看)>>
Justin Wu: We admit defeat today, but it will not change our long term plan.
Justin Wu: Keep doing what we do, save what we must save.
Justin Wu: As Silencio lives on, our work goes on.
Wyatt Ke: Yeah.;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局右看轉左)>>;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局右看轉低頭)>>
Kaylee Chen: ...For sure.;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_壞結局右看返回)>>
Penelope Lai: ...
Penelope Lai: Do we just keep doing this indefinitely...?
Justin Wu: No.;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局左看轉右)>>;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_壞結局右看)>>
Justin Wu: We are not giving up our cause. Silencio must be shut down.;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局低頭轉右看)>>;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站隱忍)>>
Justin Wu: One single defeat doesn't deprive us of our future.
Justin Wu: As long as we have a consensus that that Silencio must go...
Justin Wu: As long as we care about juicers and each other...
Justin Wu: We shall not give up.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站隱忍返回)>>
//眾人猶豫
Penelope Lai: ...;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局右看轉低頭)>>
Wyatt Ke: ...;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局右看放手)>>;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_壞結局右看返回)>>
Kaylee Chen: ...;<<Play(陳家騏,MBU_陳家騏_正站插腰右看返回)>>
Justin Wu: I know that the outlook seems grim.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站抬雙手)>>
Justin Wu: If any of you want to transfer to a better farm, you have every right to.;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局低頭轉右看)>>;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_壞結局右看)>>;<<Play(陳家騏,MBU_陳家騏_正站插腰右看)>>
Justin Wu: I will even broker transfer deals for you, if only—
<<Set($DLState,失敗路線二度拍胖胖)>>
Paxton Luo: I won't go.;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局右看放手轉正)>>;<<Play(陳家騏,MBU_陳家騏_正站插腰右看返回)>>;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站抬雙手返回)>>
Justin Wu: ...Packs.;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局右看返回)>>
Jesse Lai: Me, neither.
Penelope Lai: ...
Penelope Lai: Count me in! I'm not leaving!;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局右看)>>;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_壞結局右看返回)>>;<<Play(陳家騏,MBU_陳家騏_正站插腰右看)>>
Wyatt Ke: Hey, what can you guys do without me, eh?;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局右看放手轉正返回)>>
Kaylee Chen: I will stay, too. To look after as many juicers as I can.
Justin Wu: ...;<<Set($DLState,失敗路線二度拍胖胖結束)>>;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_壞結局右看)>>;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站低頭)>>
Wyatt Ke: Whatever comes this way, we sure can handle it together!;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局右看轉左)>>;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局右看放手返回)>>
Penelope Lai: Yep!
Justin Wu: ...Thank you, everyone.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站低頭感動)>>
Kaylee Chen: No need to. We are doing it for our own values and faiths!;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局左看轉右)>>
Justin Wu: Still, thank you...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站低頭感動返回)>>
Kaylee Chen: Hey, no more thanks!
Justin Wu: Ah, sorry...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站低頭返回)>>
Wyatt Ke: Hahahahaha!;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局右看抬頭)>>
Penelope Lai: Haha!;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局右看微笑)>>
Paxton Luo: Heh.;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局右看抬頭返回)>>
Justin Wu: Anyway...;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局右看微笑返回)>>;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站插腰)>>
Justin Wu: Today's work is done. Spend the rest of the day on whatever you like.
Justin Wu: You have all done good work.
Paxton Luo: You, too!;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局右看返回)>>;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_結局右看返回)>>
Penelope Lai: We're all doing great!;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站插腰返回)>>
<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_結局左看)>>
<<#End>>

title: 兩人一起抵達
body: <> Wayne Zheng: Mr. Yan. Justin Wu: Mr. Yan. //if篩選 <<#If($MCBelongSelectDepartment=1)>> Shane Yan: Wayne. Shane Yan: ...And Justin, too? <<#EndIf>> //if操作 <<#If($MCBelongControlDepartment=1)>> Shane Yan: Justin. Shane Yan: ...And Wayne, too? <<#EndIf>> Shane Yan: I don't remember asking for you both? Wayne Zheng: We have a joint proposal, sir. Shane Yan: Proposal? Good. We could always use good proposals.;<>;<> Shane Yan: Send it to my office, please. I will read it later. Wayne Zheng: Mr. Yan.;<>;<> Shane Yan: ...;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Hear me out, please. Shane Yan: Ha, of course I will.;<>;<>;<> Shane Yan: You look like you're betting your life on it. I'm all ears. Shane Yan: Go on.;<> Wayne Zheng: I, together with Justin, have a proposal for the board.;<> Shane Yan: Not for me, but the board, huh.;<>;<> Shane Yan: So... Shane Yan: To which director shall I present it?;<> Wayne Zheng: We know that the board is divided into two factions. Wayne Zheng: This proposal is for the entire board as a whole.;<> Shane Yan: ... Thought: Yan's putting that snobby grin away. He's finally taking this seriously. Shane Yan: That is quite a surprise. Shane Yan: Could you please elaborate? Wayne Zheng: We plan to upgrade the Cellar's life support systems, so that Silencio could take in more contributors.;<> Shane Yan: ... Shane Yan: Do continue, please. //if篩選 <<#If($MCBelongSelectDepartment=1)>> Wayne Zheng: This contributes to one faction's objective.;<> Wayne Zheng: Help the government to curb unemployment, and gain political leverage in return. Shane Yan: This idea involves considerable money, a huge figure.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Indeed, however—;<> Shane Yan: AND. Shane Yan: Upgrading the Cellar is impossible without help from the Red Room team. Correct me if I'm wrong.;<> Wayne Zheng: Yes.;<> Shane Yan: I trust you're well aware that the board is in two confronting factions. Shane Yan: The Red Room leads one. They want to retire Silencio, because this pile of old junk has little salvage value.;<> Wayne Zheng: I am aware of that. Shane Yan: Give me one reason why the Red Room would want to lend you any support?;<> Wayne Zheng: If they stay out, they will lose not just the battle, but the whole war.;<>;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Their opponent is dealt a new hand. If they don't follow, they simply lose. Shane Yan: ...;<>;<> Shane Yan: You are aware that you're supposed to represent the interests of the pro-Silencio faction?;<> Wayne Zheng: I am. Shane Yan: Assigning a role to their opponents is very much against their best interests, no? Wayne Zheng: I wouldn't put it that way.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: This proposal represents the best interests of both sides. Shane Yan: Is that so?;<>;<> Thought: That cheeky grin again... Wayne Zheng: The pro-Silencio side could protest as much as they want. But once presented, this proposal will break the equilibrium.;<>;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: The Red Room will find their own role in this plan and dash into action.;<> Wayne Zheng: This may not be the most appropriate way to put it, but... Wayne Zheng: Once the ball starts rolling, no one could stop it.;<> <<#EndIf>> //if操作 <<#If($MCBelongControlDepartment=1)>> Justin Wu: This contributes to one faction's objective.;<> Justin Wu: Namely, find new growth for Vision through technological advancements. Shane Yan: If my memory serves, the Red Room specifically told you to shut Silencio down?;<>;<>;<> Justin Wu: Yes, however— Shane Yan: AND.;<> Shane Yan: Suppose you manage to upgrade the Cellar, what then? Keep Silencio running? Wayne Zheng: We will increase the Cellar's operational capacity, so that Vision—;<>;<> Shane Yan: Gains more political leverage by helping to curb unemployment. Wayne Zheng: Exactly.;<>;<> Shane Yan: ...;<>;<> Shane Yan: ...Interesting ideas. More importantly, it could actually work. Shane Yan: You came up with this idea?;<> Justin Wu: No, Wayne did the thinking.;<>;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: Based on crucial facts and analysis provided by Justin. Shane Yan: Yeah yeah yeah, I can tell you're good friends.;<>;<>;<> Shane Yan: ...That is quite a surprise.;<> Shane Yan: So, you are aware of the struggle for power within the board, and want to use it to your advantage.;<> Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Shane Yan: Relax. I'm not saying it's a bad thing. Wayne Zheng: ...I see.;<>;<> Shane Yan: Hmmm...;<>;<> Shane Yan: One faction hopes to enhance their ties with the government, by helping to curb unemployment.;<>;<> Shane Yan: The object is to oust Sunrise and eventually make Vision the de facto owner of the government. Shane Yan: I suppose you don't need any reminding, though... Shane Yan: The Red Room leads the other faction. They want to retire Silencio, because this pile of old junk has little salvage value.;<> Wayne Zheng: I am aware of that. Shane Yan: ...;<> Shane Yan: You are aware that you're supposed to represent the interests of the pro-closure faction? Justin Wu: I am.;<>;<>;<> Shane Yan: Assigning a role to their opponents is very much against their best interests, no? Justin Wu: ...I wouldn't put it that way. Wayne Zheng: This proposal represents the best interests of both sides.;<> Shane Yan: Is that so?;<>;<> Thought: That cheeky grin again... Wayne Zheng: The majority would agree that our proposal is in Vision's best interest, once we present it.;<>;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: The Red Room may fight against it all they want. By then their opponents would be in action.;<> Wayne Zheng: This may not be the most appropriate way to put it, but...;<> Wayne Zheng: Once the ball starts rolling, no one could stop it.;<> <<#EndIf>> Shane Yan: Hahahahaha!;<> Shane Yan: Great!;<> Shane Yan: OK. Let's assume your plan is feasible. Shane Yan: There is one crucial factor to make it truly work, yes? Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: ...Yes, and this factor is you. Shane Yan: You know I don't belong to either side. As long as some change happens to Silencio, I'm good.;<>;<> Shane Yan: This plan of yours takes months to take effect. Shane Yan: Hell, maybe even years.;<>;<> Shane Yan: During this time, Silencio will continue in this half-dead state. Shane Yan: Why should I present your proposal to the board? Shane Yan: Why should I prolong a problem that could potentially solve itself in no time?;<> //if篩選 <<#If($MCBelongSelectDepartment=1)>> Shane Yan: And risk offending half the board, the pro-Silencio faction? <<#EndIf>> //if操作 <<#If($MCBelongControlDepartment=1)>> Shane Yan: And risk offending half the board, the pro-closure faction? <<#EndIf>> Shane Yan: They could have solved my problem for me. Thought: This is it, the last hurdle...;<>;<> Thought: It's all for naught if this bastard refuses to pitch the idea to the board... Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: Because it will potentially give you a quick path to join the board.;<>;<> Shane Yan: ... Wayne Zheng: ...The way I see it, this is your next career milestone, no? Shane Yan: ...;<>;<>;<> Shane Yan: In a sense. Shane Yan: Now I'm interested. Go on.;<> Wayne Zheng: To join the board, you will need support from as many directors as possible.;<> //if篩選 <<#If($MCBelongSelectDepartment=1)>> Wayne Zheng: The current situation is, if you show support to the pro-Silencio side as agreed, the other side will see you as a public enemy. <<#EndIf>> //if操作 <<#If($MCBelongControlDepartment=1)>> Wayne Zheng: The current situation is, if you show support to the pro-closure side as agreed, the other side will see you as a public enemy. <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: With our plan, you might under-deliver on some promises previously made. This will raise some eyebrows.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: But the bottom line is, no one will take serious offense. Wayne Zheng: Both sides get something out of this deal. Vision will love to see that.;<> Wayne Zheng: And you will prove to the board that you're a man with extraordinary navigation skills.;<> Wayne Zheng: Everybody wins. Shane Yan: ...;<> Shane Yan: Sounds perfect. Shane Yan: But you know there are too many moving parts in this plan, don't you?;<> Shane Yan: The original plan is much easier to pull off. Wayne Zheng: Yes, but—;<>;<> Shane Yan: Don't interrupt. Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Shane Yan: However, I didn't become the CEO by playing it safe.;<> Shane Yan: Higher risk comes with more return, vice versa. This is my motto.;<> Shane Yan: So, weighing all the pros and cons, I do agree to work with you.;<> Wayne Zheng: This is...great!;<>;<> Justin Wu: So, you will present our proposal to the board? Shane Yan: Not only present, I'll actively pitch your ideas to them.;<>;<> Shane Yan: You have done great work, you both. Wayne Zheng: Thank you very much, sir.;<> Shane Yan: Don't be so formal. Relax.;<> Shane Yan: Especially you, Wayne. You started this conversation looking like a man ready to die at any moment. Wayne Zheng: Haha, sorry about that.;<>;<>;<> Shane Yan: And Justin. I see this plan is backed by your professional analysis.;<> Shane Yan: Well done.;<>;<> Justin Wu: Thank you. Shane Yan: Now, we have only one issue left.;<>;<> Shane Yan: Presuming this plan works, Silencio still needs a new manager.;<> Shane Yan: Wouldn't you agree? Wayne Zheng: ...;<>;<> Justin Wu: ... Shane Yan: Hey, do I sense tension building up again?;<> Shane Yan: Don't tell me you never considered this...? Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Justin Wu: ...;<> Shane Yan: Hmm, who should I choose, eh?;<>;<> Shane Yan: ...;<> Shane Yan: Relax, haha, that was just for suspense.;<> Shane Yan: You will be the next manager, Wayne. Wayne Zheng: I—;<>;<> Shane Yan: The upgrade and expansion will need a lot of negotiation skill. You are the ideal person to take the lead. Shane Yan: Of course, since the upgrade itself is very much tech-intensive... Shane Yan: Justin, you will provide support as the lead of the Ops Division.;<>;<>;<> Shane Yan: With you two in the forefront, I believe Silencio will have a bright future.;<> Shane Yan: No, I am confident.;<>;<> Shane Yan: You are already exhibiting exceptional leadership. Wayne Zheng: Thank you, sir. Shane Yan: You're welcome. Shane Yan: It's a shame that Ms. Yeh is not here with us. I'd pay an arm and a leg to see her face.;<> Shane Yan: She hated me so much, yet ended up leaving me some of the best talent. Ha!;<> Shane Yan: OK. If you have nothing more to add, I should be on my way.;<> Shane Yan: There is a mountain of preparation to do.;<> <> Wayne Zheng: Thank you, sir, and have a nice day.;<> Shane Yan: Ah, you two might want to keep an eye on the news for the next couple of days.;<>;<> Shane Yan: If everything is working as it's supposed to, Vision will have an announcement to make. Wayne Zheng: May I know what it's about?;<> Shane Yan: Don't let me spoil the fun. You will know soon enough.;<> Shane Yan: And you, too, Jason. Don't forget to check the news.;<>;<> Shane Yan: Goodbye.;<> <<#End>>

title: 雙贏路線回辦公室
body:
//球球跟胖胖在對戰，胖胖坐在穩哥的位子
//曹泰銘跟賴傑在嘴胖胖球球
//采欣跟幾個操作組的人在她的座位參考她寫的東西
//廖哥還在自己座位工作


//葉慈恩在茶水間生悶氣
Jesse Lai: Hey! Jason!;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_結局左看)>>;<<Play(吳晉峰,MBU_吳晉峰_轉身左看)>>
Thought: Jesse...?;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看)>>;<<Play(曹泰銘,MBU_曹泰銘_側站插腰看外返回)>>
Quentin Qiu: Welcome back!;<<Set($DLState,雙贏主角轉頭看球那邊)>>;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_轉身)>>;<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局右看)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Hey.
    //
->What's going on?
    Quentin Qiu: Celebrate our victory!
->...
<<#EndSimple>>

Quentin Qiu: Of course they are! Ops and Screening will be a lot closer going forward!;<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局右看轉抬頭)>>;<<Play(曹泰銘,MBU_曹泰銘_側站插腰看內)>>;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看返回)>>
Quentin Qiu: No more turf wars. We're all brothers and sisters!
Tyler Cao: ...We're not gangsters, dude.
Jesse Lai: Your office is nice. A lot better than ours.;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看)>>;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看內)>>;<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局抬頭轉右看)>>;<<Play(曹泰銘,MBU_曹泰銘_側站插腰看內返回)>>
Kaylee Chen: Yeah, it's roomy and cozy.;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_結局左看轉右)>>;<<Play(陳家騏,MBU_陳家騏_結局左看)>>
Quentin Qiu: But you get better computers, no?;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看內返回)>>
Kaylee Chen: That we do.
<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_結局右看轉左)>>;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看返回)>>
//IF有邦交朋友
<<#If($SQ_PonPonStatus=3)>>
    Paxton Luo: Say, what computers do you use here at Screening? Got any specs sheet?;<<Play(羅龐安,MBU_羅龐安_好結局左看)>>;<<Play(曹泰銘,MBU_曹泰銘_側站插腰看內)>>;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_側站看內)>>;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看返回)>>
    Quentin Qiu: Heeey Packs! You're in charge of computers at Ops, don't you?;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_側站抱胸看內)>>
    Quentin Qiu: Let me show ya what we have in a bit. And we have some cool stuff!
    Paxton Luo: G-great!;<<Play(曹泰銘,MBU_曹泰銘_側站插腰看內返回)>>;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_側站抱胸看內返回)>>
    <<Play(羅龐安,MBU_羅龐安_好結局左看返回)>>;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_側站看內返回)>>;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看)>>
<<#EndIf>>
Tyler Cao: Jason! Don't just stand there. Help yourself to some food!;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_側站抱胸看內)>>
Quentin Qiu: Don't hold back! Everything's on Wayne and Justin!;<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局右看歡呼)>>;<<Play(曹泰銘,MBU_曹泰銘_側站插腰看內)>>;<<Play(吳晉峰,MBU_吳晉峰_轉身左看轉右)>>
Quentin Qiu: They said order whatever we want!;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_側站看內)>>;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看返回)>>
Jeff Wu: And you did exactly that...;<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局右看歡呼返回)>>;<<Play(曹泰銘,MBU_曹泰銘_側站插腰看內返回)>>
Wyatt Ke: Can we really handle this much food...?
Quentin Qiu: Come on, be a man!;<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局右看轉抬頭)>>;<<Play(曹泰銘,MBU_曹泰銘_側站插腰看外)>>
Quentin Qiu: I thought you Ops people could do better than that!
Wyatt Ke: Guess I'll have to live up to expectations, then.
Quentin Qiu: Show me your best! Ops loses if this party ends with any leftovers!
Jesse Lai: Hey!;<<Play(陳家騏,MBU_陳家騏_結局左看返回)>>;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看)>>;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看內)>>;<<Play(曹泰銘,MBU_曹泰銘_側站插腰看外返回)>>;<<Play(吳晉峰,MBU_吳晉峰_轉身右看轉左)>>;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看)>>
Jesse Lai: You call this making friends?;<<Play(邱奕宏,MBU_邱奕宏_好結局抬頭轉右看)>>;<<Play(柯義偉,MBU_柯義偉_側站抱胸看內返回)>>;<<Play(賴羽嬪,MBU_賴羽嬪_側站看內返回)>>
Quentin Qiu: A bit of friendly competition doesn't hurt!;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看內返回)>>
Jesse Lai: Ha! Well said!;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_結局左看思考)>>
<<Set($DLState,廖哥提醒食物要吃完進場)>>
<<#Wait(0.5)>>
George Liao: Don't waste your food.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_看外)>>
Quentin Qiu: Yessir, Mr. Liao!;<<Play(賴潔,MBU_賴潔_結局左看思考返回)>>
Cathy Hsieh: Yessir, Mr. Liao!
