<<#End>>

title: 簽約駱思敏
body:
//跟駱思敏簽約的時候，對駱思敏表達會保護他的孩子他就會很配合。問他一些問題，他就會自己以為主角他們是萬象幫忙收購小孩的人，然後很心急的問自己的孩子怎麼了？
//隆生扮黑臉的話結束以後她難過，她不扮黑臉也會難過對這個人這麼壞。以前白臉黑臉都是對罪犯，但現在只是一個很需要幫助的媽媽。
//這裡讓他開頭有分期但後面都是一直隻隻烏烏玩家可以插畫施壓


//這裡之後要補上透漏白壁虎傭兵載具的情報



Simone Lo: ...Hello.
Thought: She's rather cautious.
Simone Lo: I can ask questions?
Simone Lo: I mean, about the contract?
Lawrence Chang: Yes, but—;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約側坐點頭)>>
Simone Lo: I'm applying for all bonuses!;<<Play(駱思敏,MBU_駱思敏_簽約側坐前傾)>>
Simone Lo: I've done my research. All bonuses, including Vision's employee package.
Simone Lo: I'm qualified for all of them...right?;<<Play(駱思敏,MBU_駱思敏_簽約側坐前傾返回)>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Before that, Ms. Lo, we must verify a few things.
    Simone Lo: I knew it! This is one of your tricks!;<<Play(駱思敏,MBU_駱思敏_簽約側坐抬頭)>>
    Simone Lo: I read about it. You distract the candidates when they start to talk about bonuses.
    Simone Lo: It's against your company's policies. I-I'm gonna report you!;<<Play(駱思敏,MBU_駱思敏_簽約側坐抬頭返回)>>
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Calm down, please.
        Simone Lo: Why should I calm down? I'm fighting for my bonuses.
        Lawrence Chang: It's alright. Yes, you are qualified for all of them.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約側坐冷靜)>>;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約側坐看內)>>
        Lawrence Chang: Please just answer a few quick questions.
        <<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約側坐冷靜返回)>>;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_簽約側坐看內返回)>>
    ->We don't have a problem with your qualification. Just a few questions.
    <<#EndSimple>>
->Yes, you're qualified.
    Simone Lo: Good. Great.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約側坐看外)>>
    Simone Lo: That's it? Where do I sign?
    Lawrence Chang: One moment, please. We need you to answer a few quick questions.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約側坐看外返回)>>
<<#EndSimple>>

Simone Lo: What questions?
Lawrence Chang: Now, Ms. Lo. I trust you have heard about human trafficking activities in Taihe District?;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約側坐抬手)>>
Simone Lo: ...Human trafficking?;<<Play(駱思敏,MBU_駱思敏_簽約側坐低頭)>>
Thought: She's tensing up.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約側坐抬手返回)>>
Lawrence Chang: Yes. We are cooperating with the police to investigate these cases.;<<Play(駱思敏,MBU_駱思敏_簽約側坐低頭返回)>>;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約側坐點頭)>>
Lawrence Chang: Because we work with Taihe residents all the time and do due diligence, the police came to us asking for help.
Lawrence Chang: For that reason, we have some extra questions before signing the contract.
//IF玩家有面談且有問小孩的事情很多次
<<#If($AslKidsRelateQuestion>2)>>
    Simone Lo: Huh. That's why you kept asking about my daughter.
<<#EndIf>>
Simone Lo: I-I have nothing to do with human trafficking!;<<Play(駱思敏,MBU_駱思敏_簽約側坐抬頭)>>
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->Why did you stutter, then?
    Simone Lo: Hmph.;<<Play(駱思敏,MBU_駱思敏_簽約側坐抬頭返回)>>
    //if白臉
    <<#If($PlayGoodBadCop=1)>>
        23(c): You're the good cop, Jason.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約側坐看外)>>
        23(c): Befriend her.
        <<CloseCommunicator()>>
        <<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約側坐看外返回)>>
    <<#EndIf>>
->Yes, we are not suggesting otherwise.
    Simone Lo: You'd better.;<<Play(駱思敏,MBU_駱思敏_簽約側坐抬頭返回)>>
    //if黑臉
    <<#If($PlayGoodBadCop=-1)>>
        23(c): Jason, you're supposed to be the bad cop. Press her.
        <<CloseCommunicator()>>
    <<#EndIf>>
<<#EndSimple>>
Simone Lo: So? What now?;<<Play(駱思敏,MBU_駱思敏_簽約側坐抬手)>>
Lawrence Chang: We need a verbal record of all members of your family, and their current whereabouts.
Simone Lo: Sure.;<<Play(駱思敏,MBU_駱思敏_簽約側坐抬手返回)>>
Lawrence Chang: Let's start with your daughter. According to the documents you submitted, she should be at home.;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約側坐抬手)>>
Lawrence Chang: That is not consistent with our findings. Care to explain?
Simone Lo: ...;<<Play(張隆生,MBU_安全帽_簽約側坐冷靜抬手返回)>>
Simone Lo: I don't understand. What do you mean?
//IF黑臉
<<#If($PlayGoodBadCop=-1)>>
    <<#GoTo(@簽約駱思敏黑臉)>>
<<#EndIf>>
//IF白臉
<<#If($PlayGoodBadCop=1)>>
    <<#GoTo(@簽約駱思敏白臉)>>
<<#EndIf>>

title: 簽約駱思敏(黑臉)
body: <<@簽約駱思敏黑臉>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Where is your daughter, really? //IF之前問過 <<#If($KnowLuoWhereKids=0)>> Simone Lo: We talked about this before... <<#EndIf>> Simone Lo: She's at home, with her grandparents. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I see. // ->Prove it. Simone Lo: How am I supposed to prove that?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Then you're lying.;<> <<@駱思敏慌亂時間>> Simone Lo: W-wait! But... ->You tell me. How do you prove it? Simone Lo: But I— <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Your contract depends on this. Mind you. ->Get out if you can't.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> ->Liar. There is only one grandparent and she's disabled.;//者個選項要姊所 Simone Lo: How did you know that!?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Let's say we have done our homework. ->Vision knows everything. Think twice before you lie. ->We know what you know. Don't play tricks. <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@駱思敏慌亂時間)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: Sorry, Ms. Lo, but our profile shows that your daughter is not home.;<> Simone Lo: No way! It's my daughter! You don't know about her!;<> <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Think twice before you say that. Simone Lo: I don't need to! I know where my own child is!;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I say you're lying. ->Shut up.;<> <<#EndSimple>> ->Liar, liar, pants on fire.;<> Simone Lo: What!?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Are you deaf, too?;<> ->I'm the boss in this room. I can kick you out any second.;<> ->Answer me if you want this job. The truth!;<> <<#EndSimple>> Simone Lo: You... <<#EndSimple>> ->You sold her, didn't you? Simone Lo: What the...!?;<>;<> Thought: Am I progressing too fast? Simone Lo: How dare you say that!?;<> Simone Lo: Ridiculous! You're crazy!;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I know you sold her. Admit it already. Simone Lo: I didn't! You have no proof! ->Heartless bitch.;<>;<> Simone Lo: You!;<> Thought: Lawrence looks surprised. Am I playing my role too hard? Simone Lo: I'm not a... <> ->Relax. I was just asking. Simone Lo: ... <<#Simple(6)>> ->But your reaction was rather interesting. That makes me think. Simone Lo: It was just...!? ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Simone Lo: ... Simone Lo: What the hell do you want?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I want you to tell me, honestly, where the hell your daughter is. ->You know what I want.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Simone Lo: ... Simone Lo: I told you over and over again. She's home.;<> Simone Lo: Why don't you listen? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Because you're a damn bad liar.;<> ->One last warning. I want the truth.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Simone Lo: ...;<> Simone Lo: No, this is not right... Simone Lo: Say...;<> Simone Lo: What does my daughter have to do with my contract? Simone Lo: Right?;<> Thought: She's pleading with Lawrence for help. Lawrence Chang: ...;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You know my job better than I do, eh? Is that what you think?;<> Simone Lo: No. Simone Lo: I don't, really. ->Show yourself out if you really don't want to cooperate.;<> Simone Lo: Please. I beg you... Simone Lo: I really need this job. It's my last straw... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Simone Lo: Please.;<>;<> Simone Lo: I really don't know how to prove myself... <<#Simple(8)>> ->You know we can hand you to the police? ->Start with telling us where your daughter is. ->... <<#EndSimple>> //if數值夠高 <<#If($AddStressOnRo>2)>> Simone Lo: I told you! She's home!!;<> Simone Lo: You don't believe me, it's not my fault! Simone Lo: What the hell do you want me to do!!! 23(c): Good job, Jason. She's crumbling.;<> 23(c): Get ready for the next phase. <> Thought: ...Time for me to take a break? <<#EndIf>> //if數值不夠高 <<#If($AddStressOnRo<3)>> Simone Lo: We're running in circles.;<> Simone Lo: Just tell me already. What proof do you want? Simone Lo: You just don't believe. What can I do, huh? 23(c): We're running out of time.;<> 23(c): Ready or not, let's move to the next phase. <> Thought: Time for me to take a break?;<>;<> <<#EndIf>> Lawrence Chang: Um, Jason? How about you take a break?;<> Lawrence Chang: Leave her to me. I can handle it. <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Good. I could use a pee break. ->You'd better be cooperative when I'm back. <<#EndSimple>> Lawrence Chang: OK, sure. <> <<#End>>

title: 說服謝采欣2
body: Cathy Hsieh: This is a good place.;<> Thought: She looks all tensed up. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Are we safe here? Cathy Hsieh: Not exactly, but better than in the office. ->How are you feeling? Cathy Hsieh: Me? Cathy Hsieh: I'm feeling alright... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: Let me guess. Justin wants me to give him Screening data. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes. // ->How do you know? <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: He mentioned once, that if one day he's in a position to shut down Silencio, he'd need data from Screening... <<#Simple(8)>> ->He said he'd ask you for it? Cathy Hsieh: No, he didn't, not specifically. Cathy Hsieh: But he only had this conversation with me, right? Cathy Hsieh: Others couldn't do it. ->What do you make of it? Cathy Hsieh: I-I'm not sure yet. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: I don't think I should give him the data. Cathy Hsieh: Morale is horribly low in the office right now. If I do that...;<> <> <<#Wait(1)>> Quentin Qiu: Do what?;<> Cathy Hsieh: QQ!?;<> Quentin Qiu: What's up, Cathy? You look like you're about to cry. Quentin Qiu: What did you say to her, Jason? Thought: He's confronting me.;<> Thought: I can't tell him the truth.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I was asking her to hand Screening data over to Ops. Quentin Qiu: What!?;<> ->It's our secret. ->I can't tell you. Quentin Qiu: No, you must tell me! Quentin Qiu: What are you forcing her to do?;<> Cathy Hsieh: QQ!;<> Quentin Qiu: Step aside, Cathy. I will get an answer from him today. Cathy Hsieh: Why should I step aside? I'm involved! I have a say!;<> Quentin Qiu: I... Cathy Hsieh: Jason, I'm going to tell QQ what you asked for.;<>;<> Cathy Hsieh: Can I? I need his opinion. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No, don't. Cathy Hsieh: Then I refuse to work with you. Cathy Hsieh: Is that what you want? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->OK, tell him. <<#GoTo(@采欣告訴球球主角來要資料)>> ->I'll tell Justin you refused. Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: So be it. Quentin Qiu: Huh? What exactly is happening?;<> Cathy Hsieh: Let's go, QQ.;<> Quentin Qiu: Oh... <> Thought: ...;<> Thought: I think I screwed up... <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> ->OK, go ahead. ->Your call. <<@采欣告訴球球主角來要資料>> Cathy Hsieh: Justin wants me to give him Screening data. Jason is here to deliver the message.;<> Quentin Qiu: What!? <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Why would Cathy help Ops!?;<>;<> <<#Wait(1)>> Quentin Qiu: What do you want this data for?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Once farm closure is in motion, we need the complete data of all juicers to handle their contracts. // ->That is classified. Quentin Qiu: Asking for a favor and not bothering to explain? Where are your manners? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I don't need to explain. ->I'm not asking for your favor. No need to explain to you. Quentin Qiu: You! <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: They need contributor data to handle their contracts, once farm closure is in motion. <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Compensation for terminating contracts early? Cathy Hsieh: Yes.;<> Cathy Hsieh: What do you think?;<> Quentin Qiu: ...;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: I think they should go fuck themselves. Cathy Hsieh: QQ... <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->This "them", you mean Ops? Quentin Qiu: Who else? Quentin Qiu: And you, too. ->I'm sorry you feel that way. Quentin Qiu: The feeling is mutual. ->That would make life hard for everyone. Quentin Qiu: Great freaking idea. You people deserve some hard life. Quentin Qiu: You made our life hard enough. It's about time to return the favor. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Jason, I can't believe you're forcing Cathy.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I didn't force her. ->I was just passing a message. <<@你明明知道采欣有多仰慕吳俊恩！>> Quentin Qiu: You know how she idolizes Justin!;<> Quentin Qiu: What you did is essentially forcing her to backstab us! Quentin Qiu: You know damn well what you're doing. You're using her! <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I respect Cathy's decisions. Quentin Qiu: After you mess up her head, huh?;<> ->I was doing my job, assigned by my boss. Quentin Qiu: What if your boss told you to eat shit? You do it without questioning?;<> ->I don't see it that way. I'm not using her. Quentin Qiu: You are.;<> <<#EndSimple>> <<#GoTo(@球球開始發神經)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Got nothing else to say? Cathy Hsieh: QQ... <<#GoTo(@你明明知道采欣有多仰慕吳俊恩！)>> <<@球球開始發神經>> Cathy Hsieh: Please, QQ. Jason is not using me. He's just...;<> Quentin Qiu: Stand aside and calm down for a while, Cathy. He's a— <<#Simple(8)>> ->I'm a what? Quentin Qiu: You're a goddamn— ->Let Cathy speak. Quentin Qiu: No, I'll say it out loud for her. You're— ->I'm a good man treated unfairly. Quentin Qiu: Jason Dai! You— ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: Quentin Qiu!!!;<>;<>;<> Cathy Hsieh: Let me speak!!! Quentin Qiu: Eek! Thought: I never thought Cathy had so much force in her. Cathy Hsieh: I know you want to help me, but I don't want this! Cathy Hsieh: I need your opinion to help me make decisions. Don't make it for me! Quentin Qiu: ... Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: Sorry...;<> Quentin Qiu: Ah, huh...? Quentin Qiu: What are you sorry for?;<> Cathy Hsieh: I-I was too loud.;<> Quentin Qiu: Haha, don't you worry about that.;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: I should apologize. I didn't mean to—;<>;<> Cathy Hsieh: I know. You were blinded by anger.;<> Cathy Hsieh: ...;<> Quentin Qiu: ...;<>;<> Thought: Where is this going? Cathy Hsieh: Jason.;<>;<> Cathy Hsieh: I can't just hand the data over to Justin, not without Wayne's approval. <<#Simple(8)>> ->But he won't approve. Cathy Hsieh: Yes. ->Let's go ask him. Cathy Hsieh: He won't agree. Quentin Qiu: What else do you expect? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: You know that if I hand over the data without approval...;<> Cathy Hsieh: I would be essentially backstabbing the entire Screening team. <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That depends on your perspective.;<>;//這要記錄 Cathy Hsieh: From my perspective, it is backstabbing. ->You're right. Cathy Hsieh: Good. ->I wouldn't call it backstabbing.;<>;//這要記錄 Cathy Hsieh: But I think it is. <<#EndSimple>> Thought: She's adamant. Cathy Hsieh: There is one reason why I even consider doing it. Cathy Hsieh: I want Justin's promise, to protect the Screening team's interest.;<> Quentin Qiu: That snob won't agree!;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What interest? Cathy Hsieh: That we will have new jobs once Silencio is shut down.;<> ->About that, you should talk to Justin in person. Cathy Hsieh: Then he must come see me in person.;<> ->He said he will try to make arrangements for the Screening team. Cathy Hsieh: "Try to" is not good enough. I need a promise.;<> Cathy Hsieh: We can't tell if he's really trying. <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: We all know farm closure is not inevitable. Quentin Qiu: Morale is devastated. Cathy Hsieh: ...;<> Cathy Hsieh: So, I'm thinking, if we can't stop it, maybe we can make the most out of it. Cathy Hsieh: Did Justin ever talk about it?;<> Cathy Hsieh: About the fate of Screening post-closure? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He will keep a part of the Screening team for a special taskforce. Quentin Qiu: To terminate contracts, I guess. Cathy Hsieh: How big a part is it? Thought: He said about two thirds. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Two thirds. Cathy Hsieh: Meaning one third of us will be jobless. ->He didn't give a firm number. Cathy Hsieh: In any case, some of us will be jobless. <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: So? What do they do about the jobless part?;<> Quentin Qiu: Just kick them out? <> <<#GoTo(@說明怎麼在廢廠後照顧篩選組的人)>> ->He will try his best to make arrangements for members of the Screening team. Cathy Hsieh: How, exactly? <<@說明怎麼在廢廠後照顧篩選組的人>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->He's gonna find openings in other Vision farms. Quentin Qiu: Really?;<> ->That you should ask Justin himself. Cathy Hsieh: ...I trust he will have a plan. <> ->He didn't give any details. Quentin Qiu: No details means no plan.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->If that's how you want to see it. Quentin Qiu: He never cared about us. I'm not expecting a sudden change of heart. ->He's gonna help you look for new jobs outside Vision. Quentin Qiu: Really? ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Why should we believe him?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->You're being unreasonably hostile, QQ. Quentin Qiu: Unreasonable? Quentin Qiu: They have brainwashed you, Jason. ->I believe he means it. Quentin Qiu: You're either lying with a straight face or incredibly stupid. ->Because we don't have any other choice. Quentin Qiu: Don't you "we" me. Quentin Qiu: You're not one of us. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: QQ!;<>;<> Cathy Hsieh: Stop it! Quentin Qiu: Tsk.;<> Cathy Hsieh: OK, Jason.;<> Cathy Hsieh: Now that Justin has a plan for the Screening team, I will give him the data. <<#Simple(8)>> ->But he also said he may not be able to get a job for everyone.;//D Cathy Hsieh: I understand. Cathy Hsieh: Of course it can't be that perfect. ->Thank you. Cathy Hsieh: I'm just trying to minimize the damage. Cathy Hsieh: Like what Justin is doing. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Cathy!? Don't tell me you're serious!;<> Cathy Hsieh: Feel free to tell Wayne. I will tell him myself, after I hand over the data.;<> Quentin Qiu: But why...? Cathy Hsieh: Farm closure is inevitable. The best we can do is to minimize the damage.;<> Cathy Hsieh: Duking it out with Ops now will only make things worse. Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: Think about it, QQ!;<> Quentin Qiu: I see, I see!;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: I know what you mean... Quentin Qiu: I wasn't thinking straight. You're right.;<> Quentin Qiu: If we fight Ops to the end, contributors will take most of the damage. Cathy Hsieh: Apologize to Jason. He did nothing wrong.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->No need. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: ...I'm sorry. Thought: He's forcing himself to say it. Cathy Hsieh: That doesn't count.;<> Quentin Qiu: Huh?;<>;<> Cathy Hsieh: You didn't mean it.;<> Quentin Qiu: What else do you want? Cathy Hsieh: A genuine apology to Jason. Quentin Qiu: Sorry, I just can't.;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->It's really not necessary, Cathy. Quentin Qiu: ... ->Do you hate me that much? Quentin Qiu: Yes, right now. Cathy Hsieh: Quentin Qiu! ->You said we're still friends even if I end up going to Ops. Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: I want to see you as a friend... Quentin Qiu: Somehow, I just can't. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: Why is this happening? Quentin Qiu: First Jason, then Cathy.;<> Quentin Qiu: Why are you all siding with Ops? <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I never chose a side. Quentin Qiu: You chose to work for Ops. That's your side.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->There are different sides within Ops. Quentin Qiu: They all want to shut down the farm. What's the difference? ->Choosing to work for them doesn't mean I agree with everything they say. Quentin Qiu: Maybe not everything. Quentin Qiu: You agree with them on farm closure. That's what really counts. <<#EndSimple>> ->Because they know the situation better. Quentin Qiu: They know the Cellar better.;<> Quentin Qiu: Have they ever bothered to learn a bit more about our situation? <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: From the beginning, they've been telling us over and over again that shutting down Silencio is the only solution.;<> Quentin Qiu: We keep telling them about the consequences, but they just don't listen. Quentin Qiu: Plus, is it really the only solution?;<> Quentin Qiu: Can't we just give the Cellar a complete fix and keep the farm running?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Justin says that would require an impossible figure of funding. Quentin Qiu: Impossible to him! Quentin Qiu: Maybe someone else can get the money! <<#Simple(8)>> ->A maybe isn't good enough, QQ. Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: Maybe Wayne could help! <<#GoTo(@球球表示阿緯會幫忙找錢欸)>> ->Someone else? Like whom? Quentin Qiu: Like Wayne! Maybe he could! <<@球球表示阿緯會幫忙找錢欸>> Cathy Hsieh: ... Quentin Qiu: Don't you just write Wayne off! He's been working on it for ages! Quentin Qiu: He knows Ops needs funding to fix the Cellar, but Justin just wouldn't talk to him. <<#Simple(8)>> ->They never talked about it? Quentin Qiu: As far as I know, Justin actively refused to talk.;<> Quentin Qiu: Every time Wayne comes up with a potential solution, he turns it down without listening. Quentin Qiu: I know you think I'm biased!;<> Quentin Qiu: But even you two must admit that Justin is as stubborn as a mule. Quentin Qiu: Trust me, he's the one who refused to talk! Cathy Hsieh: ...You're right about Justin. ->It can't be helped even if they wanted to work together. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: And at this stage, it's too late. Cathy Hsieh: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->No way. You have no idea how bad the situation is. Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: Maybe. You and Cathy, you both changed your mind after seeing the Cellar. <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: I just can't deal with how fast this is happening.;<> Quentin Qiu: We tried so hard to make Silencio better, but everything failed. Quentin Qiu: I...;<> Quentin Qiu: No, I can't accept reality as it is. Quentin Qiu: I know you have done nothing wrong, Jason. I'm sorry... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't be sorry. I know how you feel. ->It's nothing. Quentin Qiu: Hmm... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: Is this really the end of it?;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->The end of what? Quentin Qiu: Silencio. ->Afraid so. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Quentin Qiu: We really can't do anything about it? Cathy Hsieh: Yeah.;<>;<> Cathy Hsieh: It's the only way to stop the damage caused by the Cellar. Quentin Qiu: By kicking tens of thousands out, without a job?;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: By sending all those contributors back home? <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->They will be paid. Quentin Qiu: But not as much as finishing the contract term. Thought: Most likely. ->It's not an excuse to keep the Cellar running. Quentin Qiu: I know, still... ->I agree it stinks, but we don't have any other option. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: It's the necessary evil.;<>;<> Cathy Hsieh: Justin would say. Thought: He did in fact say that. Quentin Qiu: Easier said than done... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Maybe you're right. He doesn't care much about people. Cathy Hsieh: I don't think so. ->This is not an easy decision for him. ->He's under a mountain of pressure and responsibilities. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: Necessary evil is still evil. Cathy Hsieh: Acknowledging it means he is aware that what he does, some of it, is bad. Cathy Hsieh: He knew people will hate him for it, but still went forward.;<> Cathy Hsieh: It's not that he doesn't care. Cathy Hsieh: He weighed all possibilities, and decided to choose the lesser of two evils.;<> Cathy Hsieh: He is not proud of this decision. Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: If you both believe he's a good guy, suit yourselves.;<>;<> Cathy Hsieh: QQ... Quentin Qiu: I don't know him, OK? Don't force me to fall in love with him.;<> Cathy Hsieh: You're right. Sorry.;<>;<> Quentin Qiu: ... Quentin Qiu: Now I believe he's not a selfish snob, because you two told me he's not.;<> Quentin Qiu: I believe you.;<> Quentin Qiu: But! Even if I do believe Justin Wu is a living god...;<> Quentin Qiu: I'd still say farm closure is a shitty idea. Cathy Hsieh: ...;<> Cathy Hsieh: QQ... Quentin Qiu: I'm off to get my soda. Three of them.;<> Quentin Qiu: Need at least three for this shitty day. <> <<#Wait(4)>> //等球球離開以後 Cathy Hsieh: ...;<> Cathy Hsieh: He didn't take the message well.;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->The others at Screening will only do worse. Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: Yeah. ->Were you expecting any better? Cathy Hsieh: Hoping. I was hoping he could do better. Cathy Hsieh: Too much to hope for, huh... ->That's his own problem. Cathy Hsieh: You should watch out, Jason. Cathy Hsieh: Working closely with Justin, you may become him. Cathy Hsieh: His lack of empathy is contagious... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: ...;<> Cathy Hsieh: Sometimes I think he's unreasonable, too. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Are you really handing the data to Justin? // ->Are you really backstabbing the Screening team? //IF剛剛有說不是背叛或是看個人觀點 <<#If($DLStateFloat=-1||$DLStateFloat=0)>> Cathy Hsieh: But you just said it's not backstabbing?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm speaking from your perspective. ($DLStateFloat);//說依觀點才出現 Cathy Hsieh: Yes, from my perspective this is backstabbing... ->I changed my mind. ->That is not the point. <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#EndIf>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: I will send the data to Justin. That will complete your mission.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->He said he doesn't want to force you. Are you sure? Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: Did he say that? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yes, he said he respects whatever you decide. ->He specifically told me not to lobby you. <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: ...I see. ->No regret? Cathy Hsieh: No. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: You may go now, Jason. <<#Simple(8)>> ->See you later. Cathy Hsieh: Yes. ->What about you? Cathy Hsieh: I want to stay for a while. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I see. // ->Something's wrong?;<>;//這要記錄 Cathy Hsieh: ...;<> Cathy Hsieh: I... Cathy Hsieh: Jason, I don't think Justin is a bad man. <<#Simple(8)>> ->So? ->I don't, either. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: But sometimes he hurts people really bad.;<> Cathy Hsieh: He always talks about being reasonable, respecting your opinions. Cathy Hsieh: At the end of the day, he doesn't really care.;<> Cathy Hsieh: If you know him well enough, you'd know his brain is wired that way. Still... Cathy Hsieh: Being treated like that doesn't feel good.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You lost me. ->Like how you feel right now? ->I think I know what you mean. Cathy Hsieh: ...Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: He knows me too well, knows I'd choose to help him, making myself a public enemy... Cathy Hsieh: He knows I will give him the data, even before asking. <<#Simple(8)>> ->He knows this will hurt you but does it anyway. Cathy Hsieh: Because it's the right thing, the necessary evil. Cathy Hsieh: I don't blame him. <<#Simple(6)>> ->How would you like him to handle it? <<#GoTo(@采欣解釋希望俊恩道個歉讓他比較好過)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->How would you like him to handle it? <<@采欣解釋希望俊恩道個歉讓他比較好過>> Cathy Hsieh: I...;<> Thought: That question caught her by surprise. Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: I wish he could say sorry. Cathy Hsieh: Sorry for putting me in such a difficult position.;<> Cathy Hsieh: Then I'll tell him... Cathy Hsieh: It's alright. You're only doing the right thing. It's not your fault. Cathy Hsieh: That's about it. Thought: She sounds almost apologetic herself. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: Do what has to be done, and break things if it can't be helped.;<> Cathy Hsieh: I guess that's what Justin thinks, this whole time. Cathy Hsieh: That is why I never applied to transfer to Ops.;<> Cathy Hsieh: Everyone at Screening is nice. We care for each other. Cathy Hsieh: It's a heartwarming place, because Wayne is a heartwarming leader.;<> Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: I can imagine how sad Wayne must be, right now. Cathy Hsieh: He's been trying so hard to make Justin listen...;<> Cathy Hsieh: Ha, c'est la vie, I guess. Bad things happen. Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: Sorry for boring you out.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->No, I'm glad you shared that with me. Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: Thank you. Cathy Hsieh: You're a nice guy, you know that? Cathy Hsieh: Screening is a better fit for you, really. Cathy Hsieh: Now it's too late to win you over. The farm itself will be gone soon. Haha...;<> Cathy Hsieh: ... <> ->It's nothing. Cathy Hsieh: Thank you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: With that off my mind, I'm feeling a bit better, although a bit more alone time wouldn't hurt.;<> Cathy Hsieh: I will go back to the officer later. Thought: ... Cathy Hsieh: See you, Jason.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: Bye. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Yeah, bye. // ->Hey Cathy, do you think Wayne is OK? Cathy Hsieh: Wayne? Cathy Hsieh: He's equal parts angry and sad.;<> Cathy Hsieh: We don't know how to make him feel better. Cathy Hsieh: He just left, said he needed a break. Cathy Hsieh: ...Are you worried about him?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Yeah, I am. ->I saw him just now. He didn't look too well. <<#EndSimple>> Cathy Hsieh: I see...;<> Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: I know it's not specifically anyone's fault... Cathy Hsieh: But this is truly a shame. Cathy Hsieh: ... Cathy Hsieh: I can't do anything about it, though.;<> Cathy Hsieh: Just leave it be. Cathy Hsieh: Bye, Jason. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Yeah, bye. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <> <> <<#End>>

title: 說服阿緯2
body: Wayne Zheng: ... Thought: I can't see his face from this angle...;<> Wayne Zheng: ...Don't worry. I have calmed down.;<> Wayne Zheng: The breeze up here helps a lot to clear my head. Wayne Zheng: I'm not angry anymore. I'm just...;<> Wayne Zheng: Tired. Wayne Zheng: ... //if巧遇正緯時沒有讓他道歉 <<#If(SelectionGoRest=-1)>> Wayne Zheng: I must apologize.;<> Wayne Zheng: Back then when I saw you in the corridor, I was... Wayne Zheng: Not nice. Wayne Zheng: Really sorry about that. I mean it. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't worry about it. Wayne Zheng: Thank you. ->You were scary. Wayne Zheng: Haha... ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#EndIf>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: What do you think I should do, Jason? Wayne Zheng: I'm so lost. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Don't you have any plan? Wayne Zheng: I said I'm so lost.;<> Wayne Zheng: You won't make a good therapist, Jason. Wayne Zheng: Hahaha... Wayne Zheng: Ha...;<> ->Take the day off and go home? Wayne Zheng: Sounds like an idea. Wayne Zheng: Hot bath and beer should do the trick. ->Just don't think about it. Wayne Zheng: I'm trying. Wayne Zheng: Looks like I just can't, haha... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: Somehow I just can't stop. Wayne Zheng: This defeat is occupying my whole mind.;<> Wayne Zheng: How did it end up like this? Wayne Zheng: I mean, I've had a plan all along. Wayne Zheng: In my plan, we can fix the Cellar, and there's no need to shut Silencio down.;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Did you talk about it with Justin? I mean, seriously talk about it. Wayne Zheng: Of course I did! Wayne Zheng: Now you know pretty well what he's like.;<> Wayne Zheng: You tell me. Is he the kind who listens? Thought: He'd just brush Wayne's plan off and call it ignorant amateur or something.;<> ->Why didn't you put the plan into practice? Wayne Zheng: Because I need the full support of Ops. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: You know what? We don't need to fix the entire Cellar. Wayne Zheng: Since the objective is just to make the Cellar safe, better planning and rotation should do the trick. Wayne Zheng: The Cellar is not running at full capacity, meaning we can move racks around. Wayne Zheng: Move all contributors out of the most damaged zones, then bring local systems offline for repair and maintenance.;<> Wayne Zheng: Naturally, that will reduce Silencio's efficiency. Not by much, but enough to leave a visible dent on the annual profit. Wayne Zheng: We just need to talk the leadership into accepting it. Wayne Zheng: That way, we could make everyone happy. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Why would they accept it when there's a visible drop in profit? Wayne Zheng: They care about more than just profit. ->Justin doesn't agree with it? Wayne Zheng: He thought I was just inventing things on the fly to stop him.;<> Wayne Zheng: He didn't believe my plan had any chance. Wayne Zheng: The reason being, according to him, the leadership couldn't possibly accept a plan with less profit. Wayne Zheng: The issue is, he's not seeing the big picture.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: When we talk about leadership, we're talking about the board of directors. Wayne Zheng: The board, in turn, means a group of people. <<#Simple(8)>> ->The board is in favor of farm closure. I have met with their representative. Wayne Zheng: Not their representative. Wayne Zheng: You met with the representative of one single director. ->You think there are different factions within the board? Wayne Zheng: Exactly! ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: We know at least one director is backing Justin. Wayne Zheng: Meanwhile, there should be directors on our side. <<#Simple(8)>> ->But isn't Silencio suffering a sustained loss of profit? Wayne Zheng: True, but... ->Should be? Wayne Zheng: Let me finish. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Vision as a corporation wants more than just money. For example, political power. Wayne Zheng: As a flop farm, Silencio is pretty much doomed. Outdated technology, low efficiency. Wayne Zheng: But on the other hand, it's huge, the largest of all flop farms under Vision. Wayne Zheng: If we see it as a welfare facility, it could hold a considerable portion of the unemployed population. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You think some directors are interested in doing welfare/charity? Wayne Zheng: Not charity itself. Think about what we can gain from this charity business. ->And lose a lot of money in the process. Wayne Zheng: Not "lose". Think of it as an investment with non-monetary returns. Wayne Zheng: The return is better than money. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Vision's growth has hit a bottleneck. To go any further, we will need favorable policies from the government.;<> Wayne Zheng: Now you see, at this particular stage, Vision needs political influence more than money.;<> Wayne Zheng: By keeping Silencio running, we show the government that we can help them curb unemployment. Wayne Zheng: Then we're talking about public relationship, about votes, about real power.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->The government will be very grateful if Vision could help them curb unemployment. Wayne Zheng: Yes. ->I see. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Better still, for Vision, the cost isn't very high. Wayne Zheng: Silencio's not exactly a money maker, but self-sufficiency isn't a problem. Wayne Zheng: Here comes the important part.;<> Wayne Zheng: Justin keeps complaining about budgets, right? Wayne Zheng: If they lower the expectations, from making a profit to breaking even...;<> Wayne Zheng: Getting the necessary budget from the board isn't that hard. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You believe this plan has a chance? Wayne Zheng: A very good chance. <<#GoTo(@啊緯說它有偷偷發現有留廠派董事動手腳)>> ->Why do you think there are directors on our side? <<@啊緯說它有偷偷發現有留廠派董事動手腳>> Wayne Zheng: I can't disclose too much. Let's just say I have solid proof.;<> Wayne Zheng: There's a pro-Silencio faction in the board, and they have been pulling strings in this farm, too. Wayne Zheng: All they need is an agent to make things happen, and I can be that agent.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: He's quite confident. I guess his plan really has a chance...? <<#Simple(8)>> ->You told all this to Justin and he dismissed the whole idea? Wayne Zheng: He called me delusional, said I was just desperate to keep Silencio running. Wayne Zheng: Then he left. There was no discussion. Wayne Zheng: In his logic, I and my plan were not worth his "cost of communication". ->You need to get into action, now. Wayne Zheng: I need Justin's support to convince the board. Wayne Zheng: Can't do it just on my own. Wayne Zheng: Especially when I don't even know which specific directors are on our side. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: It's all irrelevant now.;<> Wayne Zheng: Justin has won. We're out of time.;<> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: Hey, it feels great to share my secret plan with someone. Wayne Zheng: Now I feel I'm not entirely worthless. Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: You should go now, Jason.;<>;<> Wayne Zheng: I could use a bit more alone time. Wayne Zheng: I'm glad you chose Ops over Screening. Wayne Zheng: Wouldn't want to see you losing the job just when you got it, haha... Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: See you later. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Bye. // ->Wayne, you need to, no, you must, talk this over with Justin, one last time. <<#GoTo(@嘗試說服阿緯跟俊嗯最後說一次)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>> <<@嘗試說服阿緯跟俊嗯最後說一次>> Wayne Zheng: ...;<> Wayne Zheng: Hell no! I've had enough of him! Wayne Zheng: Really. So tired of it. Wayne Zheng: You have no idea how much willpower it takes to just stay around him.;<> Wayne Zheng: He's a self-righteous, arrogant bastard with a toxic aura. Wayne Zheng: Plus, why would he listen to me when he's winning both the battle and the war?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Justin is under enormous pressure. He doesn't enjoy this victory. Wayne Zheng: My question stands. Why would he listen to me? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->If you offer a better alternative, he might seriously consider it. ->Now he is more open to fresh ideas. <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Why's that? He's been talking about pressure all the time. What's new now? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Now he is facing the real consequences of farm closure, for the first time. ->He has won. Now the situation is a lot less confrontational. <<#EndSimple>> ->Are you just giving up like that? Wayne Zheng: I... Wayne Zheng: I tried. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Not hard enough. Not unless you give it one last shot. ->You know you still have a chance. Wayne Zheng: I... ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: ... Thought: He's wrestling himself. Wayne Zheng: ...;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You must make him see the alternative. ->Wayne, you still have a chance to save the farm. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Dammit...;<> Wayne Zheng: I see. You're right. I must see Justin again. Wayne Zheng: ...This is my responsibility as a division lead. Wayne Zheng: Can't give up, as long as there is a slim chance.;<> Wayne Zheng: Hopefully this time it will be different. Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Thank you, Jason.;<> Wayne Zheng: If I do give up now, I'll hate myself till the day I die. Wayne Zheng: Again, thank you, Jason. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Save your breath. Go find Justin. Wayne Zheng: Haha, I sure will. ->You're welcome. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: I'm calling him for an emergency meeting, right now.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Right now? Wayne Zheng: He's met with the director's representative and Mr. Yan. ->What are you going to talk about? Wayne Zheng: I don't know. Wayne Zheng: At least I must make him hear my entire plan. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: We are very, very close to the point of no return. It's now or never. Wayne Zheng: ... Wayne Zheng: Honestly, I'm not counting on it. This could end up exactly like every single time before.;<> Wayne Zheng: But I promise to give it my best shot. <<#Simple(8)>> ->No need for promise. I trust you. Wayne Zheng: Yeah! ->This time it will work. Wayne Zheng: Hopefully. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Surely! Wayne Zheng: Yeah, surely! Haha... ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Wayne Zheng: Let's go, Jason. Wayne Zheng: To our last chance.;<>;<> <> <<#End>>

title: 俊恩抵達2
body: //IF操作勝利 <<#If($ConvincedTsaiShin=0)>> Justin Wu: Phew...;<>;<> Justin Wu: That was intense. Five more minutes and I'd explode on the spot. Thought: ...Huh?;<> Thought: I thought he was totally, absolutely calm...? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What? You were as cool as a cucumber. Justin Wu: Was I? Good.;<> ->You didn't look nervous at all. Justin Wu: I was being grilled alive on the inside. Justin Wu: But Mr. Yan seems to have taken it well. I guess that means our proposal worked.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: We have finally done it, man!;<> Justin Wu: Finally, we can put an end to Silencio! Justin Wu: ... Thought: Is he shaking...? Justin Wu: ...;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->What's up? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: It feels so weird when this gigantic load is finally off my mind.;<> Justin Wu: It's so...unreal. Justin Wu: Now, we have a lot of work to do.;<> Justin Wu: The whole Ops team will be very busy for a while. Justin Wu: Including you, Jason.;<> Justin Wu: You are an integral part of the team now. <<#Simple(8)>> ->You can trust us. Justin Wu: I sure can. ->It's not gonna be easy. Justin Wu: Yes. All the more reason to work hard. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Thought: He's rarely this happy. Justin Wu: Our goal is not impossibly far away. Justin Wu: We just need a good plan and cover the distance one milestone at a time. Justin Wu: But for today, we should take a break.;<> Justin Wu: I'll inform the team. Justin Wu: Everyone should take the day off. Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Now I should go back to the office.;<> Justin Wu: You will need to stay a bit longer, Jason. <<#EndIf>> //if說服采欣失敗 <<#If($ConvincedTsaiShin=-1)>> Justin Wu: ...;<>;<> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: I failed.;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->You tried. Justin Wu: Yes, I tried. Justin Wu: That doesn't change the result. Justin Wu: I have trouble facing this failure. ->It was a mission impossible to begin with. Justin Wu: You're right. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: If only we had the data from Screening... Thought: If only I managed to convince Cathy... <<#Simple(8)>> ->Is it my fault? ->Sorry, I failed to get Cathy on our side. Justin Wu: True. This could have ended up very differently if we had Cathy. Justin Wu: Although I wouldn't say it's your fault. Justin Wu: My bad. I should have talked to Cathy sooner. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: We had an opportunity, but I failed to grasp it.;<> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Now I'm feeling guilty.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't. We don't blame you. Justin Wu: I do. Justin Wu: I made a mistake that could have easily been avoided. ->Me, too. Justin Wu: Is that so... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Now we must deal with the same old pain indefinitely. Justin Wu: Ugly things like what you saw in the control room the other day, they will keep happening.;<> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Maybe I shouldn't take the position of the division lead.;<>;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Don't. Justin Wu: Why?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->The team needs you, now more than ever. ->And give it back to that asshole? <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: ... <<#Simple(8)>> ->In a moment like this you must step up, not down. ->Stay with us. Eventually there will be another chance. Justin Wu: Maybe. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: I will think about it.;<> ->If that's what you want. Justin Wu: Yeah, I'll think about it. Justin Wu: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Anyway, I should inform the team of the outcome. Justin Wu: ...;<> Justin Wu: I wonder how well they will take this news...? Thought: He sounds actively hating himself. <> <<#Simple(10)>> ->Maybe not too well, considering that our plan has failed.;<> Justin Wu: Yeah... ->They won't be happy to hear it, but no one would blame you. Justin Wu: ...Is that so? Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Maybe yes. They are nice people. Thought: Despite saying that, he doesn't really believe himself. ->I guess they will just take it as it is. Justin Wu: ...Is that so? Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: I think not. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Sorry, Jason, I'm not in a good mood. Justin Wu: I tried so hard not to disappoint, yet...;<> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Well, guess I can't do much about it.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->You didn't disappoint. They will hug you with a big smile. Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Thank you, Jason. Justin Wu: Thanks for saying that. <<#Simple(8)>> ->I'm serious. //if剛剛選失落 <<#If($DLStateFloat=0)>> Justin Wu: Didn't you just say they may not take it very well? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I was kidding. ->They may, not necessarily. Justin Wu: Okay. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> Justin Wu: Let's just hope so. ->You're welcome! ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: ... Justin Wu: Now I should go back to the office. Justin Wu: You will need to stay a bit longer, Jason. <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Why? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Yuna Hsu of the IIB wants to see you.;<> Justin Wu: She said you know about the appointment? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->OK, I see. Justin Wu: Yes.;<> ->Nope, I don't. Justin Wu: Yeah...;<> <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: You just wait here. Justin Wu: She got in touch with me before my meeting with Mr. Yan. Told me to pass the message that she needs you for something. Justin Wu: If she makes any unreasonable demand, you just stand up and leave. Justin Wu: I will handle the fallout. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I see. ->It's gonna be OK. <<#EndSimple>> Justin Wu: Good.;<> Justin Wu: I'll be going then. Justin Wu: Don't forget to come to the office when you're done.;<> Justin Wu: See you later. <> <<#End>>

title: 操作組勝利慶功宴2
body:
Justin Wu: ...
Thought: He doesn't look very happy...
Thought: I guess whatever news comes next won't be good.
Justin Wu: I'll be straightforward with you, Jason.
Justin Wu: Considering your background and training...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站低頭返回)>>
Justin Wu: Once the farm closure process starts, you might be fired.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Huh? What? Why?
    Justin Wu: I thought you already knew it.
->Because I'm a racker?
    Justin Wu: Yes.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: As I said before, the first step of farm closure is to stop taking in new juicers.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站抬手)>>
Justin Wu: That means we won't have much racking to do.
Justin Wu: We will need rackers for unracking juicers in batches.
Justin Wu: But for that we will prioritize experienced, senior staff.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站抬手返回)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Makes sense.
    Justin Wu: Thank you for taking the news so calmly. I was expecting an argument.
    Justin Wu: Now that the worst part is said, here comes the good news.
->So, you're here to fire me?
    Justin Wu: No.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: Let me explain.
Justin Wu: The responsibilities of a racker also includes equipment maintenance.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站抬雙手)>>
Justin Wu: There will be a high demand for maintenance talents as we retire the Cellar in small chunks.
Justin Wu: The closure process will start in six months, give or take.
Justin Wu: We will have a new position for you if you can finish maintenance training within this timeframe.
Justin Wu: When Silencio is completely shut down, you will have a better chance of being transferred to another farm, too.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站抬雙手返回)>>
Justin Wu: Don't forget, though.
Justin Wu: If you fail to complete the training and pass the test within the given time, you will be fired.
Justin Wu: Now you have two choices.
Justin Wu: One, complete advanced racker course within six months. Very hard, but not impossible.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站手指)>>
Justin Wu: Two, quit now and start to look for your next job.
Justin Wu: This is a big decision. Take your time. I don't need an immediate response.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站手指返回)>>
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Didn't I just do Ops a huge favor?
    Justin Wu: Yes, but you have not exhibited any talent that we will need going forward.
->I'll think about it.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: ...;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站低頭)>>
Justin Wu: I know you might feel angry. You have every right to.
Justin Wu: But know this—the same applies to every worker of this farm.
Justin Wu: We must handle the situation with a definite standard, fair and just.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站低頭返回)>>
Justin Wu: There's no happy ending in farm closure. It's bad news for everyone, including the Ops team.
Justin Wu: We are putting an end to a horrible mistake, and the cost is everyone's job.
Justin Wu: The best I could do is to minimize the damage by all means possible.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->I know that, and I know you're doing your best.
    Justin Wu: ...
    Justin Wu: Thank you.
->Don't blame yourself too hard.
    Justin Wu: I won't.
    Justin Wu: Still...
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Justin Wu: I made farm closure happen. For that I'm responsible.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站低頭)>>
Justin Wu: ...
Justin Wu: Jason...
Justin Wu: You helped us a lot. No matter how this ends, we owe you one.;<<Play(吳俊恩,MBU_吳俊恩_側站低頭返回)>>
Justin Wu: Thank you.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->And thank you for making this happen.
->I did my part.
    Thought: He approves.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>

Justin Wu: OK, let's go inside.
Justin Wu: Enjoy yourself, hangout with everyone.
Justin Wu: At least today we deserve a break.

<<#End>>

title: 被隆生叫出去對話_自動
body:

Lawrence Chang: Eh...
Lawrence Chang: Let's go to the break room.
<<#Simple(6)>>
->You left before I did. What took you so long?
    Lawrence Chang: Screw it.
    Lawrence Chang: What took me so long?
    Lawrence Chang: The stupid parking lot downstairs!
    Lawrence Chang: There's no space for choppers!
    Lawrence Chang: Every time it takes a damn hour to wedge my chopper in.

    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Lawrence Chang: I'm leaving as much space as I can, and these stupid mopeds just keep pressing against me.
    Lawrence Chang: How am I supposed to get my ride out?
    Lawrence Chang: Sometimes I've got to move a whole row of mopeds just to take my ride out of that tiny space.
    Lawrence Chang: You have no idea!
    Lawrence Chang: And I'm—!
    Lawrence Chang: ...
    Lawrence Chang: I'm bitching a lot, aren't I?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Kinda. Haha.
        Lawrence Chang: Haha...
    ->Yep.
        Lawrence Chang: Sorry.
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Lawrence Chang: Ahem!
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
<<#End>>

title: 俊恩聯絡說執行長找自己

Justin Wu(c): Sorry for this abrupt call. There's something urgent that I need your help.
Justin Wu(c): Please meet me in the entrance lobby on the ground floor.
Justin Wu(c): I have already granted you elevator access.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站抬頭返回)>>
Justin Wu(c): That's it. See you later.
<<CloseCommunicator()>>
Thought: He hung up...;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭)>>
Frances Cheng(c): Go meet him, J.
Frances Cheng(c): Signing won't start in a while, and Ms. Situ is still out there. We have time.
Thought: ...It's not like I have a choice.;<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_工廠_側站低頭返回)>>
<<#End>>

title: 打探完成23通知

->...
<<#EndSimple>>

<<Play(戴士傑,MBU_戴士傑_便服3_側站低頭返回_臉譜)>>
<<#End>>

title: 主角不再回工廠想法
操作路線_回辦公室2
body:
Paxton Luo: Justin!
Justin Wu: Packs? But—
Paxton Luo: Quickly! They're all waiting for you.;<<Set($DLState,胖胖走去賴潔位置準備開始廢廠計畫說明)>>;//開始自動走路
Paxton Luo: Oh, I'm using your office. Need that computer to run a few tasks. Sorry.
Justin Wu: ...Do I have an office?
Paxton Luo: You do! You're the division lead now.
Justin Wu: Not yet...
Paxton Luo: Nah, if everyone thinks you are, you are.
Justin Wu: I...
<<#End>>
//吳俊恩走去賴潔座位，玩家也要自己跟過去。
